# datamodel-code-generator - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#datamodel-code-generator)

datamodel-code-generator[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#datamodel-code-generator "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================================

🚀 Generate Python data models from schema definitions in seconds.

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/datamodel-code-generator.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/datamodel-code-generator)
 [![Conda-forge](https://img.shields.io/conda/v/conda-forge/datamodel-code-generator)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/datamodel-code-generator)
 [![Downloads](https://pepy.tech/badge/datamodel-code-generator/month)](https://pepy.tech/project/datamodel-code-generator)
 [![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/datamodel-code-generator)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/datamodel-code-generator)
 [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/graph/badge.svg?token=plzSSFb9Li)](https://codecov.io/gh/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator)
 ![license](https://img.shields.io/github/license/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator.svg) [![Pydantic v1](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pydantic/pydantic/main/docs/badge/v1.json)](https://pydantic.dev/)
 [![Pydantic v2](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pydantic/pydantic/main/docs/badge/v2.json)](https://pydantic.dev/)

* * *

✨ What it does[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#what-it-does "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   📄 Converts **OpenAPI 3**, **JSON Schema**, **GraphQL**, and raw data (JSON/YAML/CSV) into Python models
*   🐍 Generates from **existing Python types** (Pydantic, dataclass, TypedDict) via `--input-model`
*   🎯 Generates **Pydantic v1/v2**, **dataclasses**, **TypedDict**, or **msgspec** output
*   🔗 Handles complex schemas: `$ref`, `allOf`, `oneOf`, `anyOf`, enums, and nested types
*   ✅ Produces type-safe, validated code ready for your IDE and type checker

* * *

📦 Installation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#installation "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[uv tool (Recommended)](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__tabbed_1_1)
[pip](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__tabbed_1_2)
[uv (project)](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__tabbed_1_3)
[conda](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__tabbed_1_4)
[pipx](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__tabbed_1_5)
[uvx (one-shot)](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__tabbed_1_6)

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-0-1) uv tool install datamodel-code-generator`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install datamodel-code-generator`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-2-1) uv add datamodel-code-generator`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-3-1) conda install -c conda-forge datamodel-code-generator`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-4-1) pipx install datamodel-code-generator`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-5-1) uvx datamodel-codegen --help`

* * *

Omitting --output-model-type is deprecated

Starting from version 0.53.0, omitting `--output-model-type` is deprecated. The implicit default `pydantic.BaseModel` (Pydantic v1) will be removed in a future version.

We recommend using `--output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel` for new projects.

* * *

🏃 Quick Start[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#quick-start "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 1️⃣ Create a schema file[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#1-create-a-schema-file "Permanent link")

pet.json

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-5)   "required": ["name", "species"],  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-6)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-7)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-8)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-9)       "description": "The pet's name"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-10)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-11)     "species": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-12)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-13)       "enum": ["dog", "cat", "bird", "fish"]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-14)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-15)     "age": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-16)       "type": "integer",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-17)       "minimum": 0,      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-18)       "description": "Age in years"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-19)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-20)     "vaccinated": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-21)       "type": "boolean",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-22)       "default": false    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-23)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-24)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-6-25) }`

### 2️⃣ Run the generator[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#2-run-the-generator "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-7-1) datamodel-codegen --input pet.json --input-file-type jsonschema --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --output model.py`

### 3️⃣ Use your models[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#3-use-your-models "Permanent link")

model.py

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-2) #   filename:  tutorial_pet.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-4) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-6) from enum import Enum [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-7) from typing import Optional [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-9) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-10) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-11)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-12) class Species(Enum):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-13)     dog = 'dog'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-14)     cat = 'cat'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-15)     bird = 'bird'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-16)     fish = 'fish' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-17) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-18)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-19) class Pet(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-20)     name: str = Field(..., description="The pet's name")    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-21)     species: Species    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-22)     age: Optional[int] = Field(None, description='Age in years', ge=0)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-8-23)     vaccinated: Optional[bool] = False`

🎉 That's it! Your schema is now a fully-typed Python model.

* * *

📥 Choose Your Input[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#choose-your-input "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Input Type | File Types | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 📘 [OpenAPI 3](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/) | `.yaml`, `.json` | API specifications |
| 📋 [JSON Schema](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/) | `.json` | Data validation schemas |
| 🔷 [GraphQL](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/) | `.graphql` | GraphQL type definitions |
| 📊 [JSON/YAML Data](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/) | `.json`, `.yaml` | Infer schema from data |
| 🐍 [Python Models](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/) | `.py` | Pydantic, dataclass, TypedDict |

* * *

📤 Choose Your Output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#choose-your-output "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-1) # 🆕 Pydantic v2 (recommended for new projects) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-2) datamodel-codegen --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-4) # 🔄 Pydantic v1 (default, for compatibility) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-5) datamodel-codegen --output-model-type pydantic.BaseModel ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-7) # 🏗️ Python dataclasses [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-8) datamodel-codegen --output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-10) # 📝 TypedDict (for type hints without validation) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-11) datamodel-codegen --output-model-type typing.TypedDict ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-12)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-13) # ⚡ msgspec (high-performance serialization) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-9-14) datamodel-codegen --output-model-type msgspec.Struct ...`

See [Supported Data Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/)
 for the full list.

* * *

🍳 Common Recipes[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#common-recipes "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 🤖 Get CLI Help from LLMs[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#get-cli-help-from-llms "Permanent link")

Generate a prompt to ask LLMs about CLI options:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-10-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt "Best options for Pydantic v2?" | claude -p`

See [LLM Integration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/)
 for more examples.

### 🌐 Generate from URL[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#http-extra-option "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-11-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[http]' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-11-2) datamodel-codegen --url https://example.com/api/openapi.yaml --output model.py`

### ⚙️ Use with pyproject.toml[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#use-with-pyprojecttoml "Permanent link")

pyproject.toml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-12-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-12-2) input = "schema.yaml" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-12-3) output = "src/models.py" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-12-4) output-model-type = "pydantic_v2.BaseModel"`

Then simply run:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-13-1) datamodel-codegen`

See [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)
 for more options.

### 🔄 CI/CD Integration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#cicd-integration "Permanent link")

Validate generated models in your CI pipeline:

.github/workflows/validate-models.yml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-14-1) - uses: koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator@0.53.0   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-14-2)   with:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-14-3)     input: schemas/api.yaml    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#__codelineno-14-4)     output: src/models/api.py`

See [CI/CD Integration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/)
 for more options.

# 📋 GraphQL-only Options - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#graphql-only-options)

📋 GraphQL-only Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#graphql-only-options "Permanent link")

====================================================================================================================================================

📋 Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#options "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--graphql-no-typename`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#graphql-no-typename) | Exclude \_\_typename field from generated GraphQL models. |

* * *

`--graphql-no-typename`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#graphql-no-typename "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Exclude \_\_typename field from generated GraphQL models.

The `--graphql-no-typename` flag prevents the generator from adding the `typename__` field (aliased to `__typename`) to generated models. This is useful when using generated models for GraphQL mutations, as servers typically don't expect this field in input data.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --graphql-no-typename` 

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-1) type Book {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-2)   id: ID!  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-3)   title: String [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-4) } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-6) interface Node {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-7)   id: ID! [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-8) } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-10) input BookInput {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-11)   title: String! [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-1-12) }`

**Output:**

``[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-2) #   filename:  no-typename.graphql [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-3) #   timestamp: 2019-07-26T00:00:00+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-7) from typing import TypeAlias [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-9) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-10)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-11) Boolean: TypeAlias = bool [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-12) """ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-13) The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`. [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-14) """ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-15) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-16)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-17) ID: TypeAlias = str [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-18) """ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-19) The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID. [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-20) """ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-21) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-22)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-23) String: TypeAlias = str [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-24) """ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-25) The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text. [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-26) """ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-27) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-28)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-29) class Node(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-30)     id: ID [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-31) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-32)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-33) class Book(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-34)     id: ID    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-35)     title: String | None = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-36) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-37)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-38) class BookInput(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/graphql-only-options/#__codelineno-2-39)     title: String``

* * *

# 📝 Utility Options - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#utility-options)

📝 Utility Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#utility-options "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================================================================

📋 Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#options "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--debug`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#debug) | Show debug messages during code generation |
| [`--generate-prompt`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#generate-prompt) | Generate a prompt for consulting LLMs about CLI options |
| [`--help`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#help) | Show help message and exit |
| [`--no-color`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#no-color) | Disable colorized output |
| [`--profile`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#profile) | Use a named profile from pyproject.toml |
| [`--version`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#version) | Show program version and exit |

* * *

`--debug`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#debug "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Show debug messages during code generation.

Enables verbose debug output to help troubleshoot issues with schema parsing or code generation. Requires the `debug` extra to be installed.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --debug` 

Requires extra dependency

The debug feature requires the `debug` extra:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[debug]'`

* * *

`--generate-prompt`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#generate-prompt "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate a prompt for consulting LLMs about CLI options.

Outputs a formatted prompt containing your current options, all available options by category, and full help text. Pipe to CLI LLM tools or copy to clipboard for web-based LLM chats.

**See also:** [LLM Integration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/)
 for detailed usage examples

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-2-2) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt "How do I generate strict types?"` 

Quick Examples

**Pipe to CLI tools:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | claude -p    # Claude Code [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-3-2) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | codex exec   # OpenAI Codex`

**Copy to clipboard:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | pbcopy      # macOS [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-4-2) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | xclip -selection clipboard  # Linux [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-4-3) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | clip.exe    # WSL2`

* * *

`--help`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#help "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Show help message and exit.

Displays all available command-line options with their descriptions and default values.

**Aliases:** `-h`

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-5-1) datamodel-codegen --help` 

Output

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-6-1) usage: datamodel-codegen [-h] [--input INPUT] [--url URL] ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-6-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-6-3) Generate Python data models from schema files. [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-6-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-6-5) options:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-6-6)   -h, --help            show this help message and exit  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-6-7)   --input INPUT         Input file path (default: stdin)  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-6-8)   ...`

* * *

`--no-color`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#no-color "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Disable colorized output.

By default, datamodel-codegen uses colored output for better readability. Use this option to disable colors, which is useful for CI/CD pipelines or when redirecting output to files.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-7-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --no-color` 

Environment variable

You can also disable colors by setting the `NO_COLOR` environment variable:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-8-1) NO_COLOR=1 datamodel-codegen --input schema.json`

* * *

`--profile`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#profile "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use a named profile from pyproject.toml configuration.

Profiles allow you to define multiple named configurations in your pyproject.toml file. Each profile can override the default settings with its own set of options.

**Related:** [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --profile strict` 

Configuration (pyproject.toml)

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-2) # Default configuration [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-3) output-model-type = "pydantic_v2.BaseModel" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-5) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.strict] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-6) # Strict profile with additional options [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-7) strict-types = ["str", "int", "float", "bool"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-8) strict-nullable = true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-10) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.legacy] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-11) # Legacy profile for Pydantic v1 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-10-12) output-model-type = "pydantic.BaseModel"`

Use profiles:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-11-1) # Use the strict profile [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-11-2) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --profile strict [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-11-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-11-4) # Use the legacy profile [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-11-5) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --profile legacy`

* * *

`--version`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#version "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Show program version and exit.

Displays the installed version of datamodel-code-generator.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-12-1) datamodel-codegen --version` 

Output

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#__codelineno-13-1) datamodel-codegen version: 0.x.x`

* * *

# 📁 Base Options - datamodel-code-generator

`--encoding`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#encoding "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify character encoding for input and output files.

The `--encoding` flag sets the character encoding used when reading the schema file and writing the generated Python code. This is useful for schemas containing non-ASCII characters (e.g., Japanese, Chinese). Default is UTF-8, which is the standard encoding for JSON and most modern text files.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --encoding utf-8` 

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-3)   "title": "日本語Model",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-4)   "description": "モデルの説明文",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-5)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-6)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-7)     "名前": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-8)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-9)       "description": "ユーザー名"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-10)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-11)     "年齢": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-12)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-13)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-14)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-1-15) }`

**Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-2) #   filename:  encoding_test.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-3) #   timestamp: 2019-07-26T00:00:00+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-7) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-8) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-10) class 日本語Model(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-11)     名前: str | None = Field(None, description='ユーザー名')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-2-12)     年齢: int | None = None`

`--input`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#input "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify the input schema file path.

The `--input` flag specifies the path to the schema file (JSON Schema, OpenAPI, GraphQL, etc.). Multiple input files can be specified to merge schemas. Required unless using `--url` to fetch schema from a URL.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --input pet_simple.json --output output.py` 

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-4-16) }`

`--input-file-type`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#input-file-type "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify the input file type for code generation.

The `--input-file-type` flag explicitly sets the input format.

**Important distinction:**

*   Use `jsonschema`, `openapi`, or `graphql` for **schema definition files**
*   Use `json`, `yaml`, or `csv` for **raw sample data** to automatically infer a schema

For example, if you have a JSON Schema written in YAML format, use `--input-file-type jsonschema`, not `--input-file-type yaml`. The `yaml` type treats the file as raw data and infers a schema from it.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-6-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --input-file-type json` 

Examples

[JSON](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__tabbed_1_1)
[YAML](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__tabbed_1_2)

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-7-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-7-2)   "Pet": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-7-3)     "name": "dog",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-7-4)     "age": 2  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-7-5)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-7-6) }`

`--input-model`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#input-model "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Import a Python type or dict schema from a module.

Use the format `module:Object` or `path/to/file.py:Object` to specify the type.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-11-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --input-model mymodule:MyModel` 

Examples

**Output:**

* * *

`--input-model-ref-strategy`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#input-model-ref-strategy "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Strategy for referenced types when using --input-model.

The `--input-model-ref-strategy` option determines whether to regenerate or import referenced types. Use `regenerate-all` (default) to regenerate all types, `reuse-foreign` to import types from different families (like enums when generating dataclasses) while regenerating same-family types, or `reuse-all` to import all referenced types directly.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-12-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --input-model-ref-strategy reuse-foreign` 

Examples

**Output:**

* * *

`--output`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#output "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify the destination path for generated Python code.

The `--output` flag specifies where to write the generated Python code. It can be either a file path (single-file output) or a directory path (multi-file output for modular schemas). If omitted, the generated code is written to stdout.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-13-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --input pet_simple.json --output output.py` 

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-14-16) }`

`--schema-version`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#schema-version "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Schema version to use for parsing.

The `--schema-version` option specifies the schema version to use instead of auto-detection. Valid values depend on input type: JsonSchema (draft-04, draft-06, draft-07, 2019-09, 2020-12) or OpenAPI (3.0, 3.1). Default is 'auto' (detected from $schema or openapi field).

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-16-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --schema-version draft-07` 

Examples

[OpenAPI](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__tabbed_2_1)
[JSON Schema](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__tabbed_2_2)

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-17-1) openapi: "3.0.0"  version: 1.0.0  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-17-4)   title: Swagger Petstore `--schema-version-mode`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#schema-version-mode "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Schema version validation mode.

The `--schema-version-mode` option controls how schema version validation is performed. 'lenient' (default): accept all features regardless of version. 'strict': warn on features outside the declared/detected version.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-20-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --schema-version-mode lenient` 

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-21-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-21-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-21-3)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-21-4)   "properties": {"s": {"type": ["string"]}},  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-21-5)   "required": ["s"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-21-6) }`

**Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-2) #   filename:  simple_string.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-4) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-6) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-7) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-9) class Model(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-22-10)     s: str`

* * *

`--url`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#url "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Fetch schema from URL with custom HTTP headers.

The `--url` flag specifies a remote URL to fetch the schema from instead of a local file. The `--http-headers` flag adds custom HTTP headers to the request, useful for authentication (e.g., Bearer tokens) or custom API requirements. Format: `HeaderName:HeaderValue`.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-23-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --url https://api.example.com/schema.json --http-headers "Authorization:Bearer token"` 

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#__codelineno-24-16) }`

# 🔍 Quick Reference - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#quick-reference)

🔍 Quick Reference[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#quick-reference "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================================================================

All CLI options in one page for easy **Ctrl+F** searching.

👆 Click any option to see detailed documentation with examples.

* * *

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen [OPTIONS]`

📂 All Options by Category[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#all-options-by-category "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 📁 Base Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#base-options "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--encoding`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#encoding) | Specify character encoding for input and output files. |
| [`--input`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#input) | Specify the input schema file path. |
| [`--input-file-type`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#input-file-type) | Specify the input file type for code generation. |
| [`--input-model`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#input-model) | Import a Python type or dict schema from a module. |
| [`--input-model-ref-strategy`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#input-model-ref-strategy) | Strategy for referenced types when using --input-model. |
| [`--output`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#output) | Specify the destination path for generated Python code. |
| [`--schema-version`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#schema-version) | Schema version to use for parsing. |
| [`--schema-version-mode`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#schema-version-mode) | Schema version validation mode. |
| [`--url`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/base-options/#url) | Fetch schema from URL with custom HTTP headers. |

### 🔧 Typing Customization[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#typing-customization "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--allof-class-hierarchy`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#allof-class-hierarchy) | Controls how allOf schemas are represented in the generated class hierarchy. |
| [`--allof-merge-mode`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#allof-merge-mode) | Merge constraints from root model references in allOf schemas. |
| [`--disable-future-imports`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#disable-future-imports) | Prevent automatic addition of **future** imports in generated code. |
| [`--enum-field-as-literal`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#enum-field-as-literal) | Convert all enum fields to Literal types instead of Enum classes. |
| [`--enum-field-as-literal-map`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#enum-field-as-literal-map) | Override enum/literal generation per-field via JSON mapping. |
| [`--ignore-enum-constraints`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#ignore-enum-constraints) | Ignore enum constraints and use base string type instead of Enum classes. |
| [`--no-use-closed-typed-dict`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-closed-typed-dict) | Disable PEP 728 TypedDict closed/extra\_items generation. |
| [`--no-use-specialized-enum`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-specialized-enum) | Disable specialized Enum classes for Python 3.11+ code generation. |
| [`--no-use-standard-collections`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-standard-collections) | Use typing.Dict/List instead of built-in dict/list for container types. |
| [`--no-use-union-operator`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-union-operator) | Use Union\[X, Y\] / Optional\[X\] instead of X |
| [`--output-date-class`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#output-date-class) | Specify date class type for date schema fields. |
| [`--output-datetime-class`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#output-datetime-class) | Specify datetime class type for date-time schema fields. |
| [`--strict-types`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#strict-types) | Enable strict type validation for specified Python types. |
| [`--type-mappings`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#type-mappings) | Override default type mappings for schema formats. |
| [`--type-overrides`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#type-overrides) | Replace schema model types with custom Python types via JSON mapping. |
| [`--use-annotated`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-annotated) | Use typing.Annotated for Field() with constraints. |
| [`--use-closed-typed-dict`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-closed-typed-dict) | Generate TypedDict with PEP 728 closed/extra\_items (default: enabled). |
| [`--use-decimal-for-multiple-of`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-decimal-for-multiple-of) | Generate Decimal types for fields with multipleOf constraint. |
| [`--use-generic-container-types`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-generic-container-types) | Use generic container types (Sequence, Mapping) for type hinting. |
| [`--use-non-positive-negative-number-constrained-types`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-non-positive-negative-number-constrained-types) | Use NonPositive/NonNegative types for number constraints. |
| [`--use-pendulum`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-pendulum) | Use pendulum types for date/time fields instead of datetime module. |
| [`--use-root-model-type-alias`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-root-model-type-alias) | Generate RootModel as type alias format for better mypy support. |
| [`--use-specialized-enum`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-specialized-enum) | Generate StrEnum/IntEnum for string/integer enums (Python 3.11+). |
| [`--use-standard-collections`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-standard-collections) | Use built-in dict/list instead of typing.Dict/List. |
| [`--use-standard-primitive-types`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-standard-primitive-types) | Use Python standard library types for string formats instead of str. |
| [`--use-tuple-for-fixed-items`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-tuple-for-fixed-items) | Generate tuple types for arrays with items array syntax. |
| [`--use-type-alias`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-type-alias) | Use TypeAlias instead of root models for type definitions (experimental). |
| [`--use-union-operator`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-union-operator) | Use |
| [`--use-unique-items-as-set`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-unique-items-as-set) | Generate set types for arrays with uniqueItems constraint. |

### 🏷️ Field Customization[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#field-customization "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--aliases`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#aliases) | Apply custom field and class name aliases from JSON file. |
| [`--capitalize-enum-members`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#capitalize-enum-members) | Capitalize enum member names to UPPER\_CASE format. |
| [`--default-values`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#default-values) | Override field default values from external JSON file. |
| [`--empty-enum-field-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#empty-enum-field-name) | Name for empty string enum field values. |
| [`--extra-fields`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#extra-fields) | Configure how generated models handle extra fields not defined in schema. |
| [`--field-constraints`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-constraints) | Generate Field() with validation constraints from schema. |
| [`--field-extra-keys`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-extra-keys) | Include specific extra keys in Field() definitions. |
| [`--field-extra-keys-without-x-prefix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-extra-keys-without-x-prefix) | Include schema extension keys in Field() without requiring 'x-' prefix. |
| [`--field-include-all-keys`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-include-all-keys) | Include all schema keys in Field() json\_schema\_extra. |
| [`--field-type-collision-strategy`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-type-collision-strategy) | Rename type class instead of field when names collide (Pydantic v2 only). |
| [`--no-alias`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#no-alias) | Disable Field alias generation for non-Python-safe property names. |
| [`--original-field-name-delimiter`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#original-field-name-delimiter) | Specify delimiter for original field names when using snake-case conversion. |
| [`--remove-special-field-name-prefix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#remove-special-field-name-prefix) | Remove the special prefix from field names. |
| [`--set-default-enum-member`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#set-default-enum-member) | Set the first enum member as the default value for enum fields. |
| [`--snake-case-field`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#snake-case-field) | Convert field names to snake\_case format. |
| [`--special-field-name-prefix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#special-field-name-prefix) | Prefix to add to special field names (like reserved keywords). |
| [`--use-attribute-docstrings`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-attribute-docstrings) | Generate field descriptions as attribute docstrings instead of Field description... |
| [`--use-enum-values-in-discriminator`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-enum-values-in-discriminator) | Use enum values in discriminator mappings for union types. |
| [`--use-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description) | Include schema descriptions as Field docstrings. |
| [`--use-field-description-example`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description-example) | Add field examples to docstrings. |
| [`--use-inline-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-inline-field-description) | Add field descriptions as inline comments. |
| [`--use-schema-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-schema-description) | Use schema description as class docstring. |
| [`--use-serialization-alias`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-serialization-alias) | Use serialization\_alias instead of alias for field aliasing (Pydantic v2 only). |
| [`--use-title-as-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-title-as-name) | Use schema title as the generated class name. |

### 🏗️ Model Customization[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#model-customization "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--allow-extra-fields`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#allow-extra-fields) | Allow extra fields in generated Pydantic models (extra='allow'). |
| [`--allow-population-by-field-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#allow-population-by-field-name) | Allow Pydantic model population by field name (not just alias). |
| [`--base-class`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#base-class) | Specify a custom base class for generated models. |
| [`--base-class-map`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#base-class-map) | Specify different base classes for specific models via JSON mapping. |
| [`--class-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#class-name) | Override the auto-generated class name with a custom name. |
| [`--class-name-affix-scope`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#class-name-affix-scope) | Control which classes receive the prefix/suffix. |
| [`--class-name-prefix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#class-name-prefix) | Add a prefix to all generated class names. |
| [`--class-name-suffix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#class-name-suffix) | Add a suffix to all generated class names. |
| [`--collapse-reuse-models`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#collapse-reuse-models) | Collapse duplicate models by replacing references instead of inheritance. |
| [`--collapse-root-models`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#collapse-root-models) | Inline root model definitions instead of creating separate wrapper classes. |
| [`--collapse-root-models-name-strategy`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#collapse-root-models-name-strategy) | Select which name to keep when collapsing root models with object references. |
| [`--dataclass-arguments`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#dataclass-arguments) | Customize dataclass decorator arguments via JSON dictionary. |
| [`--duplicate-name-suffix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#duplicate-name-suffix) | Customize suffix for duplicate model names. |
| [`--enable-faux-immutability`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#enable-faux-immutability) | Enable faux immutability in Pydantic v1 models (allow\_mutation=False). |
| [`--force-optional`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#force-optional) | Force all fields to be Optional regardless of required status. |
| [`--frozen-dataclasses`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#frozen-dataclasses) | Generate frozen dataclasses with optional keyword-only fields. |
| [`--keep-model-order`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#keep-model-order) | Keep model definition order as specified in schema. |
| [`--keyword-only`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#keyword-only) | Generate dataclasses with keyword-only fields (Python 3.10+). |
| [`--model-extra-keys`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#model-extra-keys) | Add model-level schema extensions to ConfigDict json\_schema\_extra. |
| [`--model-extra-keys-without-x-prefix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#model-extra-keys-without-x-prefix) | Strip x- prefix from model-level schema extensions and add to ConfigDict json\_sc... |
| [`--naming-strategy`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#naming-strategy) | Use parent-prefixed naming strategy for duplicate model names. |
| [`--output-model-type`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#output-model-type) | Select the output model type (Pydantic v1/v2, dataclasses, TypedDict, msgspec). |
| [`--parent-scoped-naming`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#parent-scoped-naming) | Namespace models by their parent scope to avoid naming conflicts. |
| [`--reuse-model`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#reuse-model) | Reuse identical model definitions instead of generating duplicates. |
| [`--reuse-scope`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#reuse-scope) | Scope for model reuse detection (root or tree). |
| [`--skip-root-model`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#skip-root-model) | Skip generation of root model when schema contains nested definitions. |
| [`--strict-nullable`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#strict-nullable) | Treat default field as a non-nullable field. |
| [`--strip-default-none`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#strip-default-none) | Remove fields with None as default value from generated models. |
| [`--target-pydantic-version`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#target-pydantic-version) | Target Pydantic version for generated code compatibility. |
| [`--target-python-version`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#target-python-version) | Target Python version for generated code syntax and imports. |
| [`--union-mode`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#union-mode) | Union mode for combining anyOf/oneOf schemas (smart or left\_to\_right). |
| [`--use-default`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-default) | Use default values from schema in generated models. |
| [`--use-default-factory-for-optional-nested-models`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-default-factory-for-optional-nested-models) | Generate default\_factory for optional nested model fields. |
| [`--use-default-kwarg`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-default-kwarg) | Use default= keyword argument instead of positional argument for fields with def... |
| [`--use-frozen-field`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-frozen-field) | Generate frozen (immutable) field definitions for readOnly properties. |
| [`--use-generic-base-class`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-generic-base-class) | Generate a shared base class with model configuration to avoid repetition (DRY).... |
| [`--use-one-literal-as-default`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-one-literal-as-default) | Use single literal value as default when enum has only one option. |
| [`--use-serialize-as-any`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-serialize-as-any) | Wrap fields with subtypes in Pydantic's SerializeAsAny. |
| [`--use-subclass-enum`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-subclass-enum) | Generate typed Enum subclasses for enums with specific field types. |

### 🎨 Template Customization[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#template-customization "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--additional-imports`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#additional-imports) | Add custom imports to generated output files. |
| [`--class-decorators`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#class-decorators) | Add custom decorators to generated model classes. |
| [`--custom-file-header`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-file-header) | Add custom header text to the generated file. |
| [`--custom-file-header-path`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-file-header-path) | Add custom header content from file to generated code. |
| [`--custom-formatters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-formatters) | Apply custom Python code formatters to generated output. |
| [`--custom-formatters-kwargs`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-formatters-kwargs) | Pass custom arguments to custom formatters via JSON file. |
| [`--custom-template-dir`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-template-dir) | Use custom Jinja2 templates for model generation. |
| [`--disable-appending-item-suffix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#disable-appending-item-suffix) | Disable appending 'Item' suffix to array item types. |
| [`--disable-timestamp`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#disable-timestamp) | Disable timestamp in generated file header for reproducible output. |
| [`--enable-command-header`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#enable-command-header) | Include command-line options in file header for reproducibility. |
| [`--enable-version-header`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#enable-version-header) | Include tool version information in file header. |
| [`--extra-template-data`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#extra-template-data) | Pass custom template variables from JSON file for code generation. |
| [`--formatters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#formatters) | Specify code formatters to apply to generated output. |
| [`--no-treat-dot-as-module`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#no-treat-dot-as-module) | Keep dots in schema names as underscores for flat output. |
| [`--treat-dot-as-module`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#treat-dot-as-module) | Treat dots in schema names as module separators. |
| [`--use-double-quotes`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#use-double-quotes) | Use double quotes for string literals in generated code. |
| [`--use-exact-imports`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#use-exact-imports) | Import exact types instead of modules. |
| [`--validators`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#validators) | Add custom field validators to generated Pydantic v2 models. |
| [`--wrap-string-literal`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#wrap-string-literal) | Wrap long string literals across multiple lines. |

### 📘 OpenAPI-only Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#openapi-only-options "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--include-path-parameters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#include-path-parameters) | Include OpenAPI path parameters in generated parameter models. |
| [`--openapi-include-paths`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#openapi-include-paths) | Filter OpenAPI paths to include in model generation. |
| [`--openapi-scopes`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#openapi-scopes) | Specify OpenAPI scopes to generate (schemas, paths, parameters). |
| [`--read-only-write-only-model-type`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#read-only-write-only-model-type) | Generate separate request and response models for readOnly/writeOnly fields. |
| [`--use-operation-id-as-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#use-operation-id-as-name) | Use OpenAPI operationId as the generated function/class name. |
| [`--use-status-code-in-response-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#use-status-code-in-response-name) | Include HTTP status code in response model names. |
| [`--validation`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#validation) | Enable validation constraints (deprecated, use --field-constraints). |

| Option | Description |
