### ⚙️ General Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#general-options "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--all-exports-collision-strategy`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-collision-strategy) | Handle name collisions when exporting recursive module hierarchies. |
| [`--all-exports-scope`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-scope) | Generate **all** exports for child modules in **init**.py files. |
| [`--check`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#check) | Verify generated code matches existing output without modifying files. |
| [`--disable-warnings`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#disable-warnings) | Suppress warning messages during code generation. |
| [`--generate-cli-command`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#generate-cli-command) | Generate CLI command from pyproject.toml configuration. |
| [`--generate-pyproject-config`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#generate-pyproject-config) | Generate pyproject.toml configuration from CLI arguments. |
| [`--http-headers`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#http-headers) | Fetch schema from URL with custom HTTP headers. |
| [`--http-ignore-tls`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#http-ignore-tls) | Disable TLS certificate verification for HTTPS requests. |
| [`--http-query-parameters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#http-query-parameters) | Add query parameters to HTTP requests for remote schemas. |
| [`--http-timeout`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#http-timeout) | Set timeout for HTTP requests to remote hosts. |
| [`--ignore-pyproject`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#ignore-pyproject) | Ignore pyproject.toml configuration file. |
| [`--module-split-mode`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#module-split-mode) | Split generated models into separate files, one per model class. |
| [`--shared-module-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#shared-module-name) | Customize the name of the shared module for deduplicated models. |
| [`--watch`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#watch) | Watch input file(s) for changes and regenerate output automatically. |
| [`--watch-delay`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#watch-delay) | Set debounce delay in seconds for watch mode. |

### 📝 Utility Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/quick-reference/#utility-options "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--debug`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#debug) | Show debug messages during code generation |
| [`--generate-prompt`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#generate-prompt) | Generate a prompt for consulting LLMs about CLI options |
| [`--help`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#help) | Show help message and exit |
| [`--no-color`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#no-color) | Disable colorized output |
| [`--profile`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#profile) | Use a named profile from pyproject.toml |
| [`--version`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/utility-options/#version) | Show program version and exit |

# Help - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#help)

Help
====

`--help`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#help "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Show help message and exit.

Displays all available command-line options with their descriptions and default values.

**Aliases:** `-h`

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --help` 

Output

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-1-1) usage: datamodel-codegen [-h] [--input INPUT] [--url URL] ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-1-3) Generate Python data models from schema files. [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-1-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-1-5) options:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-1-6)   -h, --help            show this help message and exit  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-1-7)   --input INPUT         Input file path (default: stdin)  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/help/#__codelineno-1-8)   ...`

# Debug - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/debug/#debug)

Debug
=====

`--debug`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/debug/#debug "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Show debug messages during code generation.

Enables verbose debug output to help troubleshoot issues with schema parsing or code generation. Requires the `debug` extra to be installed.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/debug/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --debug` 

Requires extra dependency

The debug feature requires the `debug` extra:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/debug/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[debug]'`

---

| [`--validation`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#validation) | Enable validation constraints (deprecated, use --field-const... |

* * *

`--include-path-parameters`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#include-path-parameters "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Include OpenAPI path parameters in generated parameter models.

The `--include-path-parameters` flag adds path parameters (like /users/{userId}) to the generated request parameter models. By default, only query parameters are included. Use this with `--openapi-scopes parameters` to generate parameter models that include both path and query parameters.

**See also:** [OpenAPI-Specific Options](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --include-path-parameters --openapi-scopes schemas paths parameters # (1)!`

1.  `--include-path-parameters` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#__codelineno-1-1) openapi: "3.0.0" `--openapi-include-paths`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#openapi-include-paths "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Filter OpenAPI paths to include in model generation.

The `--openapi-include-paths` flag allows filtering which paths are processed.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --openapi-scopes paths schemas --openapi-include-paths /pets* # (1)!`

1.  `--openapi-include-paths` - the option documented here

Examples

`--openapi-scopes`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#openapi-scopes "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify OpenAPI scopes to generate (schemas, paths, parameters).

The `--openapi-scopes` flag configures the code generation behavior.

**See also:** [OpenAPI-Specific Options](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#__codelineno-6-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --openapi-scopes paths schemas # (1)!`

1.  `--openapi-scopes` - the option documented here

`--read-only-write-only-model-type`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#read-only-write-only-model-type "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate separate request and response models for readOnly/writeOnly fields.

The `--read-only-write-only-model-type` option controls how models with readOnly or writeOnly properties are generated. The 'request-response' mode creates separate Request and Response variants for each schema that contains readOnly or writeOnly fields, allowing proper type validation for API requests and responses without a shared base model.

**See also:** [OpenAPI-Specific Options](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --read-only-write-only-model-type request-response # (1)!`

1.  `--read-only-write-only-model-type` - the option documented here

`--use-operation-id-as-name`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#use-operation-id-as-name "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use OpenAPI operationId as the generated function/class name.

The `--use-operation-id-as-name` flag configures the code generation behavior.

**See also:** [OpenAPI-Specific Options](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#__codelineno-12-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-operation-id-as-name --openapi-scopes paths schemas parameters # (1)!`

1.  `--use-operation-id-as-name` - the option documented here

`--use-status-code-in-response-name`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#use-status-code-in-response-name "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Include HTTP status code in response model names.

The `--use-status-code-in-response-name` flag includes the HTTP status code in generated response model class names. Instead of generating ambiguous names like ResourceGetResponse, ResourceGetResponse1, ResourceGetResponse2, it generates clear names like ResourceGetResponse200, ResourceGetResponse400, ResourceGetResponseDefault.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#__codelineno-15-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-status-code-in-response-name --openapi-scopes schemas paths # (1)!`

1.  `--use-status-code-in-response-name` - the option documented here

`--validation`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#validation "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Enable validation constraints (deprecated, use --field-constraints).

The `--validation` flag configures the code generation behavior.

**Deprecated:** Use --field-constraints instead

**See also:** [Field Constraints](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/)
, [OpenAPI-Specific Options](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/#__codelineno-18-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --validation # (1)!`

1.  `--validation` - the option documented here

`--all-exports-collision-strategy`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-collision-strategy "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Handle name collisions when exporting recursive module hierarchies.

The `--all-exports-collision-strategy` flag determines how to resolve naming conflicts when using `--all-exports-scope=recursive`. The 'minimal-prefix' strategy adds the minimum module path prefix needed to disambiguate colliding names, while 'full-prefix' uses the complete module path. Requires `--all-exports-scope=recursive`.

**Related:** [`--all-exports-scope`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-scope)

**See also:** [Module Structure and Exports](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --all-exports-scope recursive --all-exports-collision-strategy minimal-prefix # (1)!`

1.  `--all-exports-collision-strategy` - the option documented here

`--all-exports-scope`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-scope "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate **all** exports for child modules in **init**.py files.

The `--all-exports-scope=children` flag adds **all** to each **init**.py containing exports from direct child modules. This improves IDE autocomplete and explicit exports. Use 'recursive' to include all descendant exports with collision handling.

**Related:** [`--all-exports-collision-strategy`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-collision-strategy)

**See also:** [Module Structure and Exports](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --all-exports-scope children # (1)!`

1.  `--all-exports-scope` - the option documented here

`--check`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#check "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Verify generated code matches existing output without modifying files.

The `--check` flag compares the generated output with existing files and exits with a non-zero status if they differ. Useful for CI/CD validation to ensure schemas and generated code stay in sync. Works with both single files and directory outputs.

**See also:** [CI/CD Integration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-6-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --disable-timestamp --check # (1)!`

1.  `--check` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`--disable-warnings`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#disable-warnings "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Suppress warning messages during code generation.

The --disable-warnings option silences all warning messages that the generator might emit during processing (e.g., about unsupported features, ambiguous schemas, or potential issues). Useful for clean output in CI/CD pipelines.

**See also:** [Model Reuse and Deduplication](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --disable-warnings # (1)!`

1.  `--disable-warnings` - the option documented here

`--generate-cli-command`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#generate-cli-command "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate CLI command from pyproject.toml configuration.

The `--generate-cli-command` flag reads your pyproject.toml configuration and outputs the equivalent CLI command. This is useful for debugging configuration issues or sharing commands with others.

**See also:** [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-12-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-cli-command # (1)!`

1.  `--generate-cli-command` - the option documented here

Examples

**Configuration (pyproject.toml):**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-13-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-13-2) input = "schema.yaml" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-13-3) output = "model.py"`

**Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-14-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.yaml --output model.py`

* * *

`--generate-pyproject-config`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#generate-pyproject-config "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate pyproject.toml configuration from CLI arguments.

The `--generate-pyproject-config` flag outputs a pyproject.toml configuration snippet based on the provided CLI arguments. This is useful for converting a working CLI command into a reusable configuration file.

**See also:** [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-15-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --generate-pyproject-config --input schema.yaml --output model.py # (1)!`

1.  `--generate-pyproject-config` - the option documented here

Examples

**Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-16-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-16-2) input = "schema.yaml" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-16-3) output = "model.py"`

* * *

`--http-headers`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#http-headers "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Fetch schema from URL with custom HTTP headers.

The `--url` flag specifies a remote URL to fetch the schema from instead of a local file. The `--http-headers` flag adds custom HTTP headers to the request, useful for authentication (e.g., Bearer tokens) or custom API requirements. Format: `HeaderName:HeaderValue`.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-17-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --url https://api.example.com/schema.json --http-headers "Authorization:Bearer token" # (1)!`

1.  `--http-headers` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-18-16) }`

`--http-ignore-tls`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#http-ignore-tls "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Disable TLS certificate verification for HTTPS requests.

The `--http-ignore-tls` flag disables SSL/TLS certificate verification when fetching schemas from HTTPS URLs. This is useful for development environments with self-signed certificates. Not recommended for production.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-20-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --url https://api.example.com/schema.json --http-ignore-tls # (1)!`

1.  `--http-ignore-tls` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-21-16) }`

`--http-query-parameters`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#http-query-parameters "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add query parameters to HTTP requests for remote schemas.

The `--http-query-parameters` flag adds query parameters to HTTP requests when fetching schemas from URLs. Useful for APIs that require version or format parameters. Format: `key=value`. Multiple parameters can be specified: `--http-query-parameters version=v2 format=json`.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-23-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --url https://api.example.com/schema.json --http-query-parameters version=v2 format=json # (1)!`

1.  `--http-query-parameters` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-24-16) }`

`--http-timeout`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#http-timeout "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Set timeout for HTTP requests to remote hosts.

The `--http-timeout` flag sets the timeout in seconds for HTTP requests when fetching schemas from URLs. Useful for slow servers or large schemas. Default is 30 seconds.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-26-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --url https://api.example.com/schema.json --http-timeout 60 # (1)!`

1.  `--http-timeout` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-27-16) }`

`--ignore-pyproject`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#ignore-pyproject "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ignore pyproject.toml configuration file.

The `--ignore-pyproject` flag tells datamodel-codegen to ignore any \[tool.datamodel-codegen\] configuration in pyproject.toml. This is useful when you want to override project defaults with CLI arguments, or when testing without project configuration.

**See also:** [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-29-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --ignore-pyproject # (1)!`

1.  `--ignore-pyproject` - the option documented here

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-30-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-30-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-30-3)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-30-4)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-30-5)     "firstName": {"type": "string"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-30-6)     "lastName": {"type": "string"}  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-30-7)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-30-8) }`

`--module-split-mode`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#module-split-mode "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Split generated models into separate files, one per model class.

The `--module-split-mode=single` flag generates each model class in its own file, named after the class in snake\_case. Use with `--all-exports-scope=recursive` to create an **init**.py that re-exports all models for convenient imports.

**Related:** [`--all-exports-scope`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-scope)
, [`--use-exact-imports`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#use-exact-imports)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-33-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --module-split-mode single --all-exports-scope recursive --use-exact-imports # (1)!`

1.  `--module-split-mode` - the option documented here

`--shared-module-name`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#shared-module-name "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Customize the name of the shared module for deduplicated models.

The `--shared-module-name` flag sets the name of the shared module created when using `--reuse-model` with `--reuse-scope=tree`. This module contains deduplicated models that are referenced from multiple files. Default is `shared`. Use this if your schema already has a file named `shared`.

Note: This option only affects modular output with tree-level model reuse.

**See also:** [Model Reuse and Deduplication](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-36-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --shared-module-name my_shared # (1)!`

1.  `--shared-module-name` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-37-16) }`

`--watch`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#watch "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Watch input file(s) for changes and regenerate output automatically.

The `--watch` flag enables continuous file monitoring mode. When enabled, datamodel-codegen watches the input file or directory for changes and automatically regenerates the output whenever changes are detected. Press Ctrl+C to stop watching.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-39-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --watch --check # (1)!`

1.  `--watch` - the option documented here

Requires extra dependency

The watch feature requires the `watch` extra:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-40-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[watch]'`

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`--watch-delay`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#watch-delay "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Set debounce delay in seconds for watch mode.

The `--watch-delay` option configures the debounce interval (default: 0.5 seconds) for watch mode. This prevents multiple regenerations when files are rapidly modified in succession. The delay ensures that after the last file change, the generator waits the specified time before regenerating.

**Related:** [`--watch`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#watch)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-44-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --watch --watch-delay 1.5 # (1)!`

1.  `--watch-delay` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-3)   "title": "Person",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-6)     "firstName": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-7)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-8)       "description": "The person's first name."    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-9)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-10)     "lastName": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-11)       "type": ["string", "null"],      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-12)       "description": "The person's last name."    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-13)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-14)     "age": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-15)       "description": "Age in years which must be equal to or greater than zero.",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-16)       "type": "integer",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-17)       "minimum": 0    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-18)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-19)     "friends": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-20)       "type": "array"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-21)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-22)     "comment": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-23)       "type": "null"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-24)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-25)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-45-26) }`

**Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#__codelineno-46-1) Watching`

# Version - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/version/#version)

Version
=======

`--version`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/version/#version "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Show program version and exit.

Displays the installed version of datamodel-code-generator.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/version/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --version # (1)!`

1.  `--version` - the option documented here

Output

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/version/#__codelineno-1-1) datamodel-codegen version: 0.x.x`

---

# Profile - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#profile)

Profile
=======

`--profile`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#profile "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use a named profile from pyproject.toml configuration.

Profiles allow you to define multiple named configurations in your pyproject.toml file. Each profile can override the default settings with its own set of options.

**Related:** [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --profile strict # (1)!`

1.  `--profile` - the option documented here

Configuration (pyproject.toml)

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-2) # Default configuration [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-3) output-model-type = "pydantic_v2.BaseModel" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-5) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.strict] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-6) # Strict profile with additional options [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-7) strict-types = ["str", "int", "float", "bool"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-8) strict-nullable = true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-10) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.legacy] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-11) # Legacy profile for Pydantic v1 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-1-12) output-model-type = "pydantic.BaseModel"`

Use profiles:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-2-1) # Use the strict profile [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-2-2) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --profile strict [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-2-4) # Use the legacy profile [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/profile/#__codelineno-2-5) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --profile legacy`

---

# No color - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/no-color/#no-color)

No color
========

`--no-color`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/no-color/#no-color "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Disable colorized output.

By default, datamodel-codegen uses colored output for better readability. Use this option to disable colors, which is useful for CI/CD pipelines or when redirecting output to files.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/no-color/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --no-color # (1)!`

1.  `--no-color` - the option documented here

Environment variable

You can also disable colors by setting the `NO_COLOR` environment variable:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/no-color/#__codelineno-1-1) NO_COLOR=1 datamodel-codegen --input schema.json`

---

# Generate prompt - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#generate-prompt)

Generate prompt
===============

`--generate-prompt`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#generate-prompt "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate a prompt for consulting LLMs about CLI options.

Outputs a formatted prompt containing your current options, all available options by category, and full help text. Pipe to CLI LLM tools or copy to clipboard for web-based LLM chats.

**See also:** [LLM Integration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/)
 for detailed usage examples

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt # (1)! [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#__codelineno-0-2) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt "How do I generate strict types?" # (2)!`

1.  `--generate-prompt` - generate prompt without a question
2.  Include a specific question in the prompt

Quick Examples

**Pipe to CLI tools:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#__codelineno-1-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | claude -p    # Claude Code [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#__codelineno-1-2) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | codex exec   # OpenAI Codex`

**Copy to clipboard:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | pbcopy      # macOS [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#__codelineno-2-2) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | xclip -selection clipboard  # Linux [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/manual/generate-prompt/#__codelineno-2-3) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | clip.exe    # WSL2`

---

# 📚 Supported Input Formats - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/#supported-input-formats)

📚 Supported Input Formats[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/#supported-input-formats "Permanent link")

============================================================================================================================================

This code generator supports the following input formats:

*   OpenAPI 3 (YAML/JSON, [OpenAPI Data Type](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/3.0.2.md#data-types)
    );
*   JSON Schema ([JSON Schema Core](http://json-schema.org/draft/2019-09/json-schema-validation.html)
     /[JSON Schema Validation](http://json-schema.org/draft/2019-09/json-schema-validation.html)
    );
*   JSON/YAML Data (it will be converted to JSON Schema);
*   Python dictionary (it will be converted to JSON Schema);
*   GraphQL schema ([GraphQL Schemas and Types](https://graphql.org/learn/schema/)
    );

📘 OpenAPI 3 and JSON Schema[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/#openapi-3-and-json-schema "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below are the data types and features recognized by datamodel-code-generator for OpenAPI 3 and JSON Schema.

✅ Implemented data types and features[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/#implemented-data-types-and-features "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 📊 Data Types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/#data-types "Permanent link")

*   string (supported keywords: pattern/minLength/maxLength)
*   number (supported keywords: maximum/exclusiveMaximum/minimum/exclusiveMinimum/multipleOf)
*   integer (supported keywords: maximum/exclusiveMaximum/minimum/exclusiveMinimum/multipleOf)
*   boolean
*   array
*   object

### 📝 String Formats[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/#string-formats "Permanent link")

*   date
*   datetime
*   time
*   password
*   email (requires [`email-validator`](https://github.com/JoshData/python-email-validator)
    )
*   idn-email (requires [`email-validator`](https://github.com/JoshData/python-email-validator)
    )
*   path
*   uuid (uuid1/uuid2/uuid3/uuid4/uuid5)
*   ulid (requires [`python-ulid`](https://github.com/mdomke/python-ulid)
    )
*   ipv4
*   ipv6
*   hostname
*   decimal
*   uri

### 🔗 Other schema[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/#other-schema "Permanent link")

*   enum (as enum.Enum or typing.Literal)
*   allOf (as Multiple inheritance)
*   anyOf (as typing.Union)
*   oneOf (as typing.Union)
*   $ref ([http extra](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#http-extra-option)
     is required when resolving $ref for remote files.)
*   $id (for [JSONSchema](https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/structuring.html#id)
    )

---

# 📊 Generate from JSON Data - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#generate-from-json-data)

📊 Generate from JSON Data[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#generate-from-json-data "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================================

Generate Pydantic models directly from JSON data. Under the hood, the generator uses [GenSON](https://pypi.org/project/genson/)
 to infer a JSON Schema from your input, then processes it the same way as [JSON Schema input](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/)
.

🚀 Quick Start[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#quick-start "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-0-2)     --input pets.json \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-0-3)     --input-file-type json \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-0-4)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-0-5)     --output model.py`

📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#example "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**pets.json**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-2)   "pets": [    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-3)     {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-4)       "name": "dog",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-5)       "age": 2    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-6)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-7)     {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-8)       "name": "cat",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-9)       "age": 1    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-10)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-11)     {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-12)       "name": "snake",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-13)       "age": 3,      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-14)       "nickname": "python"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-15)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-16)   ],  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-17)   "status": 200 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-1-18) }`

**✨ Generated model.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-2) #   filename:  pets.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-3) #   timestamp: 2020-04-27T16:08:21+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-7) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-8) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-10) class Pet(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-11)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-12)     age: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-13)     nickname: str | None = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-14) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-15)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-16) class Model(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-17)     pets: list[Pet]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#__codelineno-2-18)     status: int`

* * *

📖 See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsondata/#see-also "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   📋 [Generate from JSON Schema](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/)
     - JSON Schema input documentation
*   🖥️ [CLI Reference](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/)
     - Complete CLI options reference
*   📊 [Supported Data Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/)
     - Data type support details

# 🔷 Generate from GraphQL - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#generate-from-graphql)

🔷 Generate from GraphQL[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#generate-from-graphql "Permanent link")

===========================================================================================================================

Generate Pydantic models from GraphQL schema definitions.

🚀 Quick Start[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#quick-start "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-0-2)     --input schema.graphql \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-0-3)     --input-file-type graphql \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-0-4)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-0-5)     --output model.py`

Why `--output-model-type`?

Starting from version 0.53.0, explicitly specifying `--output-model-type` is recommended. See [Omitting --output-model-type is deprecated](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/#omitting---output-model-type-is-deprecated)
 for details.

📦 Installation

GraphQL support requires the `graphql` extra:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[graphql]'`

📝 Simple Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#simple-example "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Let's consider a simple GraphQL schema (more details in [GraphQL Schemas and Types](https://graphql.org/learn/schema/)
).

**schema.graphql**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-1) type Book {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-2)   id: ID!  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-3)   title: String  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-4)   author: Author [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-5) } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-7) type Author {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-8)   id: ID!  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-9)   name: String  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-10)   books: [Book] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-11) } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-12)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-13) input BooksInput {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-14)     ids: [ID!]! [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-15) } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-16)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-17) input AuthorBooksInput {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-2-18)     id: ID! 🚫 Excluding \_\_typename Field[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#excluding-__typename-field "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using generated models for GraphQL mutations, the `__typename` field may cause issues as GraphQL servers typically don't expect this field in input data.

Use the `--graphql-no-typename` option to exclude this field:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-10-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-10-2)     --input schema.graphql \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-10-3)     --input-file-type graphql \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-10-4)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-10-5)     --graphql-no-typename \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-10-6)     --output model.py`

**Before (default):**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-11-1) class Book(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-11-2)     id: ID    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-11-3)     title: String | None = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-11-4)     typename__: Literal['Book'] | None = Field('Book', alias='__typename')`

**After (with --graphql-no-typename):**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-12-1) class Book(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-12-2)     id: ID    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/graphql/#__codelineno-12-3)     title: String | None = None`

Union Type Discrimination

If your schema uses GraphQL union types and you rely on `__typename` for type discrimination during deserialization, excluding this field may break that functionality. Consider using this option only for input types or schemas without unions.

* * *
