# 📋 Generate from JSON Schema - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#generate-from-json-schema)

📋 Generate from JSON Schema[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#generate-from-json-schema "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================================================

Generate Pydantic models from JSON Schema definitions. See [Supported Data Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/#openapi-3-and-json-schema)
 for supported JSON Schema features.

🚀 Quick Start[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#quick-start "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-0-2)     --input person.json \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-0-3)     --input-file-type jsonschema \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-0-4)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-0-5)     --output model.py`

📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#example "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**person.json**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-3)   "title": "Person",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-6)     "firstName": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-7)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-8)       "description": "The person's first name."    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-9)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-10)     "lastName": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-11)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-12)       "description": "The person's last name."    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-13)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-14)     "age": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-15)       "description": "Age in years which must be equal to or greater than zero.",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-16)       "type": "integer",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-17)       "minimum": 0    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-18)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-19)     "friends": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-20)       "type": "array"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-21)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-22)     "comment": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-23)       "type": "null"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-24)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-25)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-1-26) }`

**✨ Generated model.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-2) #   filename:  person.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-3) #   timestamp: 2020-04-27T16:12:27+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-7) from typing import Any [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-9) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, conint [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-10) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-11)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-12) class Person(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-13)     firstName: str | None = Field(None, description="The person's first name.")    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-14)     lastName: str | None = Field(None, description="The person's last name.")    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-15)     age: conint(ge=0) | None = Field(        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-16)         None, description='Age in years which must be equal to or greater than zero.'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-17)     )    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-18)     friends: list | None = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-2-19)     comment: Any | None = None`

Tuple validation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#tuple-validation "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

JSON Schema's [`prefixItems`](https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/reference/array.html#tuple-validation)
 syntax lets you describe heterogeneous arrays.

When:

*   `prefixItems` is present
*   no `items` are specified
*   `minItems`/`maxItems` match the number of prefix entries

datamodel-code-generator emits precise tuple annotations.

### Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#example_1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-2)   "$defs": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-3)     "Span": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-4)       "type": "object",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-5)       "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-6)         "value": { "type": "integer" }      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-7)       },      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-8)       "required": ["value"]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-9)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-10)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-11)   "title": "defaults",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-12)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-13)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-14)     "a": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-15)       "type": "array",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-16)       "prefixItems": [        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-17)         { "$ref": "#/$defs/Span" },        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-18)         { "type": "string" }      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-19)       ],      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-20)       "minItems": 2,      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-21)       "maxItems": 2    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-22)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-23)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-24)   "required": ["a"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-3-25) }`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-1) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-2) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-4) class Span(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-5)     value: int [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-6) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-8) class Defaults(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-4-9)     a: tuple[Span, str]`

* * *

Custom Base Class with `customBasePath`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#custom-base-class-with-custombasepath "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can specify custom base classes directly in your JSON Schema using the `customBasePath` extension. This allows you to define base classes at the schema level without using CLI options.

### Single Base Class[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#single-base-class "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-3)   "title": "User",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-5)   "customBasePath": "myapp.models.UserBase",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-6)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-7)     "name": {"type": "string"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-8)     "email": {"type": "string"}  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-9)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-10)   "required": ["name", "email"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-5-11) }`

**Generated Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-6-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-6-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-6-3) from myapp.models import UserBase [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-6-4) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-6-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-6-6) class User(UserBase):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-6-7)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-6-8)     email: str`

### Multiple Base Classes (Mixins)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#multiple-base-classes-mixins "Permanent link")

You can also specify multiple base classes as a list to implement mixin patterns:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-3)   "title": "User",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-5)   "customBasePath": ["mixins.AuditMixin", "mixins.TimestampMixin"],  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-6)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-7)     "name": {"type": "string"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-8)     "email": {"type": "string"}  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-9)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-10)   "required": ["name", "email"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-7-11) }`

**Generated Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-8-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-8-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-8-3) from mixins import AuditMixin, TimestampMixin [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-8-4) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-8-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-8-6) class User(AuditMixin, TimestampMixin):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-8-7)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#__codelineno-8-8)     email: str`

Mixin Usage

When using multiple base classes, the specified classes are used directly without adding `BaseModel`. Ensure your mixins inherit from `pydantic.BaseModel` if you need Pydantic model behavior.

### Priority Resolution[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/#priority-resolution "Permanent link")

When multiple base class configurations are present, they are resolved in this order:

1.  **`--base-class-map`** (CLI option) - Highest priority
2.  **`customBasePath`** (JSON Schema extension)
3.  **`--base-class`** (CLI option) - Lowest priority (default for all models)

This allows you to set a default base class with `--base-class`, override specific models in the schema with `customBasePath`, and further override at the CLI level with `--base-class-map`.

* * *

# Schema Version Support - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#schema-version-support)

Schema Version Support[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#schema-version-support "Permanent link")

====================================================================================================================================

This document describes the JSON Schema and OpenAPI versions supported by datamodel-code-generator.

Overview[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#overview "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

datamodel-code-generator supports multiple versions of JSON Schema and OpenAPI specifications. By default, the tool operates in **Lenient mode**, accepting all features regardless of version declarations. This ensures maximum compatibility with real-world schemas that often mix features from different versions.

JSON Schema Version Support[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#json-schema-version-support "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Supported Versions[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#supported-versions "Permanent link")

| Version | Spec URL | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Draft 4 | [json-schema.org/draft-04](https://json-schema.org/draft-04/json-schema-core) | `id`, `definitions` |
| Draft 6 | [json-schema.org/draft-06](https://json-schema.org/draft-06/json-schema-release-notes) | `$id`, const, boolean schemas |
| Draft 7 | [json-schema.org/draft-07](https://json-schema.org/draft-07/json-schema-release-notes) | if/then/else, readOnly/writeOnly |
| 2019-09 | [json-schema.org/draft/2019-09](https://json-schema.org/draft/2019-09/release-notes) | `$defs`, `$anchor` |
| 2020-12 | [json-schema.org/draft/2020-12](https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/release-notes) | `prefixItems`, null in type arrays |

### Feature Compatibility Matrix[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#feature-compatibility-matrix "Permanent link")

| Feature | Draft 4 | Draft 6 | Draft 7 | 2019-09 | 2020-12 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **ID/Reference** |     |     |     |     |     |
| ID field | `id` | `$id` | `$id` | `$id` | `$id` |
| Definitions key | `definitions` | `definitions` | `definitions` | `$defs`\* | `$defs` |
| **Type Features** |     |     |     |     |     |
| Boolean schemas | \-  | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Null in type array | \-  | \-  | \-  | \-  | Yes |
| const | \-  | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| **Numeric Constraints** |     |     |     |     |     |
| exclusiveMinimum (number) | \- (boolean) | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| exclusiveMaximum (number) | \- (boolean) | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| **Array Features** |     |     |     |     |     |
| prefixItems | \-  | \-  | \-  | \-  | Yes |
| items (as array/tuple) | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | \- (single only) |
| contains | \-  | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| **Conditional** |     |     |     |     |     |
| if/then/else | \-  | \-  | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| **Metadata** |     |     |     |     |     |
| readOnly | \-  | \-  | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| writeOnly | \-  | \-  | Yes | Yes | Yes |

\*2019-09 supports both `definitions` and `$defs` for backward compatibility.

### Version Detection[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#version-detection "Permanent link")

datamodel-code-generator automatically detects the JSON Schema version:

1.  **Explicit `$schema` field**: If present, the version is detected from the URL pattern
2.  **Heuristics**: If no `$schema`, presence of `$defs` suggests 2020-12, `definitions` suggests Draft 7
3.  **Fallback**: Draft 7 (most widely used)

OpenAPI Version Support[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#openapi-version-support "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Supported Versions[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#supported-versions_1 "Permanent link")

| Version | Spec URL | JSON Schema Base |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 3.0.x | [spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3) | Draft 5 (subset) |
| 3.1.x | [spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.1.0](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.1.0) | 2020-12 (full) |

> **Note**: OpenAPI 2.0 (Swagger) support is limited. We recommend converting to OpenAPI 3.0+.

### Feature Compatibility Matrix[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#feature-compatibility-matrix_1 "Permanent link")

| Feature | OAS 3.0 | OAS 3.1 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Schema Base** | JSON Schema Draft 5 (subset) | JSON Schema 2020-12 (full) |
| **Definitions Path** | `#/components/schemas` | `#/components/schemas` |
| **Nullable** |     |     |
| `nullable: true` keyword | Yes | Deprecated |
| Null in type array | \-  | Yes |
| Type as array | \-  | Yes |
| **Array Features** |     |     |
| prefixItems | \-  | Yes |
| **Boolean Schemas** | \-  | Yes |
| **OpenAPI Specific** |     |     |
| discriminator | Yes | Yes |
| binary format | Yes | Yes |
| password format | Yes | Yes |
| webhooks | \-  | Yes |

### Version Detection[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#version-detection_1 "Permanent link")

datamodel-code-generator detects the OpenAPI version from the `openapi` field:

*   `openapi: "3.0.x"` -> OpenAPI 3.0
*   `openapi: "3.1.x"` -> OpenAPI 3.1
*   No `openapi` field -> Fallback to OpenAPI 3.1

### Supported Features (from code)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#supported-features-from-code "Permanent link")

The following features are tracked in the codebase with their implementation status:

#### JSON Schema Features[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#json-schema-features "Permanent link")

| Feature | Introduced | Status | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `Null in type array` | 2020-12 | ✅ Supported | Allows `type: ['string', 'null']` syntax for nullable types |
| `$defs` | 2019-09 | ✅ Supported | Uses `$defs` instead of `definitions` for schema definitions |
| `prefixItems` | 2020-12 | ✅ Supported | Tuple validation using `prefixItems` keyword |
| `Boolean schemas` | Draft 6 | ✅ Supported | Allows `true` and `false` as valid schemas |
| `$id` | Draft 6 | ✅ Supported | Schema identifier field (`id` in Draft 4, `$id` in Draft 6+) |
| `definitions/$defs` | Draft 4 | ✅ Supported | Key for reusable schema definitions |
| `exclusiveMinimum/Maximum as number` | Draft 6 | ✅ Supported | Numeric `exclusiveMinimum`/`exclusiveMaximum` (boolean in Draft 4) |
| `readOnly/writeOnly` | Draft 7 | ✅ Supported | Field visibility hints for read-only and write-only properties |
| `const` | Draft 6 | ✅ Supported | Single constant value constraint |
| `propertyNames` | Draft 6 | ✅ Supported | Dict key type constraints via pattern, enum, or $ref |
| `contains` | Draft 6 | ❌ Not Supported | Array contains at least one matching item |
| `deprecated` | 2019-09 | ⚠️ Partial | Marks schema elements as deprecated |
| `if/then/else` | Draft 7 | ❌ Not Supported | Conditional schema validation |
| `contentMediaType/contentEncoding` | Draft 7 | ❌ Not Supported | Content type and encoding hints for strings |
| `$anchor` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not Supported | Location-independent schema references |
| `$vocabulary` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not Supported | Vocabulary declarations for meta-schemas |
| `unevaluatedProperties` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not Supported | Additional properties not evaluated by subschemas |
| `unevaluatedItems` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not Supported | Additional items not evaluated by subschemas |
| `dependentRequired` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not Supported | Conditional property requirements |
| `dependentSchemas` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not Supported | Conditional schema application based on property presence |
| `$dynamicRef/$dynamicAnchor` | 2020-12 | ❌ Not Supported | Dynamic reference resolution across schemas |

#### OpenAPI-Specific Features[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#openapi-specific-features "Permanent link")

| Feature | Introduced | Status | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `nullable` | OAS 3.0 | ✅ Supported | Uses `nullable: true` for nullable types (deprecated in 3.1) |
| `discriminator` | OAS 3.0 | ✅ Supported | Polymorphism support via `discriminator` keyword |
| `webhooks` | OAS 3.1 | ✅ Supported | Top-level webhooks object for incoming events |
| `$ref with sibling keywords` | OAS 3.1 | ⚠️ Partial | $ref can coexist with description, summary (no allOf workaround) |
| `xml` | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not Supported | XML serialization metadata (name, namespace, prefix) |
| `externalDocs` | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not Supported | Reference to external documentation |
| `links` | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not Supported | Links between operations |
| `callbacks` | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not Supported | Callback definitions for webhooks |
| `securitySchemes` | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not Supported | API security mechanism definitions |

Data Format Support[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#data-format-support "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Common Formats (JSON Schema + OpenAPI)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#common-formats-json-schema-openapi "Permanent link")

| Type | Format | Python Type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| integer | int32 | `int` |
| integer | int64 | `int` |
| number | float | `float` |
| number | double | `float` |
| number | decimal | `Decimal` |
| string | date | `date` |
| string | date-time | `datetime` |
| string | time | `time` |
| string | duration | `timedelta` |
| string | email | `EmailStr` |
| string | uri | `AnyUrl` |
| string | uuid | `UUID` |
| string | byte | `bytes` (base64) |
| string | ipv4 | `IPv4Address` |
| string | ipv6 | `IPv6Address` |
| string | hostname | `str` |

### OpenAPI-Only Formats[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#openapi-only-formats "Permanent link")

| Type | Format | Python Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| string | binary | `bytes` | File content |
| string | password | `SecretStr` | Sensitive data |

Limitations and Known Issues[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#limitations-and-known-issues "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### JSON Schema - Unsupported Features[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#json-schema-unsupported-features "Permanent link")

| Feature | Introduced | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `$anchor` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not supported | Use `$ref` with `$id` instead |
| `$dynamicRef` / `$dynamicAnchor` | 2020-12 | ❌ Not supported | Dynamic references |
| `unevaluatedProperties` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not supported | Use `additionalProperties` instead |
| `unevaluatedItems` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not supported | Use `additionalItems` instead |
| `contentMediaType` | Draft 7 | ❌ Not supported | Content type hints ignored |
| `contentEncoding` | Draft 7 | ❌ Not supported | Encoding hints ignored |
| `contentSchema` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not supported | Nested content schema ignored |
| `$vocabulary` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not supported | Vocabulary declarations ignored |
| `$comment` | Draft 7 | ⚠️ Ignored | Comments not preserved in output |
| `deprecated` | 2019-09 | ⚠️ Partial | Recognized but not enforced |
| `examples` (array) | Draft 6 | ⚠️ Partial | Only first example used for Field default |
| Recursive `$ref` | Draft 4+ | ⚠️ Partial | Supported with `ForwardRef`, may require manual adjustment |
| `propertyNames` | Draft 6 | ✅ Supported | Dict key type constraints via pattern, enum, or $ref |
| `dependentRequired` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not supported | Dependent requirements ignored |
| `dependentSchemas` | 2019-09 | ❌ Not supported | Dependent schemas ignored |

### OpenAPI - Unsupported Features[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#openapi-unsupported-features "Permanent link")

| Feature | Introduced | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OpenAPI 2.0 (Swagger) | OAS 2.0 | ⚠️ Limited | Recommend converting to 3.0+ |
| `$ref` sibling keywords | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not supported | 3.0 spec limitation (fixed in 3.1) |
| `links` | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not supported | Runtime linking not applicable |
| `callbacks` | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not supported | Webhook callbacks ignored |
| `webhooks` | OAS 3.1 | ❌ Not supported | Top-level webhooks ignored |
| `security` definitions | OAS 2.0+ | ❌ Not supported | Security schemes not generated |
| `servers` | OAS 3.0 | ❌ Not supported | Server configuration ignored |
| `externalDocs` | OAS 2.0+ | ❌ Not supported | External documentation links ignored |
| `xml` | OAS 2.0+ | ❌ Not supported | XML serialization hints ignored |
| Request body `required` | OAS 3.0 | ⚠️ Partial | Affects field optionality |
| Header/Cookie parameters | OAS 3.0 | ⚠️ Partial | Generated but not validated |

### GraphQL - Unsupported Features[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#graphql-unsupported-features "Permanent link")

| Feature | Spec | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Directives | Core | ❌ Not supported | Custom directives ignored |
| Subscriptions | Core | ❌ Not supported | Only Query/Mutation types |
| Custom scalars | Core | ⚠️ Partial | Mapped to `Any` by default |
| Interfaces inheritance | Core | ⚠️ Partial | Flattened to concrete types |
| Federation directives | Apollo | ❌ Not supported | Apollo Federation not supported |
| Input unions | Proposal | ❌ Not supported | Not yet in GraphQL spec |

### Legend[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#legend "Permanent link")

*   ✅ Fully supported
*   ⚠️ Partial support or limitations
*   ❌ Not supported

### Mixed Version Schemas[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#mixed-version-schemas "Permanent link")

Real-world schemas often mix features from different versions. datamodel-code-generator handles this in **Lenient mode** (default):

*   Features from all versions are accepted
*   No warnings for version mismatches
*   Maximum compatibility with existing schemas

In **Strict mode** (`--schema-version-mode strict`), warnings are emitted for version-incompatible features.

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported_formats/#see-also "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Supported Data Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/supported-data-types/)
     - Complete data type support
*   [JSON Schema Guide](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/)
     - JSON Schema usage examples
*   [OpenAPI Guide](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/)
     - OpenAPI usage examples

# Module Structure and Exports - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#module-structure-and-exports)

Module Structure and Exports[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#module-structure-and-exports "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================================================

When generating models to a directory structure, datamodel-code-generator can automatically create `__init__.py` files with `__all__` exports. This page explains how to control this behavior.

Quick Overview[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#quick-overview "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--all-exports-scope` | Control which modules get `__all__` exports |
| `--all-exports-collision-strategy` | Handle name collisions in recursive exports |
| `--treat-dot-as-module` | Convert dots in names to nested modules |

* * *

`--all-exports-scope`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#-all-exports-scope "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Controls the scope of `__all__` generation in `__init__.py` files.

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `none` | No `__all__` generation (default) |
| `local` | Export only the module's own definitions |
| `recursive` | Export all definitions from child modules |

### Example: `none` (default)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#example-none-default "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-1-1) # models/__init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-1-2) # (empty or minimal imports)`

### Example: `local`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#example-local "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/ --all-exports-scope local`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-3-1) # models/__init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-3-2) from .user import User [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-3-3) from .order import Order [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-3-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-3-5) __all__ = ["User", "Order"]`

### Example: `recursive`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#example-recursive "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/ --all-exports-scope recursive`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-5-1) # models/__init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-5-2) from .user import User [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-5-3) from .order import Order [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-5-4) from .common.status import Status [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-5-5) from .common.types import ID, Timestamp [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-5-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-5-7) __all__ = ["User", "Order", "Status", "ID", "Timestamp"]`

* * *

`--all-exports-collision-strategy`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#-all-exports-collision-strategy "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using `--all-exports-scope recursive`, name collisions can occur if multiple modules define the same class name. This option controls how to handle them.

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `minimal-prefix` | Add minimum module path prefix to disambiguate |
| `full-prefix` | Use complete module path for all exports |

### The Problem[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#the-problem "Permanent link")

`` [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-6-1) models/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-6-2) ├── user/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-6-3) │   └── types.py      # defines `ID` [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-6-4) └── order/     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-6-5)     └── types.py      # also defines `ID` ``

Both modules define `ID`, causing a collision when exporting recursively.

### Solution: `minimal-prefix`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#solution-minimal-prefix "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-7-1) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/ \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-7-2)   --all-exports-scope recursive \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-7-3)   --all-exports-collision-strategy minimal-prefix`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-8-1) # models/__init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-8-2) from .user.types import ID as user_ID [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-8-3) from .order.types import ID as order_ID [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-8-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-8-5) __all__ = ["user_ID", "order_ID"]`

Only colliding names get prefixed.

### Solution: `full-prefix`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#solution-full-prefix "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/ \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-9-2)   --all-exports-scope recursive \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-9-3)   --all-exports-collision-strategy full-prefix`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-10-1) # models/__init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-10-2) from .user.types import ID as user_types_ID [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-10-3) from .order.types import ID as order_types_ID [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-10-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-10-5) __all__ = ["user_types_ID", "order_types_ID"]`

All names use the full module path prefix.

* * *

`--treat-dot-as-module`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#-treat-dot-as-module "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Converts dots in schema names to nested module directories.

### Without `--treat-dot-as-module`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#without-treat-dot-as-module "Permanent link")

Schema with `title: "api.v1.User"` generates:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-11-1) # models.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-11-2) class ApiV1User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-11-3)     ...`

### With `--treat-dot-as-module`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#with-treat-dot-as-module "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-12-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models/ --treat-dot-as-module`

Schema with `title: "api.v1.User"` generates:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-13-1) models/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-13-2) ├── __init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-13-3) └── api/     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-13-4)     ├── __init__.py    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-13-5)     └── v1/        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-13-6)         ├── __init__.py        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-13-7)         └── user.py      # contains class User`

This is useful for: - Organizing large schemas by namespace - Mirroring API versioning structure - Keeping related models together

* * *

Common Patterns[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#common-patterns "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Pattern 1: Flat output with local exports[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#pattern-1-flat-output-with-local-exports "Permanent link")

Best for small to medium projects with a single output file or simple structure.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-14-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.yaml --output models/ --all-exports-scope local`

### Pattern 2: Hierarchical with recursive exports[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#pattern-2-hierarchical-with-recursive-exports "Permanent link")

Best for large projects with many schemas organized by domain.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-15-1) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/ \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-15-2)   --all-exports-scope recursive \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-15-3)   --all-exports-collision-strategy minimal-prefix \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-15-4)   --treat-dot-as-module`

### Pattern 3: OpenAPI with module structure[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#pattern-3-openapi-with-module-structure "Permanent link")

Best for OpenAPI schemas with versioned endpoints.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-16-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models/ \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-16-2)   --treat-dot-as-module \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#__codelineno-16-3)   --all-exports-scope recursive`

* * *

Troubleshooting[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#troubleshooting "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Import errors after generation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#import-errors-after-generation "Permanent link")

If you see `ImportError: cannot import name 'X'`:

1.  Check if `__all__` is generated correctly
2.  Verify the module structure matches your imports
3.  Try `--all-exports-scope local` first, then `recursive`

### Name collisions[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#name-collisions "Permanent link")

If you see duplicate class names:

1.  Use `--all-exports-collision-strategy minimal-prefix`
2.  Or use `--all-exports-collision-strategy full-prefix` for maximum clarity
3.  Consider restructuring your schemas to avoid collisions

### Circular imports[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#circular-imports "Permanent link")

If you encounter circular import errors:

1.  Check the generated `__init__.py` files
2.  Consider using `--all-exports-scope local` instead of `recursive`
3.  Use lazy imports in your application code

* * *

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/#see-also "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [CLI Reference: `--all-exports-scope`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-scope)
    
*   [CLI Reference: `--all-exports-collision-strategy`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#all-exports-collision-strategy)
    
*   [CLI Reference: `--treat-dot-as-module`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#treat-dot-as-module)
    
*   [Model Reuse and Deduplication](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/)

---

# 📘 Generate from OpenAPI - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#generate-from-openapi)

📘 Generate from OpenAPI[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#generate-from-openapi "Permanent link")

===========================================================================================================================

Generate Pydantic models from OpenAPI 3 schema definitions.

🚀 Quick Start[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#quick-start "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input api.yaml --input-file-type openapi --output model.py`

📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#example "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

api.yaml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-1) openapi: "3.0.0" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-2) info:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-3)   version: 1.0.0  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-4)   title: Swagger Petstore  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-5)   license:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-6)     name: MIT [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-7) servers:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-8)   - url: http://petstore.swagger.io/v1 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-9) paths:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-10)   /pets:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-11)     get:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-12)       summary: List all pets      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-13)       operationId: listPets      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-14)       tags:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-15)         - pets      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-1-16) 📖 readOnly / writeOnly Properties[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#readonly-writeonly-properties "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OpenAPI 3.x supports `readOnly` and `writeOnly` property annotations:

*   📤 **readOnly**: Property is only returned in responses (e.g., `id`, `created_at`)
*   📥 **writeOnly**: Property is only sent in requests (e.g., `password`)

### ⚙️ Option: `--read-only-write-only-model-type`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#option-read-only-write-only-model-type "Permanent link")

This option generates separate Request/Response models based on these annotations.

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (not set) | Default. No special handling (backward compatible) |
| `request-response` | Generate only Request/Response models (no base model) |
| `all` | Generate base model + Request + Response models |

### 🎯 Usage Patterns[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#usage-patterns "Permanent link")

| Use Case | Recommended Option | Generated Models |
| --- | --- | --- |
| API client validation | `request-response` | `UserRequest`, `UserResponse` |
| Database ORM mapping | (not set) | `User` |
| Both client & ORM | `all` | `User`, `UserRequest`, `UserResponse` |

### 🔗 Behavior with allOf Inheritance[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#behavior-with-allof-inheritance "Permanent link")

When using `allOf` with `$ref`, fields from all referenced schemas are flattened into Request/Response models:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-1) components:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-2)   schemas:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-3)     Timestamps:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-4)       type: object      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-5)       properties:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-6)         created_at:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-7)           type: string          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-8)           format: date-time          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-9)           readOnly: true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-10)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-11)     User:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-12)       allOf:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-13)         - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Timestamps"        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-14)         - type: object          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-15)           properties:            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-16)             name:              [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#__codelineno-6-17)               type: string`

Generated `UserRequest` will exclude `created_at` (readOnly from Timestamps).

### ⚠️ Collision Handling[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#collision-handling "Permanent link")

If a schema named `UserRequest` or `UserResponse` already exists, the generated model will be named `UserRequestModel` or `UserResponseModel` to avoid conflicts.

### 📤 Supported Output Formats[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#supported-output-formats "Permanent link")

This option works with all output formats:

*   `pydantic.BaseModel` / `pydantic_v2.BaseModel`
*   `dataclasses.dataclass`
*   `typing.TypedDict`
*   `msgspec.Struct`

### 🔗 Supported $ref Types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/#supported-ref-types "Permanent link")

readOnly/writeOnly resolution works with local and file reference types:

| Reference Type | Example | Support |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Local | `#/components/schemas/User` | ✅ Supported |
| File | `./common.yaml#/User` | ✅ Supported |

* * *

# Output Model Types - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#output-model-types)

Output Model Types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#output-model-types "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================================================

datamodel-code-generator supports multiple output model types. This page compares them to help you choose the right one for your project.

Quick Comparison[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#quick-comparison "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Model Type | Validation | Serialization | Performance | Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Pydantic v2** | Runtime | Built-in | Fast | New projects, APIs, data validation |
| **Pydantic v2 dataclass** | Runtime | Built-in | Fast | Pydantic validation with dataclass syntax |
| **Pydantic v1** | Runtime | Built-in | Moderate | Legacy compatibility |
| **dataclasses** | None | Manual | Fastest | Simple data containers, no validation needed |
| **TypedDict** | Static only | Dict-compatible | N/A | Type hints for dicts, JSON APIs |
| **msgspec** | Runtime | Built-in | Fastest | High-performance serialization |

* * *

Pydantic v2 (Recommended)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#pydantic-v2-recommended "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Use `--output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel`**

Pydantic v2 is recommended for new projects. It offers better performance and a modern API.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --output model.py`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-1) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, RootModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-3) class Pet(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-4)     id: int = Field(..., ge=0)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-5)     name: str = Field(..., max_length=256)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-6)     tag: str | None = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-8) class Pets(RootModel[list[Pet]]):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-1-9)     root: list[Pet]`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#when-to-use "Permanent link")

*   New projects without Pydantic v1 dependencies
*   APIs requiring data validation
*   Projects needing JSON Schema generation from models

* * *

Pydantic v2 dataclass[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#pydantic-v2-dataclass "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Use `--output-model-type pydantic_v2.dataclass`**

Pydantic v2 dataclass combines the familiar dataclass syntax with Pydantic's validation capabilities.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.dataclass --output model.py`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-3-1) from pydantic.dataclasses import dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-3-2) from typing import Optional [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-3-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-3-4) @dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-3-5) class Pet:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-3-6)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-3-7)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-3-8)     tag: Optional[str] = None`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#when-to-use_1 "Permanent link")

*   Want to use dataclass syntax with Pydantic validation
*   Migrating from dataclasses but need validation
*   Prefer decorator-based class definition

* * *

Pydantic v1[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#pydantic-v1 "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Use `--output-model-type pydantic.BaseModel`** (default)

Pydantic v1 is the default for backward compatibility with existing codebases.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic.BaseModel --output model.py`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-1) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-3) class Pet(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-4)     id: int = Field(..., ge=0)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-5)     name: str = Field(..., max_length=256)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-6)     tag: Optional[str] = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-8) class Pets(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-5-9)     __root__: List[Pet]`

### Migration from v1 to v2[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#migration-from-v1-to-v2 "Permanent link")

Key differences when migrating:

| v1  | v2  | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `__root__` | `RootModel` | Custom root types now use `RootModel` class |
| `const` | Removed | Use `Literal` types instead |
| `min_items` | `min_length` |     |
| `max_items` | `max_length` |     |
| `unique_items` | Removed | List replaced by `set` type |
| `allow_mutation` | `frozen` | Inverse value |
| `regex` | `pattern` |     |
| `pydantic.Config` | `pydantic.ConfigDict` |     |

See [Pydantic v2 Migration Guide](https://docs.pydantic.dev/2.0/migration/)
 for details.

* * *

dataclasses[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#dataclasses "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Use `--output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass`**

Python's built-in dataclasses for simple data containers without runtime validation.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-6-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass --output model.py`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-7-1) from dataclasses import dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-7-2) from typing import Optional [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-7-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-7-4) @dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-7-5) class Pet:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-7-6)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-7-7)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-7-8)     tag: Optional[str] = None`

### Options for dataclasses[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#options-for-dataclasses "Permanent link")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--frozen-dataclasses` | Generate immutable dataclasses (`frozen=True`) |
| `--keyword-only` | Require keyword arguments (`kw_only=True`, Python 3.10+) |
| `--dataclass-arguments` | Custom decorator arguments as JSON |

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-8-1) # Frozen, keyword-only dataclasses [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-8-2) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-8-3)   --frozen-dataclasses --keyword-only --target-python-version 3.10`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#when-to-use_2 "Permanent link")

*   Simple data structures without validation needs
*   Performance-critical code where validation overhead matters
*   Interoperability with code expecting dataclasses

* * *

TypedDict[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#typeddict "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Use `--output-model-type typing.TypedDict`**

TypedDict provides static type checking for dictionary structures.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type typing.TypedDict --output model.py`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-10-1) from typing import TypedDict, NotRequired [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-10-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-10-3) class Pet(TypedDict):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-10-4)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-10-5)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-10-6)     tag: NotRequired[str]`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#when-to-use_3 "Permanent link")

*   Working with JSON APIs where data remains as dicts
*   Static type checking without runtime overhead
*   Gradual typing of existing dict-based code

* * *

msgspec[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#msgspec "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Use `--output-model-type msgspec.Struct`**

[msgspec](https://github.com/jcrist/msgspec)
 offers high-performance serialization with validation.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-11-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[msgspec]' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-11-2) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type msgspec.Struct --output model.py`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-12-1) from msgspec import Struct, field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-12-2) from typing import Union, UnsetType [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-12-3) from msgspec import UNSET [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-12-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-12-5) class Pet(Struct):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-12-6)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-12-7)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-12-8)     tag: Union[str, UnsetType] = UNSET`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#when-to-use_4 "Permanent link")

*   High-performance JSON/MessagePack serialization
*   Memory-efficient data structures
*   APIs with strict performance requirements

* * *

Choosing the Right Type[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#choosing-the-right-type "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-1) graph TD     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-2)     A[Need runtime validation?] -->|Yes| B[Need best performance?]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-3)     A -->|No| C[Need type hints for dicts?]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-4)     B -->|Yes| D[msgspec.Struct]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-5)     B -->|No| E[Pydantic v1 dependency?]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-6)     E -->|Yes| F[pydantic.BaseModel]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-7)     E -->|No| G[Prefer dataclass syntax?]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-8)     G -->|Yes| H[pydantic_v2.dataclass]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-9)     G -->|No| I[pydantic_v2.BaseModel]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-10)     C -->|Yes| J[typing.TypedDict]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#__codelineno-13-11)     C -->|No| K[dataclasses.dataclass]`

### Decision Guide[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#decision-guide "Permanent link")

1.  **API with validation** → Pydantic v2
2.  **Validation with dataclass syntax** → Pydantic v2 dataclass
3.  **Legacy Pydantic v1 project** → Pydantic v1
4.  **High-performance serialization** → msgspec
5.  **Simple data containers** → dataclasses
6.  **Dict-based JSON handling** → TypedDict

* * *

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/#see-also "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [CLI Reference: `--output-model-type`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#output-model-type)

*   [Pydantic Documentation](https://docs.pydantic.dev/)
    
*   [msgspec Documentation](https://jcristharif.com/msgspec/)
