# 🎨 Custom Code Formatters - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#custom-code-formatters)

🎨 Custom Code Formatters[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#custom-code-formatters "Permanent link")

=======================================================================================================================================

Create your own custom code formatters for specialized formatting needs.

🚀 Usage[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#usage "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pass the module path containing your formatter class:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input {your_input_file} --output {your_output_file} --custom-formatters "{path_to_your_module}.your_module"`

📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#example "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 1️⃣ Create your formatter[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#1-create-your-formatter "Permanent link")

### your\_module.py[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#your_modulepy "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-1-1) from datamodel_code_generator.format import CustomCodeFormatter [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-1-3) class CodeFormatter(CustomCodeFormatter):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-1-4)     def apply(self, code: str) -> str:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-1-5)         # Apply your custom formatting here        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-1-6)         # For example, add a custom header comment:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-1-7)         header = "# This code was formatted by custom formatter\n"        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-1-8)         return header + code`

### 2️⃣ Use your formatter[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#2-use-your-formatter "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output model.py --custom-formatters "mypackage.your_module"`

🔧 Passing Arguments[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#passing-arguments "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can pass keyword arguments to your custom formatter using `--custom-formatters-kwargs`:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output model.py \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-3-2)     --custom-formatters "mypackage.your_module" \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#__codelineno-3-3)     --custom-formatters-kwargs '{"line_length": 100}'`

* * *

📖 See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/#see-also "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   🖥️ [CLI Reference: `--custom-formatters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-formatters)
     - Detailed CLI option documentation
*   🔧 [CLI Reference: `--custom-formatters-kwargs`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-formatters-kwargs)
     - Pass arguments to custom formatters
*   🖌️ [Formatting](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/)
     - Built-in code formatting with black and isort

---

# 🐍 Generate from Python Models - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#python-model)

🐍 Generate from Python Models[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#python-model "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================================

Generate code from existing Python types: Pydantic models, dataclasses, TypedDict, or dict schemas. This is useful for converting between model types or generating from programmatically-defined schemas.

🚀 Quick Start[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#quick-start "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:User --output model.py`

Format[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#format "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-1-1) --input-model <path/to/file.py>:<ObjectName>`

Simply specify the **file path** and **object name** separated by `:`.

### Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#format-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-2-1) myproject/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-2-2) ├── src/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-2-3) │   └── models/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-2-4) │       └── user.py      # class User(BaseModel): ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-2-5) └── schemas.py           # USER_SCHEMA = {...}`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-3-1) # From file path (recommended - easy to copy-paste) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-3-2) datamodel-codegen --input-model src/models/user.py:User --output model.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-3-3) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./schemas.py:USER_SCHEMA --input-file-type jsonschema [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-3-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-3-5) # Windows paths also work [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-3-6) datamodel-codegen --input-model src\models\user.py:User`

Copy-paste friendly

Just copy the file path from your editor or file explorer, add `:ClassName`, and you're done!

### Module format (alternative)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#module-format "Permanent link")

You can also use Python module notation with dots:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model src.models.user:User [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-4-2) datamodel-codegen --input-model schemas:USER_SCHEMA --input-file-type jsonschema`

Current directory is auto-added to `sys.path`

No `PYTHONPATH` configuration needed.

File and package name conflict

If both `mymodule.py` and `mymodule/` directory exist, use `./` prefix:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-5-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:Model`

* * *

Supported Input Types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#supported-input-types "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Type | Description | Requires |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Pydantic BaseModel | Pydantic v2 models with `model_json_schema()` | Pydantic v2 |
| dataclass | Standard library `@dataclass` | Pydantic v2 (for TypeAdapter) |
| Pydantic dataclass | `@pydantic.dataclasses.dataclass` | Pydantic v2 |
| TypedDict | `typing.TypedDict` subclasses | Pydantic v2 (for TypeAdapter) |
| dict | Dict containing JSON Schema or OpenAPI spec | \-  |

Pydantic v2 Required

All Python type inputs (except raw dict) require Pydantic v2 runtime to convert to JSON Schema.

* * *

📝 Examples[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Pydantic BaseModel[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#pydantic-basemodel "Permanent link")

**mymodule.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-6-1) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-6-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-6-3) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-6-4)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-6-5)     age: int`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-7-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:User --output model.py`

**✨ Generated model.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-8-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-8-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-8-3) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-8-4) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-8-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-8-6) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-8-7)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-8-8)     age: int`

### Convert Pydantic to TypedDict[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#convert-pydantic-to-typeddict "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:User --output-model-type typing.TypedDict --output model.py`

**✨ Generated model.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-10-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-10-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-10-3) from typing import TypedDict [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-10-4) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-10-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-10-6) class User(TypedDict):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-10-7)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-10-8)     age: int`

### Standard dataclass[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#dataclass "Permanent link")

**mymodule.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-11-1) from dataclasses import dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-11-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-11-3) @dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-11-4) class User:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-11-5)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-11-6)     age: int`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-12-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:User --output model.py`

### TypedDict[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#typeddict "Permanent link")

**mymodule.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-13-1) from typing import TypedDict [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-13-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-13-3) class User(TypedDict):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-13-4)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-13-5)     age: int`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-14-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:User --output model.py`

### Dict Schema (JSON Schema)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#dict-jsonschema "Permanent link")

**mymodule.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-15-1) USER_SCHEMA = {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-15-2)     "type": "object",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-15-3)     "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-15-4)         "name": {"type": "string"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-15-5)         "age": {"type": "integer"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-15-6)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-15-7)     "required": ["name", "age"], [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-15-8) }`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-16-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:USER_SCHEMA --input-file-type jsonschema --output model.py`

Dict requires --input-file-type

When using a dict schema, you must specify `--input-file-type` (e.g., `jsonschema`, `openapi`).

### Dict Schema (OpenAPI)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#dict-openapi "Permanent link")

**mymodule.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-1) OPENAPI_SPEC = {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-2)     "openapi": "3.0.0",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-3)     "info": {"title": "API", "version": "1.0.0"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-4)     "paths": {},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-5)     "components": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-6)         "schemas": {            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-7)             "User": {                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-8)                 "type": "object",                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-9)                 "properties": {                    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-10)                     "name": {"type": "string"},                    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-11)                     "age": {"type": "integer"},                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-12)                 },            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-13)             }        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-14)         }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-15)     }, [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-17-16) }`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-18-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:OPENAPI_SPEC --input-file-type openapi --output model.py`

* * *

Custom Python Types with x-python-type[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#x-python-type "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using `x-python-type` in JSON Schema (via `WithJsonSchema` in Pydantic), the generator automatically resolves and generates the required imports.

### Automatic Import Resolution[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#import-resolution "Permanent link")

The generator supports many common Python types out of the box:

| Module | Supported Types |
| --- | --- |
| `typing` | `Any`, `Union`, `Optional`, `Literal`, `Final`, `ClassVar`, `Annotated`, `TypeVar`, `TypeAlias`, `Never`, `NoReturn`, `Self`, `LiteralString`, `TypeGuard`, `Type` |
| `collections` | `defaultdict`, `OrderedDict`, `Counter`, `deque`, `ChainMap` |
| `collections.abc` | `Callable`, `Iterable`, `Iterator`, `Generator`, `Awaitable`, `Coroutine`, `AsyncIterable`, `AsyncIterator`, `AsyncGenerator`, `Mapping`, `MutableMapping`, `Sequence`, `MutableSequence`, `Set`, `MutableSet`, `Collection`, `Reversible` |
| `pathlib` | `Path`, `PurePath` |
| `decimal` | `Decimal` |
| `uuid` | `UUID` |
| `datetime` | `datetime`, `date`, `time`, `timedelta` |
| `enum` | `Enum`, `IntEnum`, `StrEnum`, `Flag`, `IntFlag` |
| `re` | `Pattern`, `Match` |

For types not in this list, the generator dynamically searches common modules to resolve imports.

### Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#x-python-type-example "Permanent link")

**mymodule.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-1) from collections import defaultdict [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-2) from typing import Any, Annotated [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-3) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, WithJsonSchema [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-5) class Config(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-6)     data: Annotated[        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-7)         defaultdict[str, Annotated[dict[str, Any], Field(default_factory=dict)]],        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-8)         WithJsonSchema({'type': 'object', 'x-python-type': 'defaultdict[str, dict[str, Any]]'})    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-19-9)     ] | None = None`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-20-1) datamodel-codegen --input-model ./mymodule.py:Config --output-model-type typing.TypedDict`

**✨ Generated output**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-3) from collections import defaultdict [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-4) from typing import Any, TypedDict [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-6) from typing_extensions import NotRequired [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-7) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-9) class Config(TypedDict):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#__codelineno-21-10)     data: NotRequired[defaultdict[str, dict[str, Any]] | None]`

Fully Qualified Paths

You can also use fully qualified paths in `x-python-type` (e.g., `collections.defaultdict`), which are always resolved correctly regardless of the static mapping.

* * *

Mutual Exclusion[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-model/#mutual-exclusion "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`--input-model` cannot be used with:

*   `--input` (file input)
*   `--url` (URL input)
*   `--watch` (file watching)

* * *

# 🖌️ Code Formatting - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#code-formatting)

🖌️ Code Formatting[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#code-formatting "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================

Generated code is automatically formatted using code formatters. By default, `black` and `isort` are used to produce consistent, well-formatted output.

🎯 Default Behavior[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#default-behavior "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Future Change

In a future version, the default formatters will change from `black` and `isort` to `ruff`. To prepare for this change, consider switching to ruff now using `--formatters ruff-format ruff-check`.

**CLI users**: To suppress this warning, use `--disable-warnings` or explicitly specify `--formatters black isort`.

**Library users**: Explicitly pass `formatters=[Formatter.BLACK, Formatter.ISORT]` to suppress this warning.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-0-2)     --input schema.yaml \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-0-3)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-0-4)     --output model.py`

This runs the following formatters in order:

1.  **isort** - Sorts and organizes imports
2.  **black** - Formats code style

🛠️ Available Formatters[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#available-formatters "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Formatter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `black` | Code formatting (PEP 8 style) |
| `isort` | Import sorting |
| `ruff-check` | Linting with auto-fix |
| `ruff-format` | Fast code formatting (black alternative) |

### ⚡ Using ruff instead of black[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#using-ruff-instead-of-black "Permanent link")

[Ruff](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff)
 is a fast Python linter and formatter. To use it:

Installation Required

ruff is an optional dependency. Install it with:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[ruff]'`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-1) # Use ruff for both linting and formatting [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-2) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-3)     --formatters ruff-check ruff-format \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-4)     --input schema.yaml \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-5)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-6)     --output model.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-8) # Use ruff-format as a black replacement [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-9) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-10)     --formatters isort ruff-format \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-11)     --input schema.yaml \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-12)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-2-13)     --output model.py`

### 🚫 Disable formatting[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#disable-formatting "Permanent link")

`datamodel-codegen` requires at least one formatter when using the CLI `--formatters` option.

To disable built-in formatting entirely, configure it via `pyproject.toml`:

pyproject.toml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-3-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-3-2) formatters = []`

⚙️ Configuration via pyproject.toml[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#configuration-via-pyprojecttoml "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Formatters read their configuration from `pyproject.toml`. The tool searches for `pyproject.toml` in:

1.  The output file's directory
2.  Parent directories (up to the git repository root)

### 📝 Example Configuration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#example-configuration "Permanent link")

pyproject.toml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-1) [tool.black] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-2) line-length = 100 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-3) skip-string-normalization = true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-5) [tool.isort] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-6) profile = "black" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-7) line_length = 100 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-9) [tool.ruff] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-10) line-length = 100 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-11)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-12) [tool.ruff.format] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-4-13) quote-style = "single"`

💬 String Quotes[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#string-quotes "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, string quote style is determined by your formatter configuration. To force double quotes regardless of configuration:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-5-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-5-2)     --use-double-quotes \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-5-3)     --input schema.yaml \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-5-4)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#__codelineno-5-5)     --output model.py`

This overrides `skip_string_normalization` in black config.

🎨 Custom Formatters[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#custom-formatters "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can create custom formatters for specialized formatting needs. See [Custom Formatters](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/)
 for details.

* * *

📖 See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/#see-also "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   🖥️ [CLI Reference: `--formatters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#formatters)
     - Specify code formatters
*   💬 [CLI Reference: `--use-double-quotes`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#use-double-quotes)
     - Force double quotes
*   🎨 [Custom Formatters](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/)
     - Create your own formatters
*   ⚙️ [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)
     - Configure datamodel-codegen options

---

# Model Reuse and Deduplication - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#model-reuse-and-deduplication)

Model Reuse and Deduplication[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#model-reuse-and-deduplication "Permanent link")

============================================================================================================================================

When generating models from schemas, you may encounter duplicate model definitions. datamodel-code-generator provides options to deduplicate models and share them across multiple files, improving output structure, reducing diff sizes, and enhancing performance.

Quick Overview[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#quick-overview "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--reuse-model` | Deduplicate identical model/enum definitions |
| `--reuse-scope` | Control scope of deduplication (`root` or `tree`) |
| `--shared-module-name` | Name for shared module in multi-file output |
| `--collapse-root-models` | Inline root models instead of creating wrappers |
| `--use-type-alias` | Create TypeAlias for reusable field types (see [Reducing Duplicate Field Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#reducing-duplicate-field-types)<br>) |

* * *

`--reuse-model`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#-reuse-model "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `--reuse-model` flag detects identical enum or model definitions and generates a single shared definition instead of duplicates.

### Without `--reuse-model`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#without-reuse-model "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output model.py`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-1) # Duplicate enums for animal and pet fields [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-2) class Animal(Enum):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-3)     dog = 'dog'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-4)     cat = 'cat' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-6) class Pet(Enum):  # Duplicate!     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-7)     dog = 'dog'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-8)     cat = 'cat' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-10) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-11)     animal: Optional[Animal] = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-1-12)     pet: Optional[Pet] = None`

### With `--reuse-model`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#with-reuse-model "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output model.py --reuse-model`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-3-1) # Single shared enum [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-3-2) class Animal(Enum):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-3-3)     dog = 'dog'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-3-4)     cat = 'cat' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-3-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-3-6) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-3-7)     animal: Optional[Animal] = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-3-8)     pet: Optional[Animal] = None  # Reuses Animal`

### Benefits[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#benefits "Permanent link")

*   **Smaller output** - Less generated code
*   **Cleaner diffs** - Changes to shared types only appear once
*   **Better performance** - Faster generation for large schemas
*   **Type consistency** - Same types are truly the same

* * *

`--reuse-scope`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#-reuse-scope "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Controls the scope for model reuse detection when processing multiple input files.

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `root` | Detect duplicates only within each input file (default) |
| `tree` | Detect duplicates across all input files |

### Single-file input[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#single-file-input "Permanent link")

For single-file input, `--reuse-scope` has no effect. Use `--reuse-model` alone.

### Multi-file input with `tree` scope[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#multi-file-input-with-tree-scope "Permanent link")

When generating from multiple schema files to a directory:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/ --reuse-model --reuse-scope tree`

**Input files:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-5-1) schemas/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-5-2) ├── user.json      # defines SharedModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-5-3) └── order.json     # also defines identical SharedModel`

**Output with `--reuse-scope tree`:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-6-1) models/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-6-2) ├── __init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-6-3) ├── user.py        # imports from shared [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-6-4) ├── order.py       # imports from shared [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-6-5) └── shared.py      # SharedModel defined once`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-1) # models/user.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-2) from .shared import SharedModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-4) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-5)     data: Optional[SharedModel] = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-7) # models/shared.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-8) class SharedModel(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-9)     id: Optional[int] = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-7-10)     name: Optional[str] = None`

* * *

`--shared-module-name`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#-shared-module-name "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Customize the name of the shared module when using `--reuse-scope tree`.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-8-1) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/ \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-8-2)   --reuse-model --reuse-scope tree --shared-module-name common`

**Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-9-1) models/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-9-2) ├── __init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-9-3) ├── user.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-9-4) ├── order.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-9-5) └── common.py      # Instead of shared.py`

* * *

`--collapse-root-models`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#-collapse-root-models "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Inline root model definitions instead of creating separate wrapper classes.

### Without `--collapse-root-models`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#without-collapse-root-models "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-10-1) class UserId(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-10-2)     __root__: str [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-10-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-10-4) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-10-5)     id: UserId`

### With `--collapse-root-models`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#with-collapse-root-models "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-11-1) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-11-2)     id: str  # Inlined`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#when-to-use "Permanent link")

*   Simpler output when wrapper classes aren't needed
*   Reducing the number of generated classes
*   When root models are just type aliases

* * *

Combining Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#combining-options "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Recommended for large multi-file projects[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#recommended-for-large-multi-file-projects "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-12-1) datamodel-codegen \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-12-2)   --input schemas/ \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-12-3)   --output models/ \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-12-4)   --reuse-model \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-12-5)   --reuse-scope tree \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-12-6)   --shared-module-name common \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-12-7)   --collapse-root-models`

This produces: - Deduplicated models across all files - Shared types in a `common.py` module - Inlined simple root models - Minimal, clean output

### Recommended for single-file projects[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#recommended-for-single-file-projects "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-13-1) datamodel-codegen \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-13-2)   --input schema.json \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-13-3)   --output model.py \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-13-4)   --reuse-model \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-13-5)   --collapse-root-models`

* * *

Performance Impact[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#performance-impact "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For large schemas with many models:

| Scenario | Without reuse | With reuse |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 100 schemas, 50% duplicates | 100 models | ~50 models |
| Generation time | Baseline | Faster (less to generate) |
| Output size | Large | Smaller |
| Git diff on type change | Multiple files | Single location |

Performance tip

For very large schemas, combine `--reuse-model` with `--disable-warnings` to speed up generation:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-14-1) datamodel-codegen --reuse-model --disable-warnings --input large-schema.json`

* * *

Output Structure Comparison[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#output-structure-comparison "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Without deduplication[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#without-deduplication "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-15-1) models/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-15-2) ├── user.py         # UserStatus enum [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-15-3) ├── order.py        # OrderStatus enum (duplicate of UserStatus!) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-15-4) └── product.py      # ProductStatus enum (duplicate!)`

### With `--reuse-model --reuse-scope tree`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#with-reuse-model-reuse-scope-tree "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-16-1) models/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-16-2) ├── __init__.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-16-3) ├── user.py         # imports Status from shared [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-16-4) ├── order.py        # imports Status from shared [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-16-5) ├── product.py      # imports Status from shared [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-16-6) └── shared.py       # Status enum defined once`

* * *

Reducing Duplicate Field Types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#reducing-duplicate-field-types "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When multiple classes share the same field type with identical constraints or metadata, you can reduce duplication by defining the type once in `$defs` and referencing it with `$ref`. Combined with `--use-type-alias`, this creates a single TypeAlias that's reused across all classes.

### Problem: Duplicate Annotated Fields[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#problem-duplicate-annotated-fields "Permanent link")

Without using `$ref`, each class gets its own inline field definition:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-17-1) class ClassA(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-17-2)     place_name: Annotated[str, Field(alias='placeName')]  # Duplicate! [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-17-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-17-4) class ClassB(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-17-5)     place_name: Annotated[str, Field(alias='placeName')]  # Duplicate!`

### Solution: Use `$defs` with `--use-type-alias`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#solution-use-defs-with-use-type-alias "Permanent link")

**Step 1: Define the shared type in `$defs`**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-2)   "$defs": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-3)     "PlaceName": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-4)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-5)       "title": "PlaceName",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-6)       "description": "A place name"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-7)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-8)     "ClassA": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-9)       "type": "object",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-10)       "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-11)         "place_name": { "$ref": "#/$defs/PlaceName" }      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-12)       }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-13)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-14)     "ClassB": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-15)       "type": "object",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-16)       "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-17)         "place_name": { "$ref": "#/$defs/PlaceName" }      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-18)       }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-19)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-20)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-18-21) }`

**Step 2: Generate with `--use-type-alias`**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-19-1) datamodel-codegen \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-19-2)   --input schema.json \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-19-3)   --output model.py \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-19-4)   --use-type-alias`

### Result: Single TypeAlias reused across classes[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#result-single-typealias-reused-across-classes "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-1) PlaceName = TypeAliasType(     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-2)     "PlaceName",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-3)     Annotated[str, Field(..., description='A place name', title='PlaceName')], [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-4) ) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-5) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-7) class ClassA(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-8)     place_name: PlaceName  # Reuses the TypeAlias [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-9) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-10)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-11) class ClassB(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#__codelineno-20-12)     place_name: PlaceName  # Reuses the TypeAlias`

### Benefits[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#benefits_1 "Permanent link")

*   **Single source of truth** - Field type is defined once
*   **Easier maintenance** - Change the type in one place
*   **Cleaner generated code** - No redundant annotations
*   **Type safety** - All fields share the exact same type

### When to Use This Pattern[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#when-to-use-this-pattern "Permanent link")

This pattern is ideal when:

*   Multiple classes share fields with the same constraints (e.g., `minLength`, `pattern`)
*   Fields have identical metadata (e.g., `description`, `examples`)
*   You want to ensure type consistency across your schema

* * *

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/#see-also "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [CLI Reference: `--reuse-model`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#reuse-model)

# Custom Class Decorators - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#custom-class-decorators)

Custom Class Decorators[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#custom-class-decorators "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================================================================

The `--class-decorators` option adds custom decorators to all generated model classes. This is useful for integrating with serialization libraries like `dataclasses_json`, or adding custom behavior to your models.

Why use this?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#why-use-this "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using `dataclasses.dataclass` output with `--snake-case-field`, Python field names are snake\_case but the original JSON keys may be camelCase. Libraries like `dataclasses_json` can handle this conversion automatically via decorators.

Example: Using dataclasses\_json[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#example-using-dataclasses_json "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Convert a JSON Schema with camelCase properties to dataclasses with snake\_case fields that serialize back to camelCase.

**schema.json**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-2)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-3)   "title": "User",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-4)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-5)     "firstName": { "type": "string" },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-6)     "lastName": { "type": "string" },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-7)     "emailAddress": { "type": "string" }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-8)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-9)   "required": ["firstName", "lastName"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-0-10) }`

### Without `--class-decorators`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#without-class-decorators "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-1-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-1-2)   --output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-1-3)   --snake-case-field`

**Generated model.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-3) from dataclasses import dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-4) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-6) @dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-7) class User:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-8)     first_name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-9)     last_name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-2-10)     email_address: str | None = None`

The field names are snake\_case, but there's no way to map them back to the original camelCase JSON keys.

* * *

### With `--class-decorators`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#with-class-decorators "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-3-2)   --output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-3-3)   --snake-case-field \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-3-4)   --class-decorators "@dataclass_json(letter_case=LetterCase.CAMEL)" \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-3-5)   --additional-imports "dataclasses_json.dataclass_json,dataclasses_json.LetterCase"`

**Generated model.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-3) from dataclasses import dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-5) from dataclasses_json import LetterCase, dataclass_json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-6) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-8) @dataclass_json(letter_case=LetterCase.CAMEL) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-9) @dataclass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-10) class User:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-11)     first_name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-12)     last_name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-4-13)     email_address: str | None = None`

Now serialization automatically converts between snake\_case and camelCase:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-5-1) user = User(first_name="John", last_name="Doe") [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-5-2) print(user.to_json()) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-5-3) # {"firstName": "John", "lastName": "Doe", "emailAddress": null}`

Usage Notes[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#usage-notes "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Multiple decorators**: Use comma separation for multiple decorators:
    
    `[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#__codelineno-6-1) --class-decorators "@decorator1,@decorator2"`
    
*   **@ prefix is optional**: Both `@dataclass_json` and `dataclass_json` work - the `@` is added automatically if missing.
    
*   **Combine with `--additional-imports`**: Always add the required imports for your decorators using `--additional-imports`.
    

Other Use Cases[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#other-use-cases "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `--class-decorators` option works with any output model type:

*   **Pydantic models**: Add custom validators or behavior
*   **TypedDict**: Add runtime type-checking decorators
*   **msgspec.Struct**: Add custom serialization hooks

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#see-also "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [CLI Reference: `--class-decorators`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#class-decorators)
     - Detailed CLI option documentation
*   [CLI Reference: `--additional-imports`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#additional-imports)
     - Adding custom imports

Related Issues[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/#related-issues "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [#2358](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/2358)
     - Feature request for dataclasses\_json support

---

# 🔒 Field Constraints - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#field-constraints)

🔒 Field Constraints[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#field-constraints "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================================

The `--field-constraints` option converts all `con*` annotations (like `conint`, `constr`) to `Field` constraint options.

🤔 Why use this?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#why-use-this "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mypy may show errors for `con*` annotations on fields. The `--field-constraints` option resolves this problem by using standard `Field()` constraints instead.

📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#example "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Convert simple JSON Schema `model.json` to pydantic model `model.py`:

**model.json**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-2)   "type":  "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-3)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-4)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-5)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-6)       "maxLength": 64    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-7)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-8)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-9)   "required": ["name"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-0-10) }`

### ❌ Without `--field-constraints` option[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#without-field-constraints-option "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-1-1) datamodel-codegen --input model.json --input-file-type jsonschema > model.py`

**Generated model.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-2) #   filename:  model.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-3) #   timestamp: 2020-07-20T15:37:56+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-7) from pydantic import BaseModel, constr [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-8) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-10) class  Model(BaseModel):   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-2-11)   name: constr(max_length=64)`

**🔴 Run mypy...**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-3-1) $ mypy model.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-3-2) model.py:3: error: Invalid type comment or annotation [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-3-3) model.py:3: note: Suggestion: use constr[...] instead of constr(...) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-3-4) Found 1 error in 1 file (checked 1 source file)`

mypy shows errors! 😱

* * *

### ✅ With `--field-constraints` option[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#with-field-constraints-option "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --input model.json --input-file-type jsonschema --field-constraints > model.py`

**Generated model.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-2) #   filename:  model.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-3) #   timestamp: 2020-07-20T15:47:21+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-7) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-8) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-10) class Model(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-5-11)     name: str = Field(..., max_length=64)`

**🟢 Run mypy...**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-6-1) $ mypy model.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#__codelineno-6-2) Success: no issues found in 1 source file`

No errors! 🎉

* * *

📖 See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#see-also "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   🖥️ [CLI Reference: `--field-constraints`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-constraints)
     - Detailed CLI option documentation with examples

🔗 Related Issues[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/#related-issues "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [pydantic/pydantic#156](https://github.com/samuelcolvin/pydantic/issues/156)

# 📦 Root Models and Type Aliases - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#root-models-and-type-aliases)

📦 Root Models and Type Aliases[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#root-models-and-type-aliases "Permanent link")

===========================================================================================================================================================

When a schema defines a simple type (not an object with properties), `datamodel-code-generator` creates a root model. If you don't want to introduce a new level of attribute access (`.root`) or want to use generated types as plain Python types in non-Pydantic code, you can use the `--use-type-alias` flag to generate type aliases instead of root models.

⚠️ Notes and Limitations[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#notes-and-limitations "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**This functionality is experimental!** Here are a few known issues:

*   📌 RootModel and type aliases do not fully support field-specific metadata (default, alias, etc). See [Named Type Aliases](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/types/#named-type-aliases)
     for details.
*   🚫 Type aliases do not support some RootModel features (e.g. `model_config`)
*   📄 A RootModel or type alias is also generated for the main schema, allowing you to define a single type alias from a schema file (e.g. `model.json` containing `{"title": "MyString", "type": "string"}`)
*   ❌ Type aliases cannot be combined with `Annotated` for Pydantic v1

📊 Type Alias Behavior by Output Type and Python Version[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#type-alias-behavior-by-output-type-and-python-version "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The type of type alias generated depends on the output model type and target Python version:

| Output Type | Python 3.12+ | Python 3.10-3.11 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Pydantic v2** | `type` statement | `TypeAliasType` (typing\_extensions) |
| **Pydantic v1** | `TypeAlias` | `TypeAlias` |
| **TypedDict** | `type` statement | `TypeAlias` |
| **dataclasses** | `type` statement | `TypeAlias` |
| **msgspec** | `type` statement | `TypeAlias` |

### 🤔 Why the difference?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#why-the-difference "Permanent link")

*   **Pydantic v2** requires `TypeAliasType` because it cannot properly handle `TypeAlias` annotations
*   **Other output types** (TypedDict, dataclasses, msgspec) use `TypeAlias` for better compatibility with libraries that may not expect `TypeAliasType` objects
*   **Python 3.12+** uses the native `type` statement for all output types

📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#example "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### model.json[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#modeljson "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-3)   "definitions": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-4)     "UserId": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-5)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-6)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-7)     "Status": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-8)       "anyOf": [        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-9)         {"type": "string"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-10)         {"type": "integer"}      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-11)       ]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-12)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-13)     "User": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-14)       "type": "object",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-15)       "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-16)         "id": {"$ref": "#/definitions/UserId"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-17)         "status": {"$ref": "#/definitions/Status"}      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-18)       }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-19)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-20)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-0-21) }`

### 🔹 Pydantic v1[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#pydantic-v1 "Permanent link")

#### Generating RootModel[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#generating-rootmodel "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-1-1) datamodel-codegen --input model.json --input-file-type jsonschema --output model.py`

### ✨ Generated model.py (Pydantic v1)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#generated-modelpy-pydantic-v1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-2-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/#__codelineno-2-2) #   filename:  model.json
