# 🎨 Custom Templates - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#custom-templates)

🎨 Custom Templates[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#custom-templates "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================================

One of the powerful features of datamodel-code-generator is the ability to use custom templates with the `--custom-template-dir` option. This allows you to provide a directory containing Jinja2 templates for customizing the generated code.

🚀 Usage[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#usage "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pass the directory path containing your custom templates:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input {your_input_file} --output {your_output_file} --custom-template-dir {your_custom_template_directory}`

📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#example "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Let's say you want to generate a custom Python data model from a JSON Schema file called `person.json`. You want the generated data model to include a custom comment at the top of the file.

### 1️⃣ Create the template directory[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#1-create-the-template-directory "Permanent link")

Create a directory structure matching the output model type:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-1-1) custom_templates/ [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-1-2) └── pydantic/     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-1-3)     └── BaseModel.jinja2`

### 2️⃣ Create the template[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#2-create-the-template "Permanent link")

**custom\_templates/pydantic/BaseModel.jinja2**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-2-1) # This is a custom comment generated with custom_template!! [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-2-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-2-3) class {{ class_name }}({{ base_class }}): [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-2-4) {%- for field in fields %}     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-2-5)     {{ field.name }}: {{ field.type_hint }} [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-2-6) {%- endfor -%}`

This custom template includes the custom comment at the top and replicates the default rendering behavior of the `BaseModel.jinja2` template.

### 3️⃣ Run the generator[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#3-run-the-generator "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --input person.json --output person.py --custom-template-dir custom_templates`

### ✨ Generated Output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#generated-output "Permanent link")

**person.py**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-2) #   filename:  person.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-3) #   timestamp: 2023-04-09T05:36:24+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-7) from typing import Optional [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-9) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-10)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-11) # This is a custom comment generated with custom_template!! [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-12) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-13)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-14) class Model(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-15)     name: Optional[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-4-16)     age: Optional[int]`

📚 Template Reference[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#template-reference "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can create more complex custom templates by copying [the default templates](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/tree/main/src/datamodel_code_generator/model/template)
. Use them as a reference for understanding the structure and available variables, and customize the code generation process according to your specific requirements.

* * *

🔧 Schema Extensions[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#schema-extensions "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OpenAPI and JSON Schema allow custom extension fields prefixed with `x-`. These schema extensions are automatically passed to templates via the `extensions` variable, enabling dynamic code generation based on your custom schema metadata.

### Example: Database Model Configuration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#example-database-model-configuration "Permanent link")

Suppose you want to add a `__collection__` class attribute based on a custom `x-database-model` extension in your OpenAPI schema:

**api.yaml**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-1) openapi: "3.0.0" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-2) info:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-3)   version: 1.0.0  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-4)   title: My API [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-5) paths: {} [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-6) components:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-7)   schemas:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-8)     User:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-9)       type: object      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-10)       x-database-model:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-11)         collection: users        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-12)         indexes:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-13)           - email      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-14)       properties:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-15)         id:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-16)           type: string        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-17)         email:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-18)           type: string      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-19)       required:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-20)         - id        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-5-21)         - email`

**custom\_templates/pydantic\_v2/BaseModel.jinja2**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-6-1) class {{ class_name }}({{ base_class }}): [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-6-2) {%- if extensions is defined and extensions.get('x-database-model') %}     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-6-3)     __collection__ = "{{ extensions['x-database-model']['collection'] }}" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-6-4) {%- endif %} [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-6-5) {%- for field in fields %}     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-6-6)     {{ field.name }}: {{ field.type_hint }} [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-6-7) {%- endfor %}`

**Run the generator**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-7-1) datamodel-codegen --input api.yaml --output models.py \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-7-2)     --custom-template-dir custom_templates \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-7-3)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel`

**Generated Output**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-3) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-4) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-6) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-7)     __collection__ = "users"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-8)     id: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#__codelineno-8-9)     email: str`

### Available Extensions[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#available-extensions "Permanent link")

Any field starting with `x-` in your schema will be available in the `extensions` dictionary:

| Schema Field | Template Access |
| --- | --- |
| `x-database-model` | `extensions['x-database-model']` |
| `x-custom-flag` | `extensions['x-custom-flag']` |
| `x-my-extension` | `extensions['x-my-extension']` |

### Use Cases[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#use-cases "Permanent link")

*   **Database mapping**: Add collection names, indexes, or ORM configurations
*   **Validation rules**: Include custom validation logic based on schema metadata
*   **Documentation**: Generate custom docstrings or comments from schema extensions
*   **Framework integration**: Add framework-specific decorators or attributes

* * *

📖 See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/#see-also "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   🖥️ [CLI Reference: `--custom-template-dir`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-template-dir)
     - Detailed CLI option documentation
*   📊 [CLI Reference: `--extra-template-data`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#extra-template-data)
     - Pass custom variables to templates

---

# Field Validators - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#field-validators)

Field Validators[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#field-validators "Permanent link")

=================================================================================================================

The `--validators` option allows you to add custom field validators to generated Pydantic v2 models. This enables you to inject validation logic into generated code without manually editing it.

Basic Usage[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#basic-usage "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output model.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-0-2)   --validators validators.json \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-0-3)   --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel`

Validators File Format[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#validators-file-format "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The validators file is a JSON file that maps model names to their validator definitions.

### Structure[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#structure "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-2)   "ModelName": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-3)     "validators": [      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-4)       {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-5)         "field": "field_name",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-6)         "function": "module.path.to.validator_function",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-7)         "mode": "after"      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-8)       }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-9)     ]  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-10)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-1-11) }`

### Fields[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#fields "Permanent link")

| Field | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `field` | Single field name to validate | One of `field` or `fields` |
| `fields` | List of field names (for multi-field validators) | One of `field` or `fields` |
| `function` | Fully qualified path to the validator function | Yes |
| `mode` | Validator mode: `before`, `after`, `wrap`, or `plain` | No (default: `after`) |

Validator Modes[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#validator-modes "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pydantic v2 supports different validator modes, each with its own signature:

### `before` / `after` Mode[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#before-after-mode "Permanent link")

Standard validators that run before or after Pydantic's own validation:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-2-1) def validate_name(v: Any, info: ValidationInfo) -> Any:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-2-2)     if not v:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-2-3)         raise ValueError("Name cannot be empty")    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-2-4)     return v.strip()`

### `wrap` Mode[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#wrap-mode "Permanent link")

Wrap validators receive a handler to call the next validator in the chain:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-1) from pydantic import ValidationInfo, ValidatorFunctionWrapHandler [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-3) def wrap_validate_name(     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-4)     v: Any,    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-5)     handler: ValidatorFunctionWrapHandler,    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-6)     info: ValidationInfo [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-7) ) -> Any:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-8)     # Pre-processing    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-9)     v = v.strip() if isinstance(v, str) else v    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-10)     # Call next validator    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-11)     result = handler(v)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-12)     # Post-processing    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-3-13)     return result.upper()`

### `plain` Mode[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#plain-mode "Permanent link")

Plain validators replace Pydantic's validation entirely:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-4-1) def plain_validate_name(v: Any) -> str:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-4-2)     if not isinstance(v, str):        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-4-3)         raise TypeError("Expected string")    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-4-4)     return v`

Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#example "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Input Schema[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#input-schema "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-2)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-3)   "title": "User",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-4)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-5)     "name": {"type": "string"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-6)     "email": {"type": "string", "format": "email"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-7)     "age": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0}  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-8)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-9)   "required": ["name", "email"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-5-10) }`

### Validators File[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#validators-file "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-2)   "User": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-3)     "validators": [      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-4)       {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-5)         "field": "name",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-6)         "function": "myapp.validators.validate_name",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-7)         "mode": "before"      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-8)       },      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-9)       {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-10)         "field": "email",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-11)         "function": "myapp.validators.validate_email",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-12)         "mode": "after"      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-13)       },      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-14)       {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-15)         "fields": ["name", "email"],        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-16)         "function": "myapp.validators.validate_contact_info",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-17)         "mode": "after"      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-18)       }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-19)     ]  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-20)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-6-21) }`

### Validator Functions (myapp/validators.py)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#validator-functions-myappvalidatorspy "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-1) from typing import Any [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-2) from pydantic import ValidationInfo [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-4) def validate_name(v: Any, info: ValidationInfo) -> Any:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-5)     if isinstance(v, str):        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-6)         return v.strip()    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-7)     return v [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-9) def validate_email(v: Any, info: ValidationInfo) -> Any:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-10)     if isinstance(v, str) and not v.endswith("@example.com"):        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-11)         # Custom email domain validation        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-12)         pass    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-13)     return v [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-14)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-15) def validate_contact_info(v: Any, info: ValidationInfo) -> Any:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-16)     # This runs for both name and email fields    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-7-17)     return v`

### Generated Output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#generated-output "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-3) from typing import Any [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-5) from myapp.validators import validate_contact_info, validate_email, validate_name [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-6) from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr, ValidationInfo, conint, field_validator [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-7) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-9) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-10)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-11)     email: EmailStr    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-12)     age: conint(ge=0) | None = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-13)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-14)     @field_validator('name', mode='before')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-15)     @classmethod    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-16)     def validate_name_validator(cls, v: Any, info: ValidationInfo) -> Any:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-17)         return validate_name(v, info) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-18)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-19)     @field_validator('email', mode='after')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-20)     @classmethod    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-21)     def validate_email_validator(cls, v: Any, info: ValidationInfo) -> Any:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-22)         return validate_email(v, info) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-23)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-24)     @field_validator('name', 'email', mode='after')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-25)     @classmethod    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-26)     def validate_contact_info_validator(cls, v: Any, info: ValidationInfo) -> Any:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#__codelineno-8-27)         return validate_contact_info(v, info)`

Notes[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#notes "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   This feature only supports Pydantic v2 (`--output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel`)
*   The `ModelName` in the validators file must match the generated Python class name
*   Validator functions are imported automatically based on the `function` path
*   When the same validator function is used multiple times, an incrementing suffix (`_1`, `_2`, etc.) is added to ensure method name uniqueness

* * *

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/#see-also "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [CLI Reference: `--validators`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/)
     - CLI option documentation
*   [Pydantic v2 Validators Documentation](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/validators/)
     - Official Pydantic documentation

---

# 🎨 Template Customization - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#template-customization)

🎨 Template Customization[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#template-customization "Permanent link")

==========================================================================================================================================================

📋 Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#options "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`--additional-imports`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#additional-imports) | Add custom imports to generated output files. |
| [`--class-decorators`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#class-decorators) | Add custom decorators to generated model classes. |
| [`--custom-file-header`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-file-header) | Add custom header text to the generated file. |
| [`--custom-file-header-path`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-file-header-path) | Add custom header content from file to generated code. |
| [`--custom-formatters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-formatters) | Apply custom Python code formatters to generated output. |
| [`--custom-formatters-kwargs`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-formatters-kwargs) | Pass custom arguments to custom formatters via JSON file. |
| [`--custom-template-dir`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-template-dir) | Use custom Jinja2 templates for model generation. |
| [`--disable-appending-item-suffix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#disable-appending-item-suffix) | Disable appending 'Item' suffix to array item types. |
| [`--disable-timestamp`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#disable-timestamp) | Disable timestamp in generated file header for reproducible ... |
| [`--enable-command-header`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#enable-command-header) | Include command-line options in file header for reproducibil... |
| [`--enable-version-header`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#enable-version-header) | Include tool version information in file header. |
| [`--extra-template-data`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#extra-template-data) | Pass custom template variables from JSON file for code gener... |
| [`--formatters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#formatters) | Specify code formatters to apply to generated output. |
| [`--no-treat-dot-as-module`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#no-treat-dot-as-module) | Keep dots in schema names as underscores for flat output. |
| [`--treat-dot-as-module`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#treat-dot-as-module) | Treat dots in schema names as module separators. |
| [`--use-double-quotes`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#use-double-quotes) | Use double quotes for string literals in generated code. |
| [`--use-exact-imports`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#use-exact-imports) | Import exact types instead of modules. |
| [`--validators`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#validators) | Add custom field validators to generated Pydantic v2 models.... |
| [`--wrap-string-literal`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#wrap-string-literal) | Wrap long string literals across multiple lines. |

* * *

`--additional-imports`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#additional-imports "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add custom imports to generated output files.

The `--additional-imports` flag allows you to specify custom imports as a comma-delimited list that will be added to the generated output file. This is useful when using custom types defined in external modules (e.g., "datetime.datetime,datetime.date,mymodule.myclass.MyCustomPythonClass").

**See also:** [Custom Class Decorators](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --additional-imports datetime.datetime,datetime.date,mymodule.myclass.MyCustomPythonClass # (1)!`

1.  `--additional-imports` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-1) scalar Date [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-3) "DateTime (ISO8601, example: 2020-01-01T10:11:12+00:00)" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-4) scalar DateTime [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-6) scalar MyCustomClass [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-8) type A {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-9)   a: Date!  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-10)   b: DateTime!  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-1-11)   c: MyCustomClass! `--class-decorators`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#class-decorators "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add custom decorators to generated model classes.

The `--class-decorators` option adds custom decorators to all generated model classes. This is useful for integrating with serialization libraries like `dataclasses_json`.

Use with `--additional-imports` to add the required imports for the decorators. The `@` prefix is optional and will be added automatically if missing.

**Related:** [`--additional-imports`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#additional-imports)
, [`--output-model-type`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#output-model-type)

**See also:** [Custom Class Decorators](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/class-decorators/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass --class-decorators @dataclass_json --additional-imports dataclasses_json.dataclass_json # (1)!`

1.  `--class-decorators` - the option documented here

Examples

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-4-1) { `--custom-file-header`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-file-header "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add custom header text to the generated file.

The `--custom-file-header` flag replaces the default "generated by datamodel-codegen" header with custom text. This is useful for adding copyright notices, license headers, or other metadata to generated files.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-6-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --custom-file-header "# Copyright 2024 MyCompany" # (1)!`

1.  `--custom-file-header` - the option documented here

`--custom-file-header-path`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-file-header-path "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add custom header content from file to generated code.

The `--custom-file-header-path` flag allows you to specify a file containing custom header content (like copyright notices, linting directives, or module docstrings) to be inserted at the top of generated Python files.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-10-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --custom-file-header-path custom_file_header.txt # (1)!`

1.  `--custom-file-header-path` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-11-1) openapi: "3.0.0" `--custom-formatters`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-formatters "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Apply custom Python code formatters to generated output.

The `--custom-formatters` flag allows you to specify custom Python functions that will be applied to format the generated code. The formatter is specified as a module path (e.g., "mymodule.formatter\_function"). This is useful for adding custom comments, modifying code structure, or applying project-specific formatting rules beyond what black/isort provide.

**See also:** [Custom Code Formatters](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-13-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --custom-formatters tests.data.python.custom_formatters.add_comment # (1)!`

1.  `--custom-formatters` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-14-5)   duration: Long! `--custom-formatters-kwargs`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-formatters-kwargs "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pass custom arguments to custom formatters via JSON file.

The `--custom-formatters-kwargs` flag accepts a path to a JSON file containing custom configuration for custom formatters (used with --custom-formatters). The file should contain a JSON object mapping formatter names to their kwargs.

Note: This option is primarily used with --custom-formatters to pass configuration to user-defined formatter modules.

**See also:** [Custom Code Formatters](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom-formatters/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-16-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --custom-formatters-kwargs formatter_kwargs.json # (1)!`

1.  `--custom-formatters-kwargs` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-17-16) }`

`--custom-template-dir`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#custom-template-dir "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use custom Jinja2 templates for model generation.

The `--custom-template-dir` option allows you to specify a directory containing custom Jinja2 templates to override the default templates used for generating data models. This enables full customization of the generated code structure and formatting. Use with `--extra-template-data` to pass additional data to the templates.

**See also:** [Custom Templates](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-19-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --custom-template-dir templates --extra-template-data openapi/extra_data.json # (1)!`

1.  `--custom-template-dir` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-20-1) openapi: "3.0.0" `--disable-appending-item-suffix`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#disable-appending-item-suffix "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Disable appending 'Item' suffix to array item types.

The `--disable-appending-item-suffix` flag configures the code generation behavior.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-22-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --disable-appending-item-suffix --field-constraints # (1)!`

1.  `--disable-appending-item-suffix` - the option documented here

Examples

`--disable-timestamp`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#disable-timestamp "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Disable timestamp in generated file header for reproducible output.

The `--disable-timestamp` flag configures the code generation behavior.

**See also:** [CI/CD Integration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-25-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --disable-timestamp # (1)!`

1.  `--disable-timestamp` - the option documented here

`--enable-command-header`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#enable-command-header "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Include command-line options in file header for reproducibility.

The `--enable-command-header` flag adds the full command-line used to generate the file to the header, making it easy to reproduce the generation.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-28-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --enable-command-header # (1)!`

1.  `--enable-command-header` - the option documented here

`--enable-version-header`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#enable-version-header "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Include tool version information in file header.

The `--enable-version-header` flag configures the code generation behavior.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-31-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --enable-version-header # (1)!`

1.  `--enable-version-header` - the option documented here

`--extra-template-data`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#extra-template-data "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pass custom template variables from JSON file for code generation.

The `--extra-template-data` flag allows you to provide additional variables (from a JSON file) that can be used in custom templates to configure generated model settings like Config classes, enabling customization beyond standard options.

**See also:** [Custom Templates](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/custom_template/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-34-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --extra-template-data openapi/extra_data.json # (1)!`

1.  `--extra-template-data` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`--formatters`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#formatters "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify code formatters to apply to generated output.

The `--formatters` flag specifies which code formatters to apply to the generated Python code. Available formatters are: black, isort, ruff, yapf, autopep8, autoflake. Default is \[black, isort\]. Use this to customize formatting or disable formatters entirely.

**See also:** [CI/CD Integration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/)
, [Code Formatting](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-38-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --formatters isort # (1)!`

1.  `--formatters` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-39-16) }`

`--no-treat-dot-as-module`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#no-treat-dot-as-module "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Keep dots in schema names as underscores for flat output.

The `--no-treat-dot-as-module` flag prevents splitting dotted schema names.

**See also:** [Module Structure and Exports](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-41-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --no-treat-dot-as-module # (1)!`

`--use-double-quotes`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#use-double-quotes "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use double quotes for string literals in generated code.

The --use-double-quotes option formats all string literals in the generated Python code with double quotes instead of the default single quotes. This helps maintain consistency with codebases that prefer double-quote formatting.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-47-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-double-quotes # (1)!`

1.  `--use-double-quotes` - the option documented here

`--use-exact-imports`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#use-exact-imports "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Import exact types instead of modules.

The `--use-exact-imports` flag changes import style from module imports to exact type imports. For example, instead of `from . import foo` then `foo.Bar`, it generates `from .foo import Bar`. This can make the generated code more explicit and easier to read.

Note: This option primarily affects modular output where imports between modules are generated. For single-file output, the difference is minimal.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-50-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-exact-imports # (1)!`

1.  `--use-exact-imports` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-3)   "title": "Pet",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-6)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-7)       "type": "integer"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-9)     "name": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-10)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-11)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-12)     "tag": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-13)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-14)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-15)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-51-16) }`

`--validators`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#validators "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add custom field validators to generated Pydantic v2 models.

The `--validators` option takes a JSON file defining validators per model. Each validator specifies the field(s) to validate, the validation function to import, and optionally the mode (before/after/wrap/plain). This allows injecting custom validation logic into generated models.

**See also:** [Field Validators](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/validators/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-53-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --validators tests/data/jsonschema/field_validators_config.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --disable-timestamp # (1)!`

1.  `--validators` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-54-1) { * * *

`--wrap-string-literal`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#wrap-string-literal "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Wrap long string literals across multiple lines.

The `--wrap-string-literal` flag breaks long string literals (like descriptions) across multiple lines for better readability, instead of having very long single-line strings in the generated code.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-56-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --wrap-string-literal # (1)!`

1.  `--wrap-string-literal` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-3)   "title": "LongDescription",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-4)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-6)     "summary": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-7)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-8)       "description": "summary for object"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-9)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-10)     "description": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-11)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-12)       "description": "datamodel-code-generator. This code generator creates pydantic model from an openapi file and others."    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-13)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-14)     "multi_line": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-15)       "description": "datamodel-code-generator\nThis code generator creates pydantic model from an openapi file and others.\n\n\nSupported source types\nOpenAPI 3 (YAML/JSON, OpenAPI Data Type)\nJSON Schema (JSON Schema Core/JSON Schema Validation)\nJSON/YAML/CSV Data (it will be converted to JSON Schema)\nPython dictionary (it will be converted to JSON Schema)",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-16)       "type": "string"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-17)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-18)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-57-19) }`

**Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-2) #   filename:  long_description.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-3) #   timestamp: 2019-07-26T00:00:00+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-7) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-8) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-10) class LongDescription(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-11)     summary: str | None = Field(None, description='summary for object')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-12)     description: str | None = Field(        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-13)         None,        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-14)         description=(            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#__codelineno-58-15)             'datamodel-code-generator.
