# ⚙️ pyproject.toml Configuration - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#pyprojecttoml-configuration)

⚙️ pyproject.toml Configuration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#pyprojecttoml-configuration "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================================================

datamodel-code-generator can be configured using `pyproject.toml`. The tool automatically searches for `pyproject.toml` in the current directory and parent directories (stopping at the git repository root).

🚀 Basic Usage[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#basic-usage "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-0-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-0-2) input = "schema.yaml" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-0-3) output = "models.py" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-0-4) target-python-version = "3.11" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-0-5) snake-case-field = true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-0-6) field-constraints = true`

All CLI options can be used in `pyproject.toml` by converting them to kebab-case (e.g., `--snake-case-field` becomes `snake-case-field`).

📋 Named Profiles[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#named-profiles "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can define multiple named profiles for different use cases within a single project:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-2) target-python-version = "3.10" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-3) snake-case-field = true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-5) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.api] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-6) input = "schemas/api.yaml" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-7) output = "src/models/api.py" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-8) target-python-version = "3.11" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-10) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.database] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-11) input = "schemas/db.json" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-12) output = "src/models/db.py" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-1-13) input-file-type = "jsonschema"`

Base settings in `[tool.datamodel-codegen]` are used when no profile is specified, and also serve as defaults for profiles.

Use a profile with the `--profile` option:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --profile api [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-2-2) datamodel-codegen --profile database`

🎯 Configuration Priority[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#configuration-priority "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Settings are applied in the following priority order (highest to lowest):

1.  **🖥️ CLI arguments** - Always take precedence
2.  **📋 Profile settings** - From `[tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.<name>]`
3.  **⚙️ Base settings** - From `[tool.datamodel-codegen]`
4.  **🔧 Default values** - Built-in defaults

🔀 Merge Rules[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#merge-rules "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using profiles, settings are merged using **shallow merge**:

*   Profile values **completely replace** base values (no deep merging)
*   Settings not specified in the profile are inherited from the base configuration
*   Lists and dictionaries are replaced entirely, not merged

### 📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#example "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-3-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-3-2) strict-types = ["str", "int"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-3-3) http-headers = ["Authorization: Bearer token"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-3-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-3-5) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.api] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-3-6) strict-types = ["bytes"]`

When using `--profile api`:

*   `strict-types` becomes `["bytes"]` (completely replaces base, not merged)
*   `http-headers` is inherited from base as `["Authorization: Bearer token"]`

🚫 Ignoring pyproject.toml[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#ignoring-pyprojecttoml "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To ignore all `pyproject.toml` configuration and use only CLI arguments:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --ignore-pyproject --input schema.yaml --output models.py`

🔧 Generating Configuration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#generating-configuration "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate a `pyproject.toml` configuration section from CLI arguments:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-5-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.yaml --output models.py --snake-case-field --generate-pyproject-config`

**✨ Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-6-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-6-2) input = "schema.yaml" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-6-3) output = "models.py" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-6-4) snake-case-field = true`

Generate CLI command from existing `pyproject.toml`:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-7-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-cli-command`

With a specific profile:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#__codelineno-8-1) datamodel-codegen --profile api --generate-cli-command`

* * *

📖 See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#see-also "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   🖥️ [CLI Reference: `--ignore-pyproject`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#ignore-pyproject)
     - Ignore pyproject.toml configuration
*   🔧 [CLI Reference: `--generate-pyproject-config`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#generate-pyproject-config)
     - Generate pyproject.toml from CLI arguments
*   🖥️ [CLI Reference: `--generate-cli-command`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#generate-cli-command)
     - Generate CLI command from pyproject.toml

---

# 🏷️ Field Aliases - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#field-aliases)

🏷️ Field Aliases[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#field-aliases "Permanent link")

============================================================================================================

The `--aliases` option allows you to rename fields in the generated models. This is useful when you want to use different Python field names than those defined in the schema while preserving the original names as serialization aliases.

🚀 Basic Usage[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#basic-usage "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-0-2)     --input schema.json \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-0-3)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-0-4)     --output model.py \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-0-5)     --aliases aliases.json`

📋 Alias File Format[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#alias-file-format "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The alias file is a JSON file that maps original field names to their Python aliases.

### 📝 Flat Format (Traditional)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#flat-format-traditional "Permanent link")

The simplest format applies aliases to all fields with the matching name, regardless of which class they belong to:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-1-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-1-2)   "id": "id_",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-1-3)   "type": "type_",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-1-4)   "class": "class_" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-1-5) }`

This will rename all fields named `id` to `id_`, all fields named `type` to `type_`, etc.

### 🎯 Scoped Format (Class-Specific)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#scoped-format-class-specific "Permanent link")

When you have the same field name in multiple classes but want different aliases for each, use the scoped format with `ClassName.field`:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-2-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-2-2)   "User.name": "user_name",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-2-3)   "Address.name": "address_name",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-2-4)   "name": "default_name" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-2-5) }`

**⚡ Priority**: Scoped aliases take priority over flat aliases. In the example above:

*   `User.name` will be renamed to `user_name`
*   `Address.name` will be renamed to `address_name`
*   Any other class with a `name` field will use `default_name`

📝 Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#example "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 📥 Input Schema[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#input-schema "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-2)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-3)   "title": "Root",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-4)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-5)     "name": {"type": "string"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-6)     "user": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-7)       "type": "object",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-8)       "title": "User",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-9)       "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-10)         "name": {"type": "string"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-11)         "id": {"type": "integer"}      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-12)       }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-13)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-14)     "address": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-15)       "type": "object",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-16)       "title": "Address",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-17)       "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-18)         "name": {"type": "string"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-19)         "city": {"type": "string"}      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-20)       }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-21)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-22)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-3-23) }`

### 🏷️ Alias File[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#alias-file "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-4-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-4-2)   "Root.name": "root_name",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-4-3)   "User.name": "user_name",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-4-4)   "Address.name": "address_name" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-4-5) }`

### ✨ Generated Output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#generated-output "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-1) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-3) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-4)     user_name: str | None = Field(None, alias='name')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-5)     id: int | None = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-7) class Address(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-8)     address_name: str | None = Field(None, alias='name')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-9)     city: str | None = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-10)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-11) class Root(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-12)     root_name: str | None = Field(None, alias='name')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-13)     user: User | None = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#__codelineno-5-14)     address: Address | None = None`

📌 Notes[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#notes "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The `ClassName` in scoped format must match the generated Python class name (after title conversion)
*   When using `--use-title-as-name`, the class name is derived from the `title` property in the schema
*   Aliases are applied during code generation, so the original field names are preserved as Pydantic `alias` values for proper serialization/deserialization

* * *

📖 See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/#see-also "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   🖥️ [CLI Reference: `--aliases`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#aliases)
     - Detailed CLI option documentation with examples

---

# Python Version Compatibility - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#python-version-compatibility)

Python Version Compatibility[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#python-version-compatibility "Permanent link")

===========================================================================================================================================================

datamodel-code-generator can generate code compatible with different Python versions. This page explains how to control type annotation syntax and imports for your target environment.

Quick Overview[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#quick-overview "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--target-python-version` | Set the minimum Python version for generated code |
| `--use-union-operator` | Use `X \\| Y` instead of `Union[X, Y]` |
| `--use-standard-collections` | Use `list`, `dict` instead of `List`, `Dict` |
| `--use-annotated` | Use `Annotated` for field metadata |
| `--use-generic-container-types` | Use `Sequence`, `Mapping` instead of `list`, `dict` |
| `--disable-future-imports` | Don't add `from __future__ import annotations` |

* * *

`--target-python-version`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#-target-python-version "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sets the minimum Python version for the generated code. This automatically adjusts type annotation syntax.

| Version | Union Syntax | Collection Syntax | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 3.10 | `X \\| Y` | `list[T]`, `dict[K, V]` | Union operator available |
| 3.11 | `X \\| Y` | `list[T]`, `dict[K, V]` | `Self` type available |
| 3.12+ | `X \\| Y` | `list[T]`, `dict[K, V]` | `type` statement available |

### Example: Python 3.10+[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#example-python-310 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-0-2)   --target-python-version 3.10`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-1-1) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-1-2)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-1-3)     tags: list[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-1-4)     metadata: str | int | None = None`

* * *

`--use-union-operator`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#-use-union-operator "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uses the `|` operator for union types instead of `Union[X, Y]`.

### Without `--use-union-operator`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#without-use-union-operator "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-2-1) from typing import Union, Optional [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-2-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-2-3) class Item(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-2-4)     value: Union[str, int]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-2-5)     label: Optional[str] = None  # Same as Union[str, None]`

### With `--use-union-operator`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#with-use-union-operator "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py --use-union-operator`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-4-1) class Item(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-4-2)     value: str | int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-4-3)     label: str | None = None`

### Compatibility Note[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#compatibility-note "Permanent link")

The union operator `|` requires: - Python 3.10+ at runtime, OR - `from __future__ import annotations` (Python 3.7+) for postponed evaluation

* * *

`--use-standard-collections`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#-use-standard-collections "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uses built-in collection types instead of `typing` module generics.

### Without `--use-standard-collections`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#without-use-standard-collections "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-5-1) from typing import List, Dict, Set, Tuple [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-5-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-5-3) class Data(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-5-4)     items: List[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-5-5)     mapping: Dict[str, int]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-5-6)     unique: Set[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-5-7)     pair: Tuple[str, int]`

### With `--use-standard-collections`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#with-use-standard-collections "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-6-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py --use-standard-collections`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-7-1) class Data(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-7-2)     items: list[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-7-3)     mapping: dict[str, int]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-7-4)     unique: set[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-7-5)     pair: tuple[str, int]`

### Compatibility Note[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#compatibility-note_1 "Permanent link")

Built-in generic syntax requires: - Python 3.10+ at runtime, OR - `from __future__ import annotations`

* * *

`--use-annotated`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#-use-annotated "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uses `typing.Annotated` to attach metadata to types, which is the modern approach for Pydantic v2.

### Without `--use-annotated`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#without-use-annotated "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-8-1) from pydantic import Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-8-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-8-3) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-8-4)     name: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=100)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-8-5)     age: int = Field(..., ge=0, le=150)`

### With `--use-annotated`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#with-use-annotated "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py --use-annotated`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-10-1) from typing import Annotated [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-10-2) from pydantic import Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-10-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-10-4) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-10-5)     name: Annotated[str, Field(min_length=1, max_length=100)]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-10-6)     age: Annotated[int, Field(ge=0, le=150)]`

### Benefits[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#benefits "Permanent link")

*   Cleaner separation of type and constraints
*   Better IDE support
*   More compatible with other tools that understand `Annotated`
*   Required types are more explicit

* * *

`--use-generic-container-types`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#-use-generic-container-types "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uses abstract container types (`Sequence`, `Mapping`, `FrozenSet`) instead of concrete types (`list`, `dict`, `set`).

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-11-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py --use-generic-container-types`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-12-1) from typing import Mapping, Sequence [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-12-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-12-3) class Data(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-12-4)     items: Sequence[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-12-5)     mapping: Mapping[str, int]`

If `--use-standard-collections` is also set, imports from `collections.abc` instead of `typing`.

This is useful when: - You want to use abstract types for better type flexibility - Maintaining compatibility with interfaces that accept any sequence/mapping

* * *

`--disable-future-imports`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#-disable-future-imports "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prevents adding `from __future__ import annotations` to generated files.

### Default behavior (with future imports)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#default-behavior-with-future-imports "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-13-1) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-13-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-13-3) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-13-4)     friends: list[User]  # Forward reference works due to PEP 563`

### With `--disable-future-imports`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#with-disable-future-imports "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-14-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py --disable-future-imports`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-15-1) from typing import List, TYPE_CHECKING [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-15-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-15-3) if TYPE_CHECKING:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-15-4)     from typing import ForwardRef [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-15-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-15-6) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-15-7)     friends: List["User"]  # String forward reference`

### When to disable[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#when-to-disable "Permanent link")

*   Compatibility with runtime annotation inspection
*   Libraries that don't support `__future__.annotations`
*   Pydantic v1 in some configurations
*   When using `get_type_hints()` at runtime

* * *

Common Patterns[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#common-patterns "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Pattern 1: Modern Python (3.10+)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#pattern-1-modern-python-310 "Permanent link")

For projects targeting Python 3.10 or later:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-16-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-16-2)   --target-python-version 3.10 \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-16-3)   --use-union-operator \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-16-4)   --use-standard-collections \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-16-5)   --use-annotated`

Output:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-17-1) from typing import Annotated [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-17-2) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-17-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-17-4) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-17-5)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-17-6)     name: Annotated[str, Field(min_length=1)]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-17-7)     tags: list[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-17-8)     metadata: dict[str, str] | None = None`

### Pattern 2: Minimum Supported Python (3.10)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#pattern-2-minimum-supported-python-310 "Permanent link")

For projects targeting Python 3.10 (minimum supported version):

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-18-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-18-2)   --target-python-version 3.10`

Output:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-19-1) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-19-2)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-19-3)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-19-4)     tags: list[str]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-19-5)     metadata: dict[str, str] | None = None`

### Pattern 3: Maximum compatibility[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#pattern-3-maximum-compatibility "Permanent link")

For libraries that need to work across multiple Python versions (3.10+):

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-20-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-20-2)   --target-python-version 3.10`

The generator will use modern Python 3.10+ syntax including union operators and built-in generic types.

### Pattern 4: CI/CD consistency[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#pattern-4-cicd-consistency "Permanent link")

Pin the Python version in `pyproject.toml` to ensure consistent output:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-21-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-21-2) target-python-version = "3.10" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-21-3) use-union-operator = true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-21-4) use-standard-collections = true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-21-5) use-annotated = true`

* * *

Version Feature Matrix[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#version-feature-matrix "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Feature | 3.10 | 3.11 | 3.12+ |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `list[T]` syntax | native | native | native |
| `X \\| Y` union | native | native | native |
| `Annotated` | native | native | native |
| `TypeAlias` | native | native | native |
| `Self` | `typing_extensions` | native | native |
| `type` statement | N/A | N/A | native |

* * *

Troubleshooting[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#troubleshooting "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### TypeError: 'type' object is not subscriptable[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#typeerror-type-object-is-not-subscriptable "Permanent link")

This occurs when using `list[T]` syntax on older Python versions without `__future__` imports.

**Solution:** Ensure you are running Python 3.10+ or use `from __future__ import annotations`.

### Pydantic validation fails with forward references[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#pydantic-validation-fails-with-forward-references "Permanent link")

This can happen when `__future__.annotations` interacts poorly with Pydantic's type resolution.

**Solution:** Try `--disable-future-imports` or update to Pydantic v2.

### Python 3.13 DeprecationWarning with `typing._eval_type`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#python-313-deprecationwarning-with-typing_eval_type "Permanent link")

When running on Python 3.13+ with `from __future__ import annotations`, you may see:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-22-1) DeprecationWarning: Failing to pass a value to the 'type_params' parameter [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-22-2) of 'typing._eval_type' is deprecated...`

This occurs because Python 3.13 deprecated calling `typing._eval_type()` without the `type_params` parameter. Libraries that evaluate forward references (like older Pydantic versions) trigger this warning.

**Solutions:**

1.  **Upgrade Pydantic** (recommended):
2.  Pydantic v1: Upgrade to version 1.10.18 or later
3.  Pydantic v2: Upgrade to the latest version
    
4.  **Use `--disable-future-imports`** as a workaround:
    
    `[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-23-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py --disable-future-imports`
    
5.  **Suppress the warning** in pytest (temporary fix):
    
    `[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-24-1) # pyproject.toml [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-24-2) [tool.pytest.ini_options] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-24-3) filterwarnings = [     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-24-4)     # For Pydantic v2's v1 compatibility layer (pydantic.v1)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-24-5)     "ignore::DeprecationWarning:pydantic.v1.typing",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-24-6)     # For standalone Pydantic v1    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-24-7)     "ignore::DeprecationWarning:pydantic.typing", [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#__codelineno-24-8) ]`
    

Note

Python 3.14+ will use native deferred annotations (PEP 649), and the generator will no longer add `from __future__ import annotations` for those versions.

### IDE shows type errors[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#ide-shows-type-errors "Permanent link")

Some IDEs don't fully understand `from __future__ import annotations`.

**Solution:** Configure your IDE's Python version or use explicit type syntax matching your runtime version.

* * *

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/#see-also "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
