# OpenAPI-Specific Options - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#openapi-specific-options)

OpenAPI-Specific Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#openapi-specific-options "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================================================

When working with OpenAPI specifications, datamodel-code-generator provides several options to control how schemas, operations, and special properties are handled. This page explains when and how to use each option.

Quick Overview[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#quick-overview "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--openapi-scopes` | Select which parts of the spec to generate models from |
| `--include-path-parameters` | Include path parameters in generated models |
| `--use-operation-id-as-name` | Name models using operation IDs |
| `--read-only-write-only-model-type` | Generate separate models for read/write contexts |
| `--validation` | Enable OpenAPI validation constraints (deprecated) |

* * *

`--openapi-scopes`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#-openapi-scopes "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Controls which sections of the OpenAPI specification to generate models from.

| Scope | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `schemas` | Generate from `#/components/schemas` (default) |
| `parameters` | Generate from `#/components/parameters` |
| `paths` | Generate from path operation parameters |

### Default behavior (schemas only)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#default-behavior-schemas-only "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py`

Generates models only from `#/components/schemas`.

### Include parameters[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#include-parameters "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-1-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-1-2)   --openapi-scopes schemas parameters`

Also generates models from `#/components/parameters`.

### Include path-level definitions[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#include-path-level-definitions "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-2-2)   --openapi-scopes schemas parameters paths`

Generates models from all sources, including inline path operation parameters.

### When to use each scope[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#when-to-use-each-scope "Permanent link")

| Use Case | Recommended Scopes |
| --- | --- |
| Basic model generation | `schemas` (default) |
| Reusable parameter types | `schemas parameters` |
| Complete API coverage | `schemas parameters paths` |

* * *

`--include-path-parameters`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#-include-path-parameters "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Includes path parameters as fields in generated models.

### OpenAPI Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#openapi-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-1) paths:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-2)   /users/{user_id}/orders/{order_id}:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-3)     get:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-4)       operationId: getOrder      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-5)       parameters:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-6)         - name: user_id          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-7)           in: path          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-8)           schema:            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-9)             type: string        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-10)         - name: order_id          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-11)           in: path          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-12)           schema:            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-3-13)             type: integer`

### Without `--include-path-parameters`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#without-include-path-parameters "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-4-1) class GetOrderResponse(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-4-2)     # Only response body fields    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-4-3)     items: list[Item]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-4-4)     total: float`

### With `--include-path-parameters`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#with-include-path-parameters "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-5-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py --include-path-parameters`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-6-1) class GetOrderResponse(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-6-2)     user_id: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-6-3)     order_id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-6-4)     items: list[Item]    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-6-5)     total: float`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#when-to-use "Permanent link")

*   Building request validation models that include URL parameters
*   Creating unified request/response types for API clients
*   Generating models for frameworks that expect all parameters in one object

* * *

`--use-operation-id-as-name`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#-use-operation-id-as-name "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uses the `operationId` from OpenAPI operations to name generated models instead of deriving names from paths.

### OpenAPI Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#openapi-example_1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-1) paths:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-2)   /users/{id}:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-3)     get:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-4)       operationId: getUserById      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-5)       responses:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-6)         '200':          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-7)           content:            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-8)             application/json:              [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-9)               schema:                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-10)                 type: object                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-11)                 properties:                  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-12)                   id: { type: integer }                  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-7-13)                   name: { type: string }`

### Without `--use-operation-id-as-name`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#without-use-operation-id-as-name "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-8-1) class UsersIdGetResponse(BaseModel):  # Derived from path     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-8-2)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-8-3)     name: str`

### With `--use-operation-id-as-name`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#with-use-operation-id-as-name "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py --use-operation-id-as-name`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-10-1) class GetUserByIdResponse(BaseModel):  # Uses operationId     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-10-2)     id: int    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-10-3)     name: str`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#when-to-use_1 "Permanent link")

*   When `operationId` values are well-designed and descriptive
*   For consistency with generated API clients (e.g., OpenAPI Generator)
*   When path-derived names are too verbose or unclear

* * *

`--read-only-write-only-model-type`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#-read-only-write-only-model-type "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generates separate model variants for properties marked as `readOnly` or `writeOnly` in OpenAPI.

### OpenAPI Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#openapi-example_2 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-1) components:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-2)   schemas:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-3)     User:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-4)       type: object      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-5)       properties:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-6)         id:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-7)           type: integer          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-8)           readOnly: true        # Only in responses        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-9)         password:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-10)           type: string          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-11)           writeOnly: true       # Only in requests        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-12)         name:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-11-13)           type: string          # In both`

### Without `--read-only-write-only-model-type`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#without-read-only-write-only-model-type "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-12-1) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-12-2)     id: Optional[int] = None      # Both included    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-12-3)     password: Optional[str] = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-12-4)     name: Optional[str] = None`

### With `--read-only-write-only-model-type`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#with-read-only-write-only-model-type "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-13-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-13-2)   --read-only-write-only-model-type all`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-1) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-2)     """Base model with all fields."""    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-3)     id: Optional[int] = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-4)     password: Optional[str] = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-5)     name: Optional[str] = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-7) class UserRead(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-8)     """For responses - excludes writeOnly fields."""    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-9)     id: Optional[int] = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-10)     name: Optional[str] = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-11)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-12) class UserWrite(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-13)     """For requests - excludes readOnly fields."""    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-14)     password: Optional[str] = None    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-14-15)     name: Optional[str] = None`

### Values[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#values "Permanent link")

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `all` | Generate both Read and Write variants |
| `read` | Generate only Read variants |
| `write` | Generate only Write variants |

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#when-to-use_2 "Permanent link")

*   APIs with distinct request/response schemas
*   Strict type checking for API clients
*   When `readOnly`/`writeOnly` properties are heavily used

* * *

`--validation` (Deprecated)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#-validation-deprecated "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Deprecated

Use `--field-constraints` instead. The `--validation` option is maintained for backward compatibility.

Enables validation constraints from OpenAPI schemas.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-15-1) # Deprecated [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-15-2) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py --validation [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-15-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-15-4) # Recommended [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-15-5) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py --field-constraints`

See [Field Constraints](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/)
 for details.

* * *

Common Patterns[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#common-patterns "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Pattern 1: Basic API models[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#pattern-1-basic-api-models "Permanent link")

For simple APIs where you only need schema models:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-16-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models.py`

### Pattern 2: Full API client models[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#pattern-2-full-api-client-models "Permanent link")

For generating complete models for an API client:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-17-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models/ \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-17-2)   --openapi-scopes schemas parameters paths \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-17-3)   --use-operation-id-as-name \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-17-4)   --include-path-parameters`

### Pattern 3: Strict request/response separation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#pattern-3-strict-requestresponse-separation "Permanent link")

For APIs with distinct input/output shapes:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-18-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models/ \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-18-2)   --read-only-write-only-model-type all \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-18-3)   --field-constraints`

### Pattern 4: Versioned API structure[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#pattern-4-versioned-api-structure "Permanent link")

For large APIs with versioned endpoints:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-19-1) datamodel-codegen --input openapi.yaml --output models/ \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-19-2)   --treat-dot-as-module \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-19-3)   --use-operation-id-as-name \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#__codelineno-19-4)   --all-exports-scope recursive`

* * *

OpenAPI Version Support[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#openapi-version-support "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| OpenAPI Version | Support |
| --- | --- |
| 3.0.x | Full support |
| 3.1.x | Full support |
| 2.0 (Swagger) | Partial support |

* * *

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi-options/#see-also "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [CLI Reference: OpenAPI-only Options](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/openapi-only-options/)
    
*   [Field Constraints](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/)
    
*   [Module Structure and Exports](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/module-exports/)
    
*   [OpenAPI Input Format](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/)

---

# One-liner Usage - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#one-liner-usage)

One-liner Usage[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#one-liner-usage "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================

This guide covers how to use datamodel-code-generator with pipes and clipboard tools for quick, one-time code generation without permanent installation.

Note

The package name is `datamodel-code-generator`, and the CLI command is `datamodel-codegen`.

* * *

One-liner Execution with uvx/pipx[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#one-liner-execution-with-uvxpipx "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You don't need to install datamodel-code-generator permanently. Use `uvx` or `pipx run` to execute it directly.

### Using uvx (Recommended)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#using-uvx-recommended "Permanent link")

[uvx](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/guides/tools/)
 runs Python tools without installation:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-0-1) # Basic usage [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-0-2) uvx datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output model.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-0-4) # With extras (e.g., HTTP support) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-0-5) uvx --from 'datamodel-code-generator[http]' datamodel-codegen --url https://example.com/api.yaml --output model.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-0-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-0-7) # With GraphQL support [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-0-8) uvx --from 'datamodel-code-generator[graphql]' datamodel-codegen --input schema.graphql --output model.py`

### Using pipx run[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#using-pipx-run "Permanent link")

[pipx](https://pipx.pypa.io/)
 also supports one-shot execution:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-1-1) pipx run datamodel-code-generator --input schema.json --output model.py`

* * *

Reading from stdin[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#reading-from-stdin "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

datamodel-code-generator can read schema input from stdin, enabling powerful pipeline workflows:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-2-1) # Pipe JSON directly [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-2-2) echo '{"type": "object", "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"}}}' | \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-2-3)   uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-2-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-2-5) # Pipe from another command [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-2-6) curl -s https://example.com/schema.json | \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-2-7)   uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema --output model.py`

* * *

Clipboard Integration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#clipboard-integration "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Combine stdin support with clipboard tools to quickly generate models from copied schema definitions.

### macOS (pbpaste/pbcopy)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#macos-pbpastepbcopy "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-3-1) # Generate from clipboard and print to stdout [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-3-2) pbpaste | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-3-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-3-4) # Generate from clipboard and save to file [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-3-5) pbpaste | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema --output model.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-3-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-3-7) # Generate from clipboard and copy result back to clipboard [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-3-8) pbpaste | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema | pbcopy`

### Linux (xclip/xsel)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#linux-xclipxsel "Permanent link")

xclipxsel

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-4-1) # Generate from clipboard and print to stdout [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-4-2) xclip -selection clipboard -o | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-4-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-4-4) # Generate and copy result to clipboard [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-4-5) xclip -selection clipboard -o | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema | xclip -selection clipboard`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-5-1) # Generate from clipboard and print to stdout [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-5-2) xsel --clipboard --output | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-5-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-5-4) # Generate and copy result to clipboard [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-5-5) xsel --clipboard --output | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema | xsel --clipboard --input`

Installing clipboard tools on Linux

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-1) # Debian/Ubuntu [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-2) sudo apt install xclip [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-3) # or [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-4) sudo apt install xsel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-6) # Fedora [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-7) sudo dnf install xclip [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-8) # or [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-6-9) sudo dnf install xsel`

### Windows (clip/PowerShell)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#windows-clippowershell "Permanent link")

PowerShellCommand Prompt

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-7-1) # Generate from clipboard and print to stdout [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-7-2) Get-Clipboard | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-7-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-7-4) # Generate and copy result to clipboard [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-7-5) Get-Clipboard | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema | Set-Clipboard`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-8-1) REM Windows Command Prompt doesn't have a built-in paste command [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-8-2) REM Use PowerShell from cmd: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-8-3) powershell -command "Get-Clipboard" | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema`

Windows clip command

The `clip` command on Windows only supports copying **to** the clipboard, not reading from it. Use PowerShell's `Get-Clipboard` and `Set-Clipboard` for full clipboard integration.

* * *

Practical Examples[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#practical-examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Quick model generation workflow[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#quick-model-generation-workflow "Permanent link")

1.  Copy a JSON Schema from documentation or an API response
2.  Run the generator from clipboard:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-9-1) # macOS [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-9-2) pbpaste | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-9-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-9-4) # Linux [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-9-5) xclip -selection clipboard -o | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-9-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-9-7) # Windows PowerShell [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-9-8) Get-Clipboard | uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel`

### Generating from API documentation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#generating-from-api-documentation "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-10-1) # Fetch OpenAPI spec and generate models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-10-2) curl -s https://petstore3.swagger.io/api/v3/openapi.json | \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-10-3)   uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type openapi --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --output petstore.py`

### Generating from GitHub raw URLs[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#generating-from-github-raw-urls "Permanent link")

You can directly fetch schemas from GitHub repositories using raw URLs:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-11-1) # Fetch JSON Schema from GitHub and generate models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-11-2) curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/main/schemas/v3.0/schema.json | \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-11-3)   uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-11-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-11-5) # Fetch OpenAPI spec from a GitHub repository [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-11-6) curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/rest-api-description/main/descriptions/api.github.com/api.github.com.json | \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-11-7)   uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type openapi --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --output github_api.py`

Using the --url option

If you have the `http` extra installed, you can use `--url` directly without curl:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-12-1) uvx --from 'datamodel-code-generator[http]' datamodel-codegen \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-12-2)   --url https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/main/schemas/v3.0/schema.json \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-12-3)   --input-file-type jsonschema \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-12-4)   --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel`

### Using with jq for JSON manipulation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#using-with-jq-for-json-manipulation "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-13-1) # Extract a specific schema definition and generate a model [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-13-2) cat openapi.yaml | yq '.components.schemas.User' | \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#__codelineno-13-3)   uvx datamodel-codegen --input-file-type jsonschema`

* * *

Comparison: Installation Methods[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/oneliner/#comparison-installation-methods "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Method | Command | Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **uvx** | `uvx datamodel-codegen` | One-liner usage, no installation |
| **pipx run** | `pipx run datamodel-code-generator` | One-liner usage, alternative to uvx |
| **pipx install** | `pipx install datamodel-code-generator` | Global installation, frequent usage |
| **uv add** | `uv add datamodel-code-generator` | Project dependency |
| **pip install** | `pip install datamodel-code-generator` | Traditional installation |

When to use each method

*   **uvx/pipx run**: Quick one-liner generation, testing different versions
*   **pipx install**: Frequent CLI usage across multiple projects
*   **uv add/pip install**: Project dependency, CI/CD pipelines, programmatic usage

---

# Type Mappings and Custom Types - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#type-mappings-and-custom-types)

Type Mappings and Custom Types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#type-mappings-and-custom-types "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================================================

datamodel-code-generator allows you to customize how schema types are mapped to Python types. This is essential for projects with specific type requirements, datetime handling preferences, or third-party library integrations.

Quick Overview[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#quick-overview "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--type-mappings` | Map schema types to custom Python types |
| `--strict-types` | Use Pydantic strict types for validation |
| `--output-datetime-class` | Choose datetime output type |
| `--use-pendulum` | Use Pendulum library for datetime types |
| `--use-decimal-for-multiple-of` | Use Decimal for multipleOf constraints |
| `format: "email"` | Email validation (requires `email-validator`) |
| `format: "ulid"` | ULID type support (requires `python-ulid`) |

* * *

`--type-mappings`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#-type-mappings "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Maps schema type+format combinations to other format names. This allows you to override how specific formats are interpreted.

### Format[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#format "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-0-1) --type-mappings <type+format>=<target_format> [<type+format>=<target_format> ...]`

### Basic Examples[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#basic-examples "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-1) # Map binary format to string (generates str instead of bytes) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-2) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-3)   --type-mappings "binary=string" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-5) # Map email format to string (generates str instead of EmailStr) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-6) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-7)   --type-mappings "string+email=string" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-9) # Multiple mappings [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-10) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-1-11)   --type-mappings "string+email=string" "string+uuid=string"`

### Mapping Syntax[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#mapping-syntax "Permanent link")

| Syntax | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `format=target` | Map format (assumes string type) | `binary=string` |
| `type+format=target` | Map type with format | `string+email=string` |

### Avoiding Pydantic Optional Extras[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#avoiding-pydantic-optional-extras "Permanent link")

Some Pydantic types require optional dependencies. Use `--type-mappings` to generate plain types instead:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-2-1) # Avoid pydantic[email] dependency (EmailStr requires email-validator) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-2-2) --type-mappings "string+email=string" "string+idn-email=string" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-2-4) # Generate str instead of UUID for uuid format [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-2-5) --type-mappings "string+uuid=string"`

### Available Target Formats[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#available-target-formats "Permanent link")

| Target | Generated Type |
| --- | --- |
| `string` | `str` |
| `integer` | `int` |
| `number` | `float` |
| `boolean` | `bool` |
| `binary` | `bytes` |
| `date` | `datetime.date` |
| `date-time` | `datetime.datetime` |
| `uuid` | `UUID` |
| `email` | `EmailStr` |
| `uri` | `AnyUrl` |

### pyproject.toml Configuration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#pyprojecttoml-configuration "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-3-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-3-2) type-mappings = [     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-3-3)     "string+email=string",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-3-4)     "string+idn-email=string",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-3-5)     "binary=string", [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-3-6) ]`

* * *

`--strict-types`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#-strict-types "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generates Pydantic strict types that don't perform type coercion during validation.

### Available Strict Types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#available-strict-types "Permanent link")

| Value | Strict Type | Rejects |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `str` | `StrictStr` | Integers, floats |
| `int` | `StrictInt` | Strings, floats |
| `float` | `StrictFloat` | Strings, integers |
| `bool` | `StrictBool` | Strings, integers |
| `bytes` | `StrictBytes` | Strings |

### Without `--strict-types`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#without-strict-types "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-4-1) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-4-2)     id: int        # Accepts "123" and converts to 123    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-4-3)     name: str      # Accepts 123 and converts to "123"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-4-4)     active: bool   # Accepts 1 and converts to True`

### With `--strict-types`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#with-strict-types "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-5-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-5-2)   --strict-types str int bool`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-6-1) from pydantic import StrictBool, StrictInt, StrictStr [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-6-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-6-3) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-6-4)     id: StrictInt      # Rejects "123", requires integer    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-6-5)     name: StrictStr    # Rejects 123, requires string    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-6-6)     active: StrictBool # Rejects 1, requires boolean`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#when-to-use "Permanent link")

*   API validation where type coercion is undesirable
*   Data pipelines requiring exact types
*   Security-sensitive applications
*   Testing environments requiring strict type checking

* * *

`--output-datetime-class`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#-output-datetime-class "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Controls the Python type used for `date-time` formatted strings.

| Value | Output Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `datetime` | `datetime.datetime` | Standard library datetime (default) |
| `AwareDatetime` | `pydantic.AwareDatetime` | Requires timezone info |
| `NaiveDatetime` | `pydantic.NaiveDatetime` | Rejects timezone info |

### Default behavior[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#default-behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-7-1) from datetime import datetime [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-7-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-7-3) class Event(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-7-4)     created_at: datetime  # Accepts both aware and naive`

### AwareDatetime (recommended for APIs)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#awaredatetime-recommended-for-apis "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-8-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-8-2)   --output-datetime-class AwareDatetime`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-9-1) from pydantic import AwareDatetime [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-9-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-9-3) class Event(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-9-4)     created_at: AwareDatetime  # Requires timezone, e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z`

### NaiveDatetime[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#naivedatetime "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-10-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-10-2)   --output-datetime-class NaiveDatetime`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-11-1) from pydantic import NaiveDatetime [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-11-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-11-3) class Event(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-11-4)     created_at: NaiveDatetime  # Rejects timezone, e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00`

### When to use each[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#when-to-use-each "Permanent link")

| Use Case | Recommended Class |
| --- | --- |
| REST APIs | `AwareDatetime` |
| Database models | `datetime` or `NaiveDatetime` |
| Logs with UTC timestamps | `AwareDatetime` |
| Local time applications | `NaiveDatetime` |

* * *

`--use-pendulum`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#-use-pendulum "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uses [Pendulum](https://pendulum.eustace.io/)
 library types instead of standard library datetime.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-12-1) pip install pendulum [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-12-2) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py --use-pendulum`

### Output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#output "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-13-1) import pendulum [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-13-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-13-3) class Event(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-13-4)     created_at: pendulum.DateTime    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-13-5)     date: pendulum.Date    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-13-6)     time: pendulum.Time`

### Benefits of Pendulum[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#benefits-of-pendulum "Permanent link")

*   Timezone handling is simpler and more intuitive
*   Human-friendly datetime manipulation
*   Better serialization defaults
*   Immutable by default

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#when-to-use_1 "Permanent link")

*   Projects already using Pendulum
*   Applications requiring complex datetime manipulation
*   When timezone handling is a priority

* * *

Email Format Support[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#email-format-support "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The generator supports the `email` and `idn-email` string formats, which generate Pydantic's `EmailStr` type.

Required Dependency

The `email` format requires the `email-validator` package to be installed:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-14-1) pip install email-validator`

Or install Pydantic with the email extra:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-15-1) pip install pydantic[email]`

### Schema Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#schema-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-2)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-3)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-4)     "email": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-5)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-6)       "format": "email"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-7)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-8)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-16-9) }`

### Output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#output_1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-17-1) from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-17-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-17-3) class MyModel(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-17-4)     email: EmailStr`

### Avoiding the Dependency[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#avoiding-the-dependency "Permanent link")

If you don't want to install `email-validator`, you can map email formats to plain strings:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-18-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-18-2)   --type-mappings "string+email=string" "string+idn-email=string"`

This generates `str` instead of `EmailStr`.

* * *

ULID Format Support[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#ulid-format-support "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The generator supports the `ulid` string format, which generates [`python-ulid`](https://github.com/mdomke/python-ulid)
 types.

Required Dependency

The `ulid` format requires the `python-ulid` package to be installed:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-19-1) pip install python-ulid`

For Pydantic integration, use:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-20-1) pip install python-ulid[pydantic]`

### Schema Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#schema-example_1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-2)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-3)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-4)     "id": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-5)       "type": "string",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-6)       "format": "ulid"    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-7)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-8)   } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-21-9) }`

### Output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#output_2 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-22-1) from ulid import ULID [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-22-2) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-22-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-22-4) class MyModel(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-22-5)     id: ULID`

### What is ULID?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#what-is-ulid "Permanent link")

ULID (Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier) is an alternative to UUID that offers:

*   **Lexicographic sorting** - ULIDs sort naturally by creation time
*   **Compactness** - 26 characters vs 36 for UUID
*   **URL-safe** - Uses Crockford's Base32 encoding
*   **Timestamp encoded** - First 10 characters encode creation time

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#when-to-use_2 "Permanent link")

*   Distributed systems requiring time-ordered IDs
*   Applications where database index performance matters
*   When you need both uniqueness and sortability

* * *

`--use-decimal-for-multiple-of`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#-use-decimal-for-multiple-of "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Uses `Decimal` type for numbers with `multipleOf` constraints to avoid floating-point precision issues.

### The Problem[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#the-problem "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-23-1) properties:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-23-2)   price:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-23-3)     type: number    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-23-4)     multipleOf: 0.01  # Currency precision`

Without this option, floating-point arithmetic can cause validation issues:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-24-1) # 0.1 + 0.2 = 0.30000000000000004 in floating-point [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-24-2) price = 0.30000000000000004  # May fail multipleOf validation`

### Solution[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#solution "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-25-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-25-2)   --use-decimal-for-multiple-of`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-26-1) from decimal import Decimal [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-26-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-26-3) class Product(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-26-4)     price: Decimal  # Exact decimal arithmetic`

### When to use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#when-to-use_3 "Permanent link")

*   Financial applications
*   Scientific calculations requiring precision
*   Any schema with `multipleOf` constraints

* * *

Common Patterns[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#common-patterns "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Pattern 1: Minimal dependencies[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#pattern-1-minimal-dependencies "Permanent link")

Avoid optional Pydantic dependencies by mapping special formats to plain types:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-27-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-27-2)   --type-mappings "string+email=string" "string+idn-email=string"`

### Pattern 2: Strict API validation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#pattern-2-strict-api-validation "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-28-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-28-2)   --strict-types str int float bool \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-28-3)   --output-datetime-class AwareDatetime \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-28-4)   --field-constraints`

### Pattern 3: Financial application[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#pattern-3-financial-application "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-29-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-29-2)   --use-decimal-for-multiple-of \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-29-3)   --strict-types str int`

### Pattern 4: Pendulum datetime handling[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#pattern-4-pendulum-datetime-handling "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-30-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output models.py \   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-30-2)   --use-pendulum \  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#__codelineno-30-3)   --strict-types str int`

* * *

Type Mapping Reference[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#type-mapping-reference "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Common Format Mappings[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#common-format-mappings "Permanent link")

| Schema Format | Default Type | With `--type-mappings "format=string"` |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `email` | `EmailStr` | `str` |
| `idn-email` | `EmailStr` | `str` |
| `uuid` | `UUID` | `str` |
| `ulid` | `ULID` (requires `python-ulid`) | `str` |
| `uri` | `AnyUrl` | `str` |
| `binary` | `bytes` | `str` |

Other type customization options

For datetime types, use `--output-datetime-class` or `--use-pendulum` instead of `--type-mappings`.

* * *

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/#see-also "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [CLI Reference: `--type-mappings`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#type-mappings)

*   [CLI Reference: `--output-datetime-class`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#output-datetime-class)
    
*   [CLI Reference: `--use-pendulum`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-pendulum)
    
*   [Field Constraints](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/)

# 🐍 Using datamodel-code-generator as a Module - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#using-datamodel-code-generator-as-a-module)

🐍 Using datamodel-code-generator as a Module[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#using-datamodel-code-generator-as-a-module "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================================================================================

datamodel-code-generator is a CLI tool, but it can also be used as a Python module.

🚀 How to Use[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#how-to-use "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can generate models with `datamodel_code_generator.generate` using parameters that match the [CLI arguments](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/)
.

### 📦 Installation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#installation "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-0-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[http]'`

### 📝 Getting Generated Code as String[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#getting-generated-code-as-string "Permanent link")

When the `output` parameter is omitted (or set to `None`), `generate()` returns the generated code directly as a string:

Note

`GenerateConfig` is only available in Pydantic v2 environments. For Pydantic v1, use `from datamodel_code_generator.config import GenerateConfig` and call `update_forward_refs()` before use.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-1) from datamodel_code_generator import InputFileType, generate, GenerateConfig, DataModelType [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-3) json_schema: str = """{     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-4)     "type": "object",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-5)     "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-6)         "number": {"type": "number"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-7)         "street_name": {"type": "string"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-8)         "street_type": {"type": "string",                        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-9)                         "enum": ["Street", "Avenue", "Boulevard"]                        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-10)                         }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-11)     } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-12) }""" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-13)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-14) config = GenerateConfig(     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-15)     input_file_type=InputFileType.JsonSchema,    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-16)     input_filename="example.json",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-17)     output_model_type=DataModelType.PydanticV2BaseModel, [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-18) ) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-19) result = generate(json_schema, config=config) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-1-20) print(result)`

### 📝 Multiple Module Output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#multiple-module-output "Permanent link")

When the schema generates multiple modules, `generate()` returns a `GeneratedModules` dictionary mapping module path tuples to generated code:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-1) from datamodel_code_generator import InputFileType, generate, GenerateConfig, GeneratedModules [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-3) # Your OpenAPI specification (string, Path, or dict) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-4) openapi_spec: str = "..."  # Replace with your actual OpenAPI spec [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-6) # Schema that generates multiple modules (e.g., with $ref to other files) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-7) config = GenerateConfig(     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-8)     input_file_type=InputFileType.OpenAPI, [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-9) ) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-10) result: str | GeneratedModules = generate(openapi_spec, config=config) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-11)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-12) if isinstance(result, dict):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-13)     for module_path, content in result.items():        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-14)         print(f"Module: {'/'.join(module_path)}")        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-15)         print(content)        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-16)         print("---") [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-17) else:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-2-18)     print(result)`

### 📝 Writing to Files[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#writing-to-files "Permanent link")

To write generated code to the file system, provide a `Path` to the `output` parameter in the config:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-1) from pathlib import Path [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-2) from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-3) from datamodel_code_generator import InputFileType, generate, GenerateConfig, DataModelType [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-5) json_schema: str = """{     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-6)     "type": "object",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-7)     "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-8)         "number": {"type": "number"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-9)         "street_name": {"type": "string"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-10)         "street_type": {"type": "string",                        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-11)                         "enum": ["Street", "Avenue", "Boulevard"]                        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-12)                         }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-13)     } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-14) }""" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-15)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-16) with TemporaryDirectory() as temporary_directory_name:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-17)     temporary_directory = Path(temporary_directory_name)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-18)     output = Path(temporary_directory / 'model.py')    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-19)     config = GenerateConfig(        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-20)         input_file_type=InputFileType.JsonSchema,        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-21)         input_filename="example.json",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-22)         output=output,        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-23)         # set up the output model types        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-24)         output_model_type=DataModelType.PydanticV2BaseModel,    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-25)     )    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-26)     generate(json_schema, config=config)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-27)     model: str = output.read_text() [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#__codelineno-3-28) print(model)`

🔧 Using the Parser Directly[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#using-the-parser-directly "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can also call the parser directly for more control. Parser classes also support the `config` parameter similar to `generate()`.

### Using `config` Parameter (Recommended)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#using-config-parameter-recommended "Permanent link")

### Available Parser Config Classes[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#available-parser-config-classes "Permanent link")

Each parser type has its own config class:

| Parser | Config Class |
| --- | --- |
| `JsonSchemaParser` | `JSONSchemaParserConfig` |
| `OpenAPIParser` | `OpenAPIParserConfig` |
| `GraphQLParser` | `GraphQLParserConfig` |

All config classes inherit from `ParserConfig` and include additional parser-specific options.

**✨ Output:**

* * *

📋 Return Value Summary[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/#return-value-summary "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| `output` Parameter | Single Module | Multiple Modules |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `None` (default) | `str` | `GeneratedModules` (dict) |
| `Path` (file) | `None` | Error |
| `Path` (directory) | `None` | `None` |

📌 **Note:** When `output` is a file path and multiple modules would be generated, `generate()` raises a `datamodel_code_generator.Error` exception. Use a directory path instead.

* * *
