# 🛠️ Development - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#development)

🛠️ Development[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#development "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================================

Install the package in editable mode:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-0-1) $ git clone [[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) :koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator.git [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-0-2) $ pip install -e datamodel-code-generator`

🤝 Contribute[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#contribute "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================================

We are waiting for your contributions to `datamodel-code-generator`.

📝 How to contribute[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#how-to-contribute "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-1) ## 1. Clone your fork repository [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-2) $ git clone [[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) :<your username>/datamodel-code-generator.git [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-3) $ cd datamodel-code-generator [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-5) ## 2. Install [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-6) $ curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-8) ## 3. Install tox with uv [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-9) $ uv tool install --python-preference only-managed --python 3.13 tox --with tox-uv [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-10)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-11) ## 4. Create developer environment [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-12) $ tox run -e dev [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-13)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-14) .tox/dev is a Python environment you can use for development purposes [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-15)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-16) ## 5. Install pre-commit hooks [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-17) $ uv tool install prek [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-18) $ prek install [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-19)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-20) ## 6. Create new branch and rewrite code. [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-21) $ git checkout -b new-branch [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-22)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-23) ## 7. Run unittest under Python 3.13 (you should pass all test and coverage should be 100%) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-24) $ tox run -e 3.13 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-25)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-26) ## 8. Format and lint code (will print errors that cannot be automatically fixed) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-27) $ tox run -e fix [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-28)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-29) ## 9. Check README help text is up to date [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-30) $ tox run -e readme [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-31)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-32) ## 10. Check CLI documentation is up to date [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-33) $ tox run -e cli-docs [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-34)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-1-35) ## 11. Commit and Push...`

➕ Adding a New CLI Option[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#adding-a-new-cli-option "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When adding a new CLI option to `datamodel-code-generator`, follow these steps:

### Step 1: Implement the option (Required)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#step-1-implement-the-option-required "Permanent link")

Add the option to `src/datamodel_code_generator/arguments.py`:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-2-1) arg_parser.add_argument(     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-2-2)     "--my-new-option",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-2-3)     help="Description of what this option does",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-2-4)     action="store_true",  # or other action type [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-2-5) )`

### Step 2: Add a test with documentation marker (Required)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#step-2-add-a-test-with-documentation-marker-required "Permanent link")

Create a test that demonstrates the option and add the `@pytest.mark.cli_doc()` marker:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-1) @pytest.mark.cli_doc(     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-2)     options=["--my-new-option"],    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-3)     input_schema="jsonschema/example.json",  # Path relative to tests/data/    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-4)     cli_args=["--my-new-option"],    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-5)     golden_output="jsonschema/example_with_my_option.py",  # Expected output [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-6) ) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-7) def test_my_new_option(output_file: Path) -> None:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-8)     """Short description of what the option does. [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-9)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-10)     This docstring becomes the documentation for the option.    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-11)     Explain when and why users would use this option.    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-12)     """    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-13)     run_main_and_assert(        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-14)         input_path=JSON_SCHEMA_DATA_PATH / "example.json",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-15)         output_path=output_file,        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-16)         extra_args=["--my-new-option"],        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-17)         ...    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-3-18)     )`

### Step 3: Categorize the option (Optional)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#step-3-categorize-the-option-optional "Permanent link")

By default, new options appear in "General Options". To place in a specific category, add to `src/datamodel_code_generator/cli_options.py`:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-4-1) CLI_OPTION_META: dict[str, CLIOptionMeta] = {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-4-2)     ...    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-4-3)     "--my-new-option": CLIOptionMeta(        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-4-4)         name="--my-new-option",        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-4-5)         category=OptionCategory.MODEL,  # or FIELD, TYPING, TEMPLATE, etc.    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-4-6)     ), [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-4-7) }`

### Step 4: Generate and verify documentation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#step-4-generate-and-verify-documentation "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-1) # Regenerate CLI docs [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-2) $ pytest --collect-cli-docs -p no:xdist -q [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-3) $ python scripts/build_cli_docs.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-5) # Verify docs are correct [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-6) $ tox run -e cli-docs [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-8) # If you modified config.py, regenerate config TypedDicts [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#__codelineno-5-9) $ tox run -e config-types`

### 🔧 Troubleshooting[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#troubleshooting "Permanent link")

If `tox run -e cli-docs` fails:

*   **"No test found documenting option --xxx"**: Add `@pytest.mark.cli_doc(options=["--xxx"], ...)` to a test
*   **"File not found: ..."**: Check that `input_schema` and `golden_output` paths are correct
*   **"CLI docs are OUT OF DATE"**: Run `python scripts/build_cli_docs.py` to regenerate

📖 CLI Documentation Marker Reference[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/development-contributing/#cli-documentation-marker-reference "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `cli_doc` marker supports:

| Parameter | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `options` | Yes | List of CLI options this test documents |
| `input_schema` | Yes | Input schema path (relative to `tests/data/`) |
| `cli_args` | Yes | CLI arguments used in the test |
| `golden_output` | Yes\* | Expected output file path |
| `model_outputs` | No  | Dict of model type → output file (for multi-model tabs) |
| `version_outputs` | No  | Dict of Python version → output file |
| `comparison_output` | No  | Baseline output without option (for before/after) |
| `primary` | No  | Set `True` if this is the main example for the option |

\*Either `golden_output` or `model_outputs` is required.

See existing tests in `tests/main/` for examples.

---

# LLM Integration - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#llm-integration)

LLM Integration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#llm-integration "Permanent link")

====================================================================================================================

The `--generate-prompt` option generates a formatted prompt that you can use to consult Large Language Models (LLMs) about datamodel-code-generator CLI options.

Overview[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#overview "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you're unsure which CLI options to use for your specific use case, you can generate a prompt containing all available options and their descriptions, then ask an LLM for recommendations.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt "How do I generate strict Pydantic v2 models?"`

The generated prompt includes:

*   Your question (if provided)
*   Current CLI options you've specified
*   All options organized by category with descriptions
*   Full help text for reference

CLI LLM Tools[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#cli-llm-tools "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pipe the generated prompt directly to CLI-based LLM tools:

### Claude Code[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#claude-code "Permanent link")

[Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)
 is Anthropic's official CLI tool. Use `-p` flag for non-interactive (pipe) mode:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-1-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt "Best options for API response models?" | claude -p`

### OpenAI Codex CLI[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#openai-codex-cli "Permanent link")

[Codex CLI](https://github.com/openai/codex)
 is OpenAI's CLI tool. Use `exec` subcommand for non-interactive mode:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-2-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt "How to handle nullable fields?" | codex exec`

### Other CLI Tools[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#other-cli-tools "Permanent link")

Other popular LLM CLI tools that accept stdin:

| Tool | Command | Repository |
| --- | --- | --- |
| llm | `\\| llm` | [simonw/llm](https://github.com/simonw/llm) |
| aichat | `\\| aichat` | [sigoden/aichat](https://github.com/sigoden/aichat) |
| sgpt | `\\| sgpt` | [TheR1D/shell\_gpt](https://github.com/TheR1D/shell_gpt) |
| mods | `\\| mods` | [charmbracelet/mods](https://github.com/charmbracelet/mods) |

Check each tool's documentation for specific usage and options.

Web LLM Chat Services[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#web-llm-chat-services "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Copy the prompt to clipboard, then paste into your preferred web-based LLM chat:

### macOS[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#macos "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-3-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | pbcopy`

### Linux (X11)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#linux-x11 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | xclip -selection clipboard`

### Linux (Wayland)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#linux-wayland "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-5-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | wl-copy`

### Windows (PowerShell)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#windows-powershell "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-6-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | Set-Clipboard`

### WSL2[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#wsl2 "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-7-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt | clip.exe`

Usage Examples[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#usage-examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic Usage[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#basic-usage "Permanent link")

Generate a prompt without a specific question:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-8-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt`

### With a Question[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#with-a-question "Permanent link")

Include your specific question in the prompt:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-9-1) datamodel-codegen --generate-prompt "What options should I use for GraphQL schema?"`

### With Current Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#with-current-options "Permanent link")

Show your current configuration and ask for improvements:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-10-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-10-2)     --input schema.json \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-10-3)     --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-10-4)     --use-annotated \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-10-5)     --generate-prompt "Are there any other options I should consider?"`

### Pipe to Claude with Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#pipe-to-claude-with-options "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-11-1) datamodel-codegen \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-11-2)     --input openapi.yaml \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-11-3)     --output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-11-4)     --generate-prompt "How can I add JSON serialization support?" \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#__codelineno-11-5)     | claude -p`

Tips[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/llm-integration/#tips "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Be specific** - Include a clear question to get more relevant recommendations
2.  **Show context** - Add your current options so the LLM understands your setup
3.  **Iterate** - Use the suggestions, then ask follow-up questions if needed

---

# ❓ Frequently Asked Questions - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#frequently-asked-questions)

❓ Frequently Asked Questions[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#frequently-asked-questions "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================================

📋 Schema Handling[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#schema-handling "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 🔀 oneOf/anyOf generates unexpected Union types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#oneofanyof-generates-unexpected-union-types "Permanent link")

When using `oneOf` or `anyOf`, the generated models may not match your expectations. Use `--union-mode` to control how unions are generated:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-0-1) # Smart union (Pydantic v2 only) - validates against types in order [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-0-2) datamodel-codegen --union-mode smart --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-0-4) # Left-to-right validation [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-0-5) datamodel-codegen --union-mode left_to_right ...`

See [CLI Reference: `--union-mode`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#union-mode)
 for details.

### 🔗 allOf doesn't merge properties as expected[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#allof-doesnt-merge-properties-as-expected "Permanent link")

Control how `allOf` schemas merge fields:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-1-1) # Merge only constraints (minItems, maxItems, pattern, etc.) - default [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-1-2) datamodel-codegen --allof-merge-mode constraints ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-1-4) # Merge constraints + annotations (default, examples) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-1-5) datamodel-codegen --allof-merge-mode all ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-1-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-1-7) # Don't merge any fields [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-1-8) datamodel-codegen --allof-merge-mode none ...`

See [CLI Reference: `--allof-merge-mode`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#allof-merge-mode)
 for details.

📎 Related: [#399](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/399)

### 📁 How to generate from multiple schema files?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#how-to-generate-from-multiple-schema-files "Permanent link")

Use a directory as input, or use `$ref` to reference other files:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-2-1) # Generate from directory containing multiple schemas [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-2-2) datamodel-codegen --input schemas/ --output models/`

For schemas with cross-file `$ref`, ensure you have the HTTP extra for remote refs:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-3-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[http]'`

📎 Related: [#215](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/215)

### 🔤 YAML bool keywords (YES, NO, true, false) in string enums[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#yaml-bool-keywords-yes-no-true-false-in-string-enums "Permanent link")

YAML 1.1 treats unquoted keywords like `YES`, `NO`, `on`, `off`, `true`, `false` as boolean values. This tool preserves them as strings to avoid unexpected conversions in schema contexts:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-4-1) # Input YAML [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-4-2) enum:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-4-3)   - YES  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-4-4)   - NO  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-4-5)   - NOT_APPLICABLE`

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-5-1) # Generated code (strings preserved) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-5-2) class MyEnum(Enum):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-5-3)     YES = 'YES'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-5-4)     NO = 'NO'    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-5-5)     NOT_APPLICABLE = 'NOT_APPLICABLE'`

This matches the expected behavior when `type: string` is specified in your schema. If you need the previous behavior where YAML bool keywords were converted to Python booleans, please [open an issue](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues)
 describing your use case.

📎 Related: [#1653](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/1653)
, [#1766](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/1766)
, [#2338](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/2338)

* * *

🔍 Type Checking[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#type-checking "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ⚠️ mypy complains about Field constraints[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#mypy-complains-about-field-constraints "Permanent link")

If mypy reports errors about `conint`, `constr`, or other constrained types, use `--field-constraints` or `--use-annotated`:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-6-1) # Use Field(..., ge=0) instead of conint(ge=0) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-6-2) datamodel-codegen --field-constraints ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-6-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-6-4) # Use Annotated[int, Field(ge=0)] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-6-5) datamodel-codegen --use-annotated ...`

See [Field Constraints](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/)
 for more information.

### 🤔 Type checker doesn't understand generated types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#type-checker-doesnt-understand-generated-types "Permanent link")

Ensure you're using the correct target Python version:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-7-1) datamodel-codegen --target-python-version 3.11 ...`

This affects type syntax generation (e.g., `list[str]` vs `List[str]`, `X | Y` vs `Union[X, Y]`).

* * *

🏷️ Field Naming[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#field-naming "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 🚫 Property names conflict with Python reserved words[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#property-names-conflict-with-python-reserved-words "Permanent link")

Properties like `class`, `from`, `import` are automatically renamed with a `field_` prefix. Control this behavior:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-8-1) # Custom prefix (default: "field") [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-8-2) datamodel-codegen --special-field-name-prefix my_prefix ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-8-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-8-4) # Remove special prefix entirely [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-8-5) datamodel-codegen --remove-special-field-name-prefix ...`

### 🔣 Field names have special characters[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#field-names-have-special-characters "Permanent link")

JSON/YAML property names with spaces, dashes, or special characters are converted to valid Python identifiers. An alias is automatically generated to preserve the original name:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-9-1) class Model(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-9-2)     my_field: str = Field(..., alias='my-field')`

To disable aliases:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-10-1) datamodel-codegen --no-alias ...`

See [Field Aliases](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/)
 for custom alias mappings.

### 🐍 Want snake\_case field names from camelCase[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#want-snake_case-field-names-from-camelcase "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-11-1) datamodel-codegen --snake-case-field ...`

This generates snake\_case field names with camelCase aliases:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-12-1) class User(BaseModel):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-12-2)     first_name: str = Field(..., alias='firstName')`

* * *

🔄 Output Stability[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#output-stability "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ⏰ Generated output changes on every run[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#generated-output-changes-on-every-run "Permanent link")

The timestamp in the header changes on each run. Disable it for reproducible output:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-13-1) datamodel-codegen --disable-timestamp ...`

### 🌍 Output differs between environments[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#output-differs-between-environments "Permanent link")

Ensure consistent formatting across environments:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-14-1) # Explicitly set formatters [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-14-2) datamodel-codegen --formatters black isort ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-14-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-14-4) # Or disable formatting entirely for raw output [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-14-5) datamodel-codegen --formatters ...`

Also ensure the same Python version and formatter configurations (`pyproject.toml`) are used.

### 🤖 CI fails because generated code is different[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#ci-fails-because-generated-code-is-different "Permanent link")

Use `--check` mode in CI to verify generated files are up-to-date:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-15-1) datamodel-codegen --check --input schema.yaml --output models.py`

This exits with code 1 if the output would differ, without modifying files.

* * *

⚡ Performance[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#performance "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 🐢 Generation is slow for large schemas[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#generation-is-slow-for-large-schemas "Permanent link")

For very large schemas with many models:

1.  Use `--reuse-model` to deduplicate identical models
2.  Consider splitting schemas into multiple files
3.  Use `--disable-warnings` to reduce output

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-16-1) datamodel-codegen --reuse-model --disable-warnings ...`

See [Model Reuse and Deduplication](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/)
 for details.

* * *

🔧 Output Model Types[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#output-model-types "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 🤷 Which output model type should I use?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#which-output-model-type-should-i-use "Permanent link")

*   **Pydantic v2** (`pydantic_v2.BaseModel`): ✨ Recommended for new projects. Better performance and modern API.
*   **Pydantic v1** (`pydantic.BaseModel`): 🔄 Default for backward compatibility with existing codebases.
*   **dataclasses**: Simple data containers without validation.
*   **TypedDict**: Type hints for dict structures.
*   **msgspec**: High-performance serialization.

See [Output Model Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/)
 for a detailed comparison.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-17-1) # For new projects [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-17-2) datamodel-codegen --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel ...`

See [Pydantic v1 vs v2](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/what_is_the_difference_between_v1_and_v2/)
 for migration details.

📎 Related: [#803](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/803)

### 💥 Generated code doesn't work with my Pydantic version[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#generated-code-doesnt-work-with-my-pydantic-version "Permanent link")

Ensure the output model type matches your installed Pydantic version:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-18-1) # Check your Pydantic version [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-18-2) python -c "import pydantic; print(pydantic.VERSION)" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-18-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-18-4) # Generate for Pydantic v2 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-18-5) datamodel-codegen --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-18-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-18-7) # Generate for Pydantic v1 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-18-8) datamodel-codegen --output-model-type pydantic.BaseModel ...`

* * *

🌐 Remote Schemas[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#remote-schemas "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 📡 Cannot fetch schema from URL[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#cannot-fetch-schema-from-url "Permanent link")

Install the HTTP extra:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-19-1) pip install 'datamodel-code-generator[http]'`

For authenticated endpoints:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-20-1) datamodel-codegen --url https://api.example.com/schema.yaml \     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-20-2)     --http-headers "Authorization: Bearer TOKEN" \    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-20-3)     --output model.py`

### 🔒 SSL certificate errors[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#ssl-certificate-errors "Permanent link")

For development/testing with self-signed certificates:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-21-1) datamodel-codegen --url https://... --http-ignore-tls --output model.py`

⚠️ Security Notice

Only use `--http-ignore-tls` in trusted environments.

* * *

📘 OpenAPI Specific[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#openapi-specific "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 📝 How to handle readOnly/writeOnly properties?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#how-to-handle-readonlywriteonly-properties "Permanent link")

Use `--read-only-write-only-model-type` to generate separate Request/Response models:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-22-1) # Generate Request/Response models only [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-22-2) datamodel-codegen --read-only-write-only-model-type request-response ... [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-22-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-22-4) # Generate Base + Request + Response models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-22-5) datamodel-codegen --read-only-write-only-model-type all ...`

📎 Related: [#727](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/727)

### ❓ Why are nullable fields not Optional?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#why-are-nullable-fields-not-optional "Permanent link")

Use `--strict-nullable` to treat nullable fields as truly optional:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-23-1) datamodel-codegen --strict-nullable ...`

📎 Related: [#327](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/327)

* * *

🔧 Advanced[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#advanced "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 📦 How to use TypeAlias instead of RootModel?[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#how-to-use-typealias-instead-of-rootmodel "Permanent link")

Use `--use-type-alias` (experimental) to generate type aliases instead of root models:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#__codelineno-24-1) datamodel-codegen --use-type-alias --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel ...`

See [Root Models and Type Aliases](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/root-model-and-type-alias/)
 for details.

📎 Related: [#2505](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues/2505)

* * *

📖 See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/faq/#see-also "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   🖥️ [CLI Reference](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/)
     - Complete option documentation
*   ⚙️ [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)
     - Configure options via file
*   🐛 [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/issues)
     - Report bugs or request features
*   💬 [Discussions](https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator/discussions)
     - Ask questions and share ideas

# Dynamic Model Generation - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#dynamic-model-generation)

Dynamic Model Generation[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#dynamic-model-generation "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================================================

Generate real Python model classes from JSON Schema or OpenAPI at runtime without writing files.

Overview[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#overview "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While `generate()` produces source code as strings, `generate_dynamic_models()` creates actual Python classes that you can use immediately for validation and data processing. This is useful for:

*   Runtime schema validation without code generation step
*   Dynamic API clients that adapt to schema changes
*   Testing and prototyping
*   Plugin systems with dynamic schemas

Quick Start[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#quick-start "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-1) from datamodel_code_generator import generate_dynamic_models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-3) schema = {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-4)     "type": "object",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-5)     "properties": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-6)         "name": {"type": "string"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-7)         "age": {"type": "integer"}    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-8)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-9)     "required": ["name"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-10) } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-11)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-12) models = generate_dynamic_models(schema) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-13) User = models["Model"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-14)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-15) # Use the model for validation [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-16) user = User(name="Alice", age=30) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-17) print(user.model_dump())  # {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30} [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-18)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-19) # Validation errors are raised [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-20) try:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-21)     User(age="not a number")  # Missing required 'name', wrong type for 'age' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-22) except Exception as e:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-0-23)     print(e)`

API Reference[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#api-reference "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### `generate_dynamic_models()`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#generate_dynamic_models "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-1-1) def generate_dynamic_models(     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-1-2)     input_: Mapping[str, Any],    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-1-3)     *,    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-1-4)     config: GenerateConfig | None = None,    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-1-5)     cache_size: int = 128, [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-1-6) ) -> dict[str, type]:`

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `input_` | `Mapping[str, Any]` | required | JSON Schema or OpenAPI schema as dict |
| `config` | `GenerateConfig \\| None` | `None` | Generation options (same as `generate()`) |
| `cache_size` | `int` | `128` | Maximum cached schemas. Set to `0` to disable |

**Returns:** `dict[str, type]` - Dictionary mapping class names to model classes.

### `clear_dynamic_models_cache()`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#clear_dynamic_models_cache "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-2-1) def clear_dynamic_models_cache() -> int:`

Clears the internal cache and returns the number of entries cleared.

Examples[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### JSON Schema with Nested Models[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#json-schema-with-nested-models "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-1) from datamodel_code_generator import generate_dynamic_models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-3) schema = {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-4)     "$defs": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-5)         "Address": {            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-6)             "type": "object",            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-7)             "properties": {                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-8)                 "street": {"type": "string"},                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-9)                 "city": {"type": "string"}            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-10)             },            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-11)             "required": ["street", "city"]        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-12)         },        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-13)         "Person": {            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-14)             "type": "object",            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-15)             "properties": {                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-16)                 "name": {"type": "string"},                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-17)                 "address": {"$ref": "#/$defs/Address"}            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-18)             },            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-3-19)             "required": ["name"] Status.approved [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-6-20) print(item.status.value)  # 'approved' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-6-21)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-6-22) # ### Circular References[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#circular-references "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-1) from datamodel_code_generator import generate_dynamic_models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-3) schema = {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-4)     "$defs": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-5)         "Node": {            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-6)             "type": "object",            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-7)             "properties": { [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-9)                 "children": { [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-14)         }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-15)     },    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-16)     "$ref": "#/$defs/Node" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-17) } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-18)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-19) models = generate_dynamic_models(schema) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-20) Node = models["Node"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-21)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-22) tree = Node(     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-23)     value="root",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-24)     children=[        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-25)         Node(value="child1", children=[]),        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-26)         Node(value="child2", children=[            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-27)             Node(value="grandchild", children=[])        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-28)         ])    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-29)     ] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-7-30) )`

Caching[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#caching "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Models are cached by schema content and configuration to avoid regeneration:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-1) from datamodel_code_generator import generate_dynamic_models, clear_dynamic_models_cache [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-3) schema = {"type": "object", "properties": {"x": {"type": "integer"}}} [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-5) # First call generates models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-6) models1 = generate_dynamic_models(schema) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-8) # Second call returns cached models (same object) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-9) models2 = generate_dynamic_models(schema) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-10) assert models1 is models2  # True [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-11)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-12) # Disable caching for specific call [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-13) models3 = generate_dynamic_models(schema, cache_size=0) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-14) assert models1 is not models3  # True [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-15)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-16) # Clear all cached models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-17) cleared = clear_dynamic_models_cache() [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-8-18) print(f"Cleared {cleared} cached schemas")`

Thread Safety[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#thread-safety "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`generate_dynamic_models()` is thread-safe. Multiple threads can safely call it concurrently:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-1) import threading [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-2) from datamodel_code_generator import generate_dynamic_models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-4) schema = {"type": "object", "properties": {"x": {"type": "integer"}}} [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-5) results = [] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-7) def worker():     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-8)     models = generate_dynamic_models(schema)    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-9)     results.append(models) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-10)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-11) threads = [threading.Thread(target=worker) for _ in range(10)] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-12) for t in threads:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-13)     t.start() [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-14) for t in threads:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-15)     t.join() [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-16)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-17) # All threads get the same cached models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#__codelineno-9-18) assert all(r is results[0] for r in results)`

Limitations[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#limitations "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Limitation | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Pydantic v2 only | Pydantic v1 is not supported |
| Not pickle-able | Use `model_dump()` to serialize instances |
| Dict input only | Schema must be a `dict`, not a file path or string |

Comparison with `generate()`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#comparison-with-generate "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Feature | `generate()` | `generate_dynamic_models()` |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Output | Source code string | Actual Python classes |
| Use case | Code generation, file output | Runtime validation |
| Caching | No  | Yes (configurable) |
| Thread-safe | Yes | Yes |
| Pydantic v1 | Yes | No  |

See Also[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/dynamic-model-generation/#see-also "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Using as Module](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/using_as_module/)
     - `generate()` function reference
*   [JSON Schema](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/jsonschema/)
     - JSON Schema examples
*   [OpenAPI](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/openapi/)
     - OpenAPI examples

# CI/CD Integration - datamodel-code-generator

 

[Skip to content](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#cicd-integration)

CI/CD Integration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#cicd-integration "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================

This guide covers how to use datamodel-code-generator in CI/CD pipelines and development workflows to ensure generated code stays in sync with schemas.

Note

The package name is `datamodel-code-generator`, and the CLI command is `datamodel-codegen`.

* * *

Official GitHub Action[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#official-github-action "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The official GitHub Action provides a simple way to validate generated models in your CI pipeline.

### Basic Usage[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#basic-usage "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-0-1) - uses: koxudaxi/[[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-0-2)   with:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-0-3)     input: schema.yaml    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-0-4)     output: src/models.py    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-0-5)     input-file-type: openapi    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-0-6)     output-model-type: pydantic_v2.BaseModel`

By default, the action runs in **check mode** (`--check`), which validates that the existing output file matches what would be generated. If they differ, the action fails.

### Inputs[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#inputs "Permanent link")

| Input | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `input` | Yes | \-  | Input schema file or directory |
| `output` | Yes | \-  | Output file or directory |
| `input-file-type` | Yes | \-  | Input file type (`openapi`, `jsonschema`, `json`, `yaml`, `csv`, `graphql`) |
| `output-model-type` | Yes | \-  | Output model type (`pydantic_v2.BaseModel`, `pydantic.BaseModel`, `dataclasses.dataclass`, `typing.TypedDict`, `msgspec.Struct`) |
| `check` | No  | `true` | Validate that existing output is up to date (no generation) |
| `working-directory` | No  | `.` | Working directory (where `pyproject.toml` is located) |
| `profile` | No  | \-  | Named profile from `pyproject.toml` |
| `extra-args` | No  | \-  | Additional CLI arguments |
| `version` | No  | \-  | Specific version to install (defaults to action's tag version) |
| `extras` | No  | \-  | Optional extras to install (comma-separated: `graphql`, `http`, `validation`, `ruff`, `all`) |

### Example: Validate on Pull Request[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#example-validate-on-pull-request "Permanent link")

.github/workflows/validate-models.yml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-1) name: Validate Generated Models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-3) on:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-4)   pull_request:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-5)     paths:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-6)       - 'schemas/**'      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-7)       - 'src/models/**' [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-9) jobs:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-10)   validate:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-11)     runs-on: ubuntu-latest    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-12)     steps:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-1-13)       -
