### Example: Monorepo with Multiple Schemas[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#example-monorepo-with-multiple-schemas "Permanent link")

.github/workflows/validate-models.yml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-1) jobs:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-2)   validate:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-3)     runs-on: ubuntu-latest    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-4)     strategy:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-5)       matrix:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-6)         include:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-7)           - working-directory: packages/api            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-8)             input: schemas/openapi.yaml            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-9)             output: src/models.py            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-10)             input-file-type: openapi          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-11)           - working-directory: packages/admin            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-12)             input: schemas/schema.json            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-13)             output: src/models.py            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-14)             input-file-type: jsonschema    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-15)     steps:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-16)       - uses: actions/checkout@v4 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-17)      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-18)       - uses: koxudaxi/[[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-19)         with:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-20)           input: ${{ matrix.input }}          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-21)           output: ${{ matrix.output }}          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-22)           input-file-type: ${{ matrix.input-file-type }}          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-23)           output-model-type: pydantic_v2.BaseModel          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-2-24)           working-directory: ${{ matrix.working-directory }}`

### Example: Using Profiles[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#example-using-profiles "Permanent link")

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-3-1) - uses: koxudaxi/[[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-3-2)   with:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-3-3)     input: schemas/api.yaml    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-3-4)     output: src/models.py    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-3-5)     input-file-type: openapi    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-3-6)     output-model-type: pydantic_v2.BaseModel    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-3-7)     profile: api`

### Example: Generate Models (Instead of Validation)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#example-generate-models-instead-of-validation "Permanent link")

Set `check: 'false'` to actually generate the models:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-4-1) - uses: koxudaxi/[[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-4-2)   with:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-4-3)     input: schema.yaml    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-4-4)     output: src/models.py    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-4-5)     input-file-type: openapi    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-4-6)     output-model-type: pydantic_v2.BaseModel    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-4-7)     check: 'false'`

### Example: GraphQL Schema[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#example-graphql-schema "Permanent link")

For GraphQL schemas, use the `extras` input to install the required dependency:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-5-1) - uses: koxudaxi/[[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-5-2)   with:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-5-3)     input: schema.graphql    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-5-4)     output: src/models.py    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-5-5)     input-file-type: graphql    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-5-6)     output-model-type: pydantic_v2.BaseModel    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-5-7)     extras: 'graphql'`

### Example: Multiple Extras[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#example-multiple-extras "Permanent link")

You can install multiple extras with comma-separated values:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-6-1) - uses: koxudaxi/[[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-6-2)   with:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-6-3)     input: schema.yaml    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-6-4)     output: src/models.py    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-6-5)     input-file-type: openapi    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-6-6)     output-model-type: pydantic_v2.BaseModel    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-6-7)     extras: 'http,validation,ruff'`

### Example: Additional CLI Options[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#example-additional-cli-options "Permanent link")

Use `extra-args` for CLI options not covered by the inputs:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-7-1) - uses: koxudaxi/[[email protected]](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-7-2)   with:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-7-3)     input: schema.yaml    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-7-4)     output: src/models.py    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-7-5)     input-file-type: openapi    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-7-6)     output-model-type: pydantic_v2.BaseModel    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-7-7)     extra-args: '--snake-case-field --field-constraints'`

Version Pinning

Always pin the action to a specific version tag (e.g., `@0.44.0`) to ensure reproducible builds. The action installs the same version of `datamodel-code-generator` as the tag.

* * *

The `--check` Flag[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#the-check-flag "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `--check` flag verifies that generated code matches existing files without modifying them. If the output would differ, it exits with a non-zero status code.

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-8-1) datamodel-codegen --check`

### Success (Exit code 0)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#success-exit-code-0 "Permanent link")

When generated code matches the existing file, the command exits silently with code 0:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-9-1) $ datamodel-codegen --check [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-9-2) $ echo $? [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-9-3) 0`

### Failure (Exit code 1)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#failure-exit-code-1 "Permanent link")

When the schema has changed and the generated code would differ, a unified diff is shown and the command exits with code 1:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-1) $ datamodel-codegen --check [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-2) --- models.py [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-3) +++ models.py (expected) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-4) @@ -12,3 +12,4 @@      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-5)      name: Optional[str] = None     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-6)      age: Optional[int] = None     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-7)      email: Optional[str] = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-8) +    active: Optional[bool] = None [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-9) $ echo $? [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-10-10) 1`

Best Practice: Use pyproject.toml

Instead of passing many CLI options, configure all settings in `pyproject.toml`. This keeps CI commands simple, ensures consistency between local development and CI, and makes configuration easier to maintain.

pyproject.toml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-11-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-11-2) input = "schemas/api.yaml" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-11-3) output = "src/models/api.py" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-11-4) output-model-type = "pydantic_v2.BaseModel" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-11-5) disable-timestamp = true`

Then simply run:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-12-1) datamodel-codegen --check`

For projects with multiple schemas, use [named profiles](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#named-profiles)
 to organize configurations by purpose.

**Related:** [`--check`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/general-options/#check)
, [`--disable-timestamp`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#disable-timestamp)
, [pyproject.toml Configuration](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/)

* * *

GitHub Actions[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#github-actions "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic Example[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#basic-example "Permanent link")

.github/workflows/ci.yml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-1) name: CI [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-3) on:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-4)   push:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-5)     branches: [main]  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-6)   pull_request: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-7)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-8) jobs:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-9)   check-generated-code:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-10)     runs-on: ubuntu-latest    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-11)     steps:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-12)       - uses: actions/checkout@v4 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-13)      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-14)       - name: Set up Python        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-15)         uses: actions/setup-python@v6        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-16)         with:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-17)           python-version: "3.14" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-18)      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-19)       - name: Install dependencies        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-20)         run: pip install datamodel-code-generator [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-21)      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-22)       - name: Verify generated models are up-to-date        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-13-23)         run: datamodel-codegen --check`

### Using Profiles for Multiple Schemas[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#using-profiles-for-multiple-schemas "Permanent link")

For projects with multiple schemas, use [named profiles](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#named-profiles)
 to organize configurations by purpose:

pyproject.toml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-2) output-model-type = "pydantic_v2.BaseModel" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-3) disable-timestamp = true [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-5) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.api] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-6) input = "schemas/openapi/api.yaml" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-7) output = "src/models/api.py" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-8)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-9) [tool.datamodel-codegen.profiles.events] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-10) input = "schemas/jsonschema/events.json" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-11) output = "src/models/events.py" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-14-12) input-file-type = "jsonschema"`

.github/workflows/ci.yml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-15-1) - name: Verify API models are up-to-date   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-15-2)   run: datamodel-codegen --profile api --check [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-15-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-15-4) - name: Verify event models are up-to-date   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-15-5)   run: datamodel-codegen --profile events --check`

### Using uv[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#using-uv "Permanent link")

If your project uses [uv](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv)
, you can run the CLI via `uv run`. This example installs the tool ephemerally (no need to add it to your project dependencies):

.github/workflows/ci.yml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-16-1) - name: Install uv   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-16-2)   uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-16-3)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-16-4) - name: Verify generated models are up-to-date   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-16-5)   run: uv run --with datamodel-code-generator datamodel-codegen --profile api --check`

* * *

Pre-commit Hook[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#pre-commit-hook "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use datamodel-code-generator as a [pre-commit](https://pre-commit.com/)
 hook to automatically check or regenerate models before commits.

Tip

Pin `rev` to a released tag (e.g., `vX.Y.Z`) to keep generated output stable and reproducible across developer machines and CI.

### With pyproject.toml (Recommended)[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#with-pyprojecttoml-recommended "Permanent link")

Configure settings in `pyproject.toml` and use a simple pre-commit hook:

.pre-commit-config.yaml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-17-1) repos:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-17-2)   - repo: https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-17-3)     rev: vX.Y.Z    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-17-4)     hooks:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-17-5)       - id: datamodel-code-generator        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-17-6)         args: [--check]        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-17-7)         files: ^schemas/`

### With Profiles[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#with-profiles "Permanent link")

For projects with multiple schemas using [named profiles](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/pyproject_toml/#named-profiles)
:

.pre-commit-config.yaml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-1) repos:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-2)   - repo: https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-3)     rev: vX.Y.Z    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-4)     hooks:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-5)       - id: datamodel-code-generator        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-6)         name: Check API models        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-7)         args: [--profile, api, --check]        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-8)         files: ^schemas/openapi/      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-9)       - id: datamodel-code-generator        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-10)         name: Check event models        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-11)         args: [--profile, events, --check]        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-18-12)         files: ^schemas/jsonschema/`

### Auto-regenerate Mode[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#auto-regenerate-mode "Permanent link")

This configuration automatically regenerates models when schema files change:

.pre-commit-config.yaml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-19-1) repos:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-19-2)   - repo: https://github.com/koxudaxi/datamodel-code-generator    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-19-3)     rev: vX.Y.Z    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-19-4)     hooks:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-19-5)       - id: datamodel-code-generator        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-19-6)         files: ^schemas/`

Installing the hook

Ensure `pre-commit` is installed, then install the hooks:

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-20-1) pip install pre-commit [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-20-2) pre-commit install`

* * *

GitLab CI[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#gitlab-ci "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

.gitlab-ci.yml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-1) check-generated-code:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-2)   image: python:3.14  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-3)   script:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-4)     - pip install datamodel-code-generator    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-5)     - datamodel-codegen --check  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-6)   rules:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-7)     - changes:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-8)         - schemas/**/*        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-21-9)         - src/models/**/*`

* * *

Makefile Integration[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#makefile-integration "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add targets to your Makefile for easy generation and checking:

Makefile

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-22-1) .PHONY: generate-models check-models [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-22-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-22-3) generate-models:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-22-4)     datamodel-codegen [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-22-5)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-22-6) check-models:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-22-7)     datamodel-codegen --check`

Then use in CI:

.github/workflows/ci.yml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-23-1) - name: Check generated models   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-23-2)   run: make check-models`

For projects with multiple profiles:

Makefile

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-1) .PHONY: generate-all check-all [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-2)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-3) generate-all:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-4)     datamodel-codegen --profile api    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-5)     datamodel-codegen --profile events [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-7) check-all:     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-8)     datamodel-codegen --profile api --check    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-24-9)     datamodel-codegen --profile events --check`

* * *

Troubleshooting[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#troubleshooting "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Check fails due to formatting differences[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#check-fails-due-to-formatting-differences "Permanent link")

Ensure you're using the same formatters in CI as locally. Configure formatters in `pyproject.toml`:

pyproject.toml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-25-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-25-2) formatters = ["ruff"]`

See [Formatting](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/formatting/)
 for details.

**Related:** [`--formatters`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#formatters)

### Check fails due to timestamp[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#check-fails-due-to-timestamp "Permanent link")

Always use `disable-timestamp = true` in `pyproject.toml`:

pyproject.toml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-26-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-26-2) disable-timestamp = true`

**Related:** [`--disable-timestamp`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/template-customization/#disable-timestamp)

### Different Python versions produce different output[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#different-python-versions-produce-different-output "Permanent link")

Some type annotations differ between Python versions. Pin the target version in `pyproject.toml` and ensure CI uses the same Python version as development:

pyproject.toml

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-27-1) [tool.datamodel-codegen] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/ci-cd/#__codelineno-27-2) target-python-version = "3.14"`

**Related:** [`--target-python-version`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#target-python-version)

---

# 🏷️ Field Customization - datamodel-code-generator

| Option | Description |

| [`--aliases`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#aliases) | Apply custom field and class name aliases from JSON file. |

| [`--default-values`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#default-values) | Override field default values from external JSON file. |
| [`--empty-enum-field-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#empty-enum-field-name) | Name for empty string enum field values. |
| [`--extra-fields`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#extra-fields) | Configure how generated models handle extra fields not defin... |
| [`--field-constraints`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-constraints) | Generate Field() with validation constraints from schema. |

| [`--field-include-all-keys`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-include-all-keys) | Include all schema keys in Field() json\_schema\_extra. |

| [`--no-alias`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#no-alias) | Disable Field alias generation for non-Python-safe property ... |
| [`--original-field-name-delimiter`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#original-field-name-delimiter) | Specify delimiter for original field names when using snake-... |
| [`--remove-special-field-name-prefix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#remove-special-field-name-prefix) | Remove the special prefix from field names. |
| [`--set-default-enum-member`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#set-default-enum-member) | Set the first enum member as the default value for enum fiel... |
| [`--snake-case-field`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#snake-case-field) | Convert field names to snake\_case format. |
| [`--special-field-name-prefix`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#special-field-name-prefix) | Prefix to add to special field names (like reserved keywords... |
| [`--use-attribute-docstrings`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-attribute-docstrings) | Generate field descriptions as attribute docstrings instead ... |
| [`--use-enum-values-in-discriminator`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-enum-values-in-discriminator) | Use enum values in discriminator mappings for union types. |
| [`--use-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description) | Include schema descriptions as Field docstrings. |
| [`--use-field-description-example`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description-example) | Add field examples to docstrings. |
| [`--use-inline-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-inline-field-description) | Add field descriptions as inline comments. |
| [`--use-schema-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-schema-description) | Use schema description as class docstring. |
| [`--use-serialization-alias`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-serialization-alias) | Use serialization\_alias instead of alias for field aliasing ... |
| [`--use-title-as-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-title-as-name) | Use schema title as the generated class name. |

* * *

`--aliases`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#aliases "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Apply custom field and class name aliases from JSON file.

The `--aliases` option allows renaming fields and classes via a JSON mapping file, providing fine-grained control over generated names independent of schema definitions.

**See also:** [Field Aliases](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --aliases openapi/aliases.json --target-python-version 3.10 # (1)!`

1.  `--aliases` - the option documented here

Examples

OpenAPIJSON SchemaGraphQL

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Capitalize enum member names to UPPER\_CASE format.

The `--capitalize-enum-members` flag converts enum member names to UPPER\_CASE format (e.g., `active` becomes `ACTIVE`), following Python naming conventions for constants.

**Aliases:** `--capitalise-enum-members` | **Related:** [`--snake-case-field`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#snake-case-field)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-8-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --capitalize-enum-members # (1)!`

1.  `--capitalize-enum-members` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-3)   "type": "string",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-4)   "enum": [    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-5)     "snake_case",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-6)     "CAP_CASE",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-7)     "CamelCase",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-8)     "UPPERCASE"  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-9)   ] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-9-10) }`

`--default-values`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#default-values "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Override field default values from external JSON file.

The `--default-values` option allows specifying default values for fields via a JSON file. Supports scoped format (ClassName.field) for hierarchical overrides.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-11-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --default-values default_values/scoped_defaults.json # (1)!`

`--extra-fields`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#extra-fields "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Configure how generated models handle extra fields not defined in schema.

The `--extra-fields` flag sets the generated models to allow, forbid, or ignore extra fields. With `--extra-fields allow`, models will accept and store fields not defined in the schema. Options: allow, ignore, forbid.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-17-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --extra-fields allow # (1)!`

1.  `--extra-fields` - the option documented here

`--field-constraints`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-constraints "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate Field() with validation constraints from schema.

The `--field-constraints` flag generates Pydantic Field() definitions with validation constraints (min/max length, pattern, etc.) from the schema. Output differs between Pydantic v1 and v2 due to API changes.

**Related:** [`--strict-types`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#strict-types)

**See also:** [Field Constraints](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/field-constraints/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-20-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --field-constraints # (1)!`

1.  `--field-constraints` - the option documented here

`--field-extra-keys`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-extra-keys "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Include specific extra keys in Field() definitions.

The `--field-extra-keys` flag configures the code generation behavior.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-26-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --field-extra-keys key2 --field-extra-keys-without-x-prefix x-repr # (1)!`

1.  `--field-extra-keys` - the option documented here

`--field-extra-keys-without-x-prefix`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-extra-keys-without-x-prefix "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Include schema extension keys in Field() without requiring 'x-' prefix.

The --field-extra-keys-without-x-prefix option allows you to specify custom schema extension keys that should be included in Pydantic Field() extras without the 'x-' prefix requirement. For example, 'x-repr' in the schema becomes 'repr' in Field(). This is useful for custom schema extensions and vendor-specific metadata.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-30-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --field-include-all-keys --field-extra-keys-without-x-prefix x-repr # (1)!`

1.  `--field-extra-keys-without-x-prefix` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-31-1) { `--field-include-all-keys`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-include-all-keys "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Include all schema keys in Field() json\_schema\_extra.

The `--field-include-all-keys` flag configures the code generation behavior.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-34-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --field-include-all-keys # (1)!`

1.  `--field-include-all-keys` - the option documented here

`--field-type-collision-strategy`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-type-collision-strategy "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Rename type class instead of field when names collide (Pydantic v2 only).

The `--field-type-collision-strategy` flag controls how field name and type name collisions are resolved. With `rename-type`, the type class is renamed with a suffix to preserve the original field name, instead of renaming the field and adding an alias.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-37-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --field-type-collision-strategy rename-type # (1)!`

1.  `--field-type-collision-strategy` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`--no-alias`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#no-alias "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Disable Field alias generation for non-Python-safe property names.

The `--no-alias` flag disables automatic alias generation when JSON property names contain characters invalid in Python (like hyphens). Without this flag, fields are renamed to Python-safe names with `Field(alias='original-name')`. With this flag, only Python-safe names are used without aliases.

**See also:** [Field Aliases](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/aliases/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-40-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --no-alias # (1)!`

1.  `--no-alias` - the option documented here

`--original-field-name-delimiter`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#original-field-name-delimiter "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify delimiter for original field names when using snake-case conversion.

The `--original-field-name-delimiter` option works with `--snake-case-field` to specify the delimiter used in original field names. This is useful when field names contain delimiters like spaces or hyphens that should be treated as word boundaries during snake\_case conversion.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-44-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --snake-case-field --original-field-name-delimiter " " # (1)!`

1.  `--original-field-name-delimiter` - the option documented here

`--snake-case-field`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#snake-case-field "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Convert field names to snake\_case format.

The `--snake-case-field` flag converts camelCase or PascalCase field names to snake\_case format in the generated Python code, following Python naming conventions (PEP 8).

**Related:** [`--capitalize-enum-members`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#capitalize-enum-members)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-53-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --snake-case-field # (1)!`

1.  `--snake-case-field` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-3)   "title": "InvalidEnum",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-4)   "type": "string",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-5)   "enum": [    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-6)     "1 value",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-7)     " space",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-8)     "*- special",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-9)     "schema",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-10)     "MRO",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-11)     "mro"  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-12)   ] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-54-13) }`

`--special-field-name-prefix`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#special-field-name-prefix "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prefix to add to special field names (like reserved keywords).

The `--special-field-name-prefix` flag configures the code generation behavior.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-56-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --special-field-name-prefix special # (1)!`

1.  `--special-field-name-prefix` - the option documented here

`--use-attribute-docstrings`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-attribute-docstrings "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate field descriptions as attribute docstrings instead of Field descriptions.

The `--use-attribute-docstrings` flag places field descriptions in Python docstring format (PEP 224 attribute docstrings) rather than in Field(..., description=...). This provides better IDE support for hovering over attributes. Requires `--use-field-description` to be enabled.

**Related:** [`--use-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-59-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --use-field-description --use-attribute-docstrings # (1)!`

1.  `--use-attribute-docstrings` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-60-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-60-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-60-3)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-60-4)   "title": "Person",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-60-5)   "properties": { `--use-enum-values-in-discriminator`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-enum-values-in-discriminator "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use enum values in discriminator mappings for union types.

The `--use-enum-values-in-discriminator` flag configures the code generation behavior.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-62-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-enum-values-in-discriminator --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel # (1)!`

1.  `--use-enum-values-in-discriminator` - the option documented here

`--use-field-description`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Include schema descriptions as Field docstrings.

The `--use-field-description` flag extracts the `description` property from schema fields and includes them as docstrings or Field descriptions in the generated models, preserving documentation from the original schema.

**Related:** [`--use-inline-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-inline-field-description)
, [`--use-schema-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-schema-description)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-65-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-type-alias --use-field-description # (1)!`

1.  `--use-field-description` - the option documented here

Examples

`--use-field-description-example`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description-example "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add field examples to docstrings.

The `--use-field-description-example` flag adds the `example` or `examples` property from schema fields as docstrings. This provides documentation that is visible in IDE intellisense.

**Related:** [`--use-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description)
, [`--use-inline-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-inline-field-description)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-70-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-field-description-example # (1)!`

1.  `--use-field-description-example` - the option documented here

`--use-inline-field-description`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-inline-field-description "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add field descriptions as inline comments.

The `--use-inline-field-description` flag adds the `description` property from schema fields as inline comments after each field definition. This provides documentation without using Field() wrappers.

**Related:** [`--use-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description)
, [`--use-schema-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-schema-description)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-73-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-inline-field-description # (1)!`

1.  `--use-inline-field-description` - the option documented here

Examples

`--use-schema-description`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-schema-description "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use schema description as class docstring.

The `--use-schema-description` flag extracts the `description` property from schema definitions and adds it as a docstring to the generated class. This is useful for preserving documentation from your schema in the generated code.

**Related:** [`--use-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-field-description)
, [`--use-inline-field-description`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-inline-field-description)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-78-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-schema-description # (1)!`

1.  `--use-schema-description` - the option documented here

`--use-serialization-alias`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-serialization-alias "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use serialization\_alias instead of alias for field aliasing (Pydantic v2 only).

The `--use-serialization-alias` flag changes field aliasing to use `serialization_alias` instead of `alias`. This allows setting values using the Pythonic field name while serializing to the original JSON property name.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-81-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-serialization-alias --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel # (1)!`

1.  `--use-serialization-alias` - the option documented here

Examples

`--use-title-as-name`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#use-title-as-name "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use schema title as the generated class name.

The `--use-title-as-name` flag uses the `title` property from the schema as the class name instead of deriving it from the property name or path. This is useful when schemas have descriptive titles that should be preserved.

**Related:** [`--class-name`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#class-name)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#__codelineno-85-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-title-as-name # (1)!`

1.  `--use-title-as-name` - the option documented here

`--allof-class-hierarchy`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#allof-class-hierarchy "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Controls how allOf schemas are represented in the generated class hierarchy. `--allof-class-hierarchy if-no-conflict` (default) creates parent classes for allOf schemas only when there are no property conflicts between parent schemas. Otherwise, properties are merged into the child class which is then decoupled from the parent classes and no longer inherits from them. `--allof-class-hierarchy always` keeps class hierarchy for allOf schemas, even in multiple inheritance scenarios where two parent schemas define the same property.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-0-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --allof-class-hierarchy always # (1)!`

1.  `--allof-class-hierarchy` - the option documented here

`--allof-merge-mode`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#allof-merge-mode "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Merge constraints from root model references in allOf schemas.

The `--allof-merge-mode constraints` merges only constraint properties (minLength, maximum, etc.) from parent schemas referenced in allOf. This ensures child schemas inherit validation constraints while keeping other properties separate.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-4-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --allof-merge-mode constraints # (1)!`

1.  `--allof-merge-mode` - the option documented here

Examples

`--disable-future-imports`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#disable-future-imports "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prevent automatic addition of **future** imports in generated code.

The --disable-future-imports option stops the generator from adding 'from **future** import annotations' to the output. This is useful when you need compatibility with tools or environments that don't support postponed evaluation of annotations (PEP 563).

**Python 3.13+ Deprecation Warning:** When using `from __future__ import annotations` with older versions of Pydantic v1 (before 1.10.18), Python 3.13 may raise deprecation warnings related to `typing._eval_type()`. To avoid these warnings:

*   Upgrade to Pydantic v1 >= 1.10.18 or Pydantic v2 (recommended)
*   Use this `--disable-future-imports` flag as a workaround

**See also:** [Python Version Compatibility](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-10-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --disable-future-imports --target-python-version 3.10 # (1)!`

1.  `--disable-future-imports` - the option documented here

* * *

`--enum-field-as-literal`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#enum-field-as-literal "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Convert all enum fields to Literal types instead of Enum classes.

The `--enum-field-as-literal all` flag converts all enum types to Literal type annotations. This is useful when you want string literal types instead of Enum classes for all enumerations.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-13-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --enum-field-as-literal all # (1)!`

1.  `--enum-field-as-literal` - the option documented here

`--enum-field-as-literal-map`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#enum-field-as-literal-map "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Override enum/literal generation per-field via JSON mapping.

The `--enum-field-as-literal-map` option allows per-field control over whether to generate Literal types or Enum classes. Overrides `--enum-field-as-literal`.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-21-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --enum-field-as-literal-map "{"status": "literal"}" # (1)!`

1.  `--enum-field-as-literal-map` - the option documented here

`--ignore-enum-constraints`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#ignore-enum-constraints "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ignore enum constraints and use base string type instead of Enum classes.

The `--ignore-enum-constraints` flag ignores enum constraints and uses the base type (str) instead of generating Enum classes. This is useful when you need flexibility in the values a field can accept beyond the defined enum members.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-24-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --ignore-enum-constraints # (1)!`

1.  `--ignore-enum-constraints` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`--no-use-closed-typed-dict`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-closed-typed-dict "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Disable PEP 728 TypedDict closed/extra\_items generation.

Use this option for compatibility with type checkers that don't yet support PEP 728 (e.g., mypy). TypedDict will be generated without `closed=True` or `extra_items`.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-28-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type typing.TypedDict --no-use-closed-typed-dict # (1)!`

1.  `--no-use-closed-typed-dict` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-3)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-4)   "title": "ClosedModel",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-6)     "name": {"type": "string"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-7)     "age": {"type": "integer"}  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-8)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-9)   "required": ["name"],  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-10)   "additionalProperties": false [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-29-11) }`

**Output:**

`--no-use-specialized-enum`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-specialized-enum "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Disable specialized Enum classes for Python 3.11+ code generation.

The `--no-use-specialized-enum` flag prevents the generator from using specialized Enum classes (StrEnum, IntEnum) when generating code for Python 3.11+, falling back to standard Enum classes instead.

**Related:** [`--target-python-version`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#target-python-version)
, [`--use-subclass-enum`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-subclass-enum)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-31-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --target-python-version 3.11 --no-use-specialized-enum # (1)!`

1.  `--no-use-specialized-enum` - the option documented here

`--no-use-standard-collections`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-standard-collections "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use typing.Dict/List instead of built-in dict/list for container types.

The `--no-use-standard-collections` flag generates typing module containers (Dict, List) instead of built-in types. This is useful for older Python versions or when explicit typing imports are preferred.

**See also:** [Python Version Compatibility](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/)

Usage

* * *

`--no-use-union-operator`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-union-operator "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use Union\[X, Y\] / Optional\[X\] instead of X | Y union operator.

The `--no-use-union-operator` flag generates union types using typing.Union and typing.Optional instead of the | operator (PEP 604). This is useful for older Python versions or when explicit typing imports are preferred.

**See also:** [Python Version Compatibility](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-41-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --no-use-union-operator # (1)!`

1.  `--no-use-union-operator` - the option documented here

`--output-date-class`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#output-date-class "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify date class type for date schema fields.

The `--output-date-class` flag controls which date type to use for fields with date format. Options include 'PastDate' for past dates only or 'FutureDate' for future dates only. This is a Pydantic v2 only feature.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-44-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-date-class PastDate # (1)!`

1.  `--output-date-class` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-1) openapi: "3.0.0" [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-2) components:   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-3)   schemas:    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-4)     Event:      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-5)       type: object      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-6)       required:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-7)         - eventDate      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-8)       properties:        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-9)         eventDate:          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-10)           type: string          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-11)           format: date          [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-45-12)           example: 2023-12-25`

**Output:**

`--output-datetime-class`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#output-datetime-class "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specify datetime class type for date-time schema fields.

The `--output-datetime-class` flag controls which datetime type to use for fields with date-time format. Options include 'AwareDatetime' for timezone-aware datetimes or 'datetime' for standard Python datetime objects.

**See also:** [Type Mappings and Custom Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-47-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-datetime-class AwareDatetime # (1)!`

1.  `--output-datetime-class` - the option documented here

`--strict-types`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#strict-types "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Enable strict type validation for specified Python types.

The --strict-types option enforces stricter type checking by preventing implicit type coercion for the specified types (str, bytes, int, float, bool). This generates StrictStr, StrictBytes, StrictInt, StrictFloat, and StrictBool types in Pydantic models, ensuring values match exactly without automatic conversion.

**See also:** [Type Mappings and Custom Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-50-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --strict-types str bytes int float bool # (1)!`

1.  `--strict-types` - the option documented here

`--type-mappings`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#type-mappings "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Override default type mappings for schema formats.

The `--type-mappings` flag configures the code generation behavior.

**See also:** [Type Mappings and Custom Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-53-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --type-mappings binary=string # (1)!`

1.  `--type-mappings` - the option documented here

`--type-overrides`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#type-overrides "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Replace schema model types with custom Python types via JSON mapping.

This option is useful for importing models from external libraries (like `geojson-pydantic`) instead of generating them.

**Override Formats:**

| Format | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `{"ModelName": "package.Type"}` | Model-level: Skip generating `ModelName` and import from `package` |
| `{"Model.field": "package.Type"}` | Scoped: Override only specific field in specific model |

**Common Use Cases:**

| Use Case | Example Override |
| --- | --- |
| GeoJSON types | `{"Feature": "geojson_pydantic.Feature"}` |
| Custom datetime | `{"Timestamp": "pendulum.DateTime"}` |
| MongoDB ObjectId | `{"ObjectId": "bson.ObjectId"}` |
| Custom validators | `{"Email": "my_app.types.ValidatedEmail"}` |

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-56-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --type-overrides "{"CustomType": "my_app.types.CustomType"}" # (1)!`

1.  `--type-overrides` - the option documented here

Model-level overrides skip generation

When you specify a model-level override (without a dot in the key), the generator will **skip generating that model entirely** and import it from the specified package instead.

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-1) {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-2)     "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-3)     "definitions": {        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-4)         "CustomType": {"type": "string"},        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-5)         "User": {            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-6)             "type": "object",            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-7)             "properties": {                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-8)                 "id": {"type": "integer"},                [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-9)                 "custom": {"$ref": "#/definitions/CustomType"}            [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-10)             }        [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-11)         }    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-12)     } [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-57-13) }`

`--use-annotated`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-annotated "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use typing.Annotated for Field() with constraints.

The `--use-annotated` flag generates Field definitions using typing.Annotated syntax instead of default values. This also enables `--field-constraints`.

**Related:** [`--field-constraints`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/field-customization/#field-constraints)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-59-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --use-annotated # (1)!`

1.  `--use-annotated` - the option documented here

Examples

`--use-closed-typed-dict`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-closed-typed-dict "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate TypedDict with PEP 728 closed/extra\_items (default: enabled).

When enabled (default), generates TypedDict with PEP 728 `closed=True` or `extra_items` parameters based on `additionalProperties` constraints in the schema.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-64-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type typing.TypedDict --use-closed-typed-dict # (1)!`

1.  `--use-closed-typed-dict` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-2)   "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-3)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-4)   "title": "ClosedModel",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-5)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-6)     "name": {"type": "string"},    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-7)     "age": {"type": "integer"}  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-8)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-9)   "required": ["name"],  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-10)   "additionalProperties": false [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-65-11) }`

**Output:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-1) # generated by datamodel-codegen: [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-2) #   filename:  typed_dict_closed.json [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-3) #   timestamp: 2019-07-26T00:00:00+00:00 [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-4)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-5) from __future__ import annotations [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-6)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-7) from typing_extensions import NotRequired, TypedDict [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-8) [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-9)[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-10) class ClosedModel(TypedDict, closed=True):     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-11)     name: str    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-66-12)     age: NotRequired[int]`

`--use-decimal-for-multiple-of`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-decimal-for-multiple-of "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate Decimal types for fields with multipleOf constraint.

The `--use-decimal-for-multiple-of` flag generates `condecimal` or `Decimal` types for numeric fields that have a `multipleOf` constraint. This ensures precise decimal arithmetic when validating values against the constraint.

**See also:** [Type Mappings and Custom Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-67-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-decimal-for-multiple-of # (1)!`

1.  `--use-decimal-for-multiple-of` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-68-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-68-2)   "type": "object",
