`--use-generic-container-types`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-generic-container-types "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use generic container types (Sequence, Mapping) for type hinting.

The `--use-generic-container-types` flag generates abstract container types (Sequence, Mapping, FrozenSet) instead of concrete types (list, dict, set). If `--use-standard-collections` is set, imports from `collections.abc`; otherwise imports from `typing`.

**Related:** [`--no-use-standard-collections`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-standard-collections)

**See also:** [Python Version Compatibility](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/python-version-compatibility/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-70-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-generic-container-types # (1)!`

1.  `--use-generic-container-types` - the option documented here

`--use-non-positive-negative-number-constrained-types`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-non-positive-negative-number-constrained-types "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use NonPositive/NonNegative types for number constraints.

The `--use-non-positive-negative-number-constrained-types` flag generates Pydantic's NonPositiveInt, NonNegativeInt, NonPositiveFloat, and NonNegativeFloat types for fields with minimum: 0 or maximum: 0 constraints, instead of using conint/confloat with ge/le parameters.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-73-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-non-positive-negative-number-constrained-types # (1)!`

1.  `--use-non-positive-negative-number-constrained-types` - the option documented here

`--use-pendulum`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-pendulum "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use pendulum types for date/time fields instead of datetime module.

The `--use-pendulum` flag generates pendulum library types (DateTime, Date, Time, Duration) instead of standard datetime types. This is useful when working with the pendulum library for enhanced timezone and date handling.

**See also:** [Type Mappings and Custom Types](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/type-mappings/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-76-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-pendulum # (1)!`

1.  `--use-pendulum` - the option documented here

`--use-root-model-type-alias`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-root-model-type-alias "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate RootModel as type alias format for better mypy support.

When enabled, root models with simple types are generated as type aliases instead of class definitions, improving mypy type inference.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-79-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-root-model-type-alias --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel # (1)!`

1.  `--use-root-model-type-alias` - the option documented here

`--use-specialized-enum`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-specialized-enum "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate StrEnum/IntEnum for string/integer enums (Python 3.11+).

The `--use-specialized-enum` flag generates specialized enum types: - `StrEnum` for string enums - `IntEnum` for integer enums

This is the default behavior for Python 3.11+ targets.

**Related:** [`--no-use-specialized-enum`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-specialized-enum)
, [`--use-subclass-enum`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#use-subclass-enum)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-82-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --target-python-version 3.11 --use-specialized-enum # (1)!`

1.  `--use-specialized-enum` - the option documented here

`--use-standard-collections`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-standard-collections "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use built-in dict/list instead of typing.Dict/List.

The `--use-standard-collections` flag generates built-in container types (dict, list) instead of typing module equivalents. This produces cleaner code for Python 3.10+ where built-in types support subscripting.

**Related:** [`--use-generic-container-types`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-generic-container-types)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-85-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-standard-collections # (1)!`

1.  `--use-standard-collections` - the option documented here

`--use-standard-primitive-types`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-standard-primitive-types "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use Python standard library types for string formats instead of str.

The `--use-standard-primitive-types` flag configures the code generation to use Python standard library types (UUID, IPv4Address, IPv6Address, Path) for corresponding string formats instead of plain str. This affects dataclass, msgspec, and TypedDict output types. Pydantic already uses these types by default.

**Related:** [`--output-datetime-class`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#output-datetime-class)
, [`--output-model-type`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#output-model-type)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-88-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type dataclasses.dataclass --use-standard-primitive-types # (1)!`

1.  `--use-standard-primitive-types` - the option documented here

`--use-tuple-for-fixed-items`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-tuple-for-fixed-items "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate tuple types for arrays with items array syntax.

When `--use-tuple-for-fixed-items` is enabled and an array has `items` as an array with `minItems == maxItems == len(items)`, generate a tuple type instead of a list.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-91-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-tuple-for-fixed-items # (1)!`

1.  `--use-tuple-for-fixed-items` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-1) {   [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-2)   "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-3)   "type": "object",  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-4)   "properties": {    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-5)     "point": {      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-6)       "type": "array",      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-7)       "items": [{"type": "number"}, {"type": "number"}],      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-8)       "minItems": 2,      [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-9)       "maxItems": 2    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-10)     }  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-11)   },  [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-12)   "required": ["point"] [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-92-13) }`

`--use-type-alias`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-type-alias "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use TypeAlias instead of root models for type definitions (experimental).

The `--use-type-alias` flag generates TypeAlias declarations instead of root model classes for certain type definitions. For Python 3.10-3.11, it generates TypeAliasType, and for Python 3.12+, it uses the 'type' statement syntax. This feature is experimental.

**Related:** [`--target-python-version`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/model-customization/#target-python-version)

**See also:** [Model Reuse and Deduplication](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/model-reuse/)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-94-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-type-alias # (1)!`

1.  `--use-type-alias` - the option documented here

`--use-union-operator`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-union-operator "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use | operator for Union types (PEP 604).

The `--use-union-operator` flag generates union types using the | operator (e.g., `str | None`) instead of `Union[str, None]` or `Optional[str]`. This is the default behavior.

**Related:** [`--no-use-union-operator`](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#no-use-union-operator)

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-99-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --output-model-type pydantic_v2.BaseModel --use-annotated --use-union-operator # (1)!`

1.  `--use-union-operator` - the option documented here

Examples

**Input Schema:**

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-100-1) type A {     [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-100-2)     field: String!    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-100-3)     optionalField: String    [](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-100-4)     listField: [String!]! `--use-unique-items-as-set`[¶](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#use-unique-items-as-set "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate set types for arrays with uniqueItems constraint.

The `--use-unique-items-as-set` flag generates Python set types instead of list types for JSON Schema arrays that have the uniqueItems constraint set to true, enforcing uniqueness at the type level.

Usage

`[](https://datamodel-code-generator.koxudaxi.dev/cli-reference/typing-customization/#__codelineno-102-1) datamodel-codegen --input schema.json --use-unique-items-as-set --field-constraints # (1)!`

1.  `--use-unique-items-as-set` - the option documented here

Examples
